title: CO - Internal Orders Master Data
page_id: 307366938
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307366938
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Internal Orders |
| Name (de) | Innenaufträge |
| ET Business Object | CO\_INTORD |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | CO\_INTORD |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | CO\_INTORD |
| IDOC Business Object | [CO60INTORD\_IDOC INTERNAL\_ORDER\_CREATE](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/191171253/CO60INTORD_IDOC+INTERNAL_ORDER_CREATE) |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AUFK | Order master data | Auftragsstammdaten |  |
| ONROR | Object Number Index, Order | Objektnummernindex Auftrag | AUFK |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer | AUFK |
| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt | ONR00 |
| JCDO | Change Documents for Status Object (Table JSTO) | Änderungsbelege zum Statusobjekt (Tabelle JSTO) | JSTO |
| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt | ONR00 |
| JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) | JEST |
| COBRA | Settlement Rule for Order Settlement | Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung | ONR00 |
| COBRB | Distribution Rules Settlement Rule Order Settlement | Aufteilungsregeln Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung | ONR00 |
| AUFK\_TEXT |  | AUFK Long Text (Mapping) | AUFK |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_INTORD
**Name (en):** Internal Orders
**General Description**
=======================

Number range setting depends on group. Note the object numbers of the orders! If the order (number) is changed, the object numbers OR\* must be changed in all relevant tables. Do not forget to migrate or delete the corresponding entries in tables ONR00 and ONROR during migration or carve out.

The temporary costs in the internal orders of the master data are stored, which makes it a shortterm cost center.

Due to the posting of revenues, internal orders are mainly responsible for the accrual of costs.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CO\_CKMLHD,  CO\_CKMLLAHD  CO\_CKMLMV009  CO\_CKMLRUN  CO\_MBEW\_KALNR  CO\_MBEW\_MATNR  ~~former ML60MLCOST~~  ~~CO60MLCOST~~
**Name (en):** Material Ledger Actual Costings
**General Description**
=======================

The application component Actual Costing/Material Ledger fulfills two basic objectives: the ability to manage material prices in multiple currencies/valuations, and actual costing.

A fundamental task of the material ledger is managing inventories in multiple currencies and/or valuations.

---
title: FI Document - S/4 Index tables Backup
page_id: 124970269
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124970269
---
General Description
===================

These BOs can be used to create a BSIK, BSID, BSIS, BSAK, BSAD, BSAS tables backup for all data set or specific data. The BOs in question are:

**please check, BO not existing**

1. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSIK
2. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSID
3. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSIS
4. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSAK
5. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSAS
6. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSAS
7. ACS4\_FI\_INDEX\_BSAD

Prerequisites
-------------

BSID\_BCK, BSIK\_BCK, BSIS\_BCK, BSAD\_BCK, BSAK\_BCK, BSAS\_BCK tables MUST exist. Are the receptor tables for the backup.

Differences in the data structure to S/4HANA
--------------------------------------------

BSIK, BSID, BSIS, BSAK, BSAD, BSAS tables no longer exists in S4. Instead of them exists a view to have backward compatibility with legacy ABAP code.

Necessary permissions
---------------------

Standard cbs-ET authorizations.

Permissions for validation of migrated object (SE16N, SE16H).

Frequently occurring difficulties
---------------------------------

Depending on the number of records to backup may be interesting analyze to apply any kind of split (technical or logical) to get optimum performance time mainly during the go-live phase.

================================================================================

---

============

| Name (de) | PP Herstellanweisung |
| ET Business Object | PP\_PISHEET |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| COCHP | PI sheet: Control Recipe Header | Herstellanweisung: Steuerrezeptkopf | X |
| COFIV | PI Sheet: Signature for Deviation | Herstellanweisung: Unterschrift zur Abweichung |  |
| COFMA | PI Sheet: Message Assignment to Process Instruction | Herstellanweisung: Zuordnung Meldung zur Prozeßvorgabe |  |
| COFTP | PI Sheet: Process Instructions | Herstellanweisung: Prozeßvorgaben |  |
| COFVP | PI Sheet: Process Instruction Characteristics | Herstellanweisung: Prozeßvorgabenmerkmale |  |
| COMEP | PI Sheet: Message Characteristics | Herstellanweisung: Meldungsmerkmale |  |
| COMHP | PI Sheet: Message Header | Herstellanweisung: Meldungskopf |  |
| COTRP | PI Sheet: Process Parameter Texts (Versions) | Herstellanweisung: Prozeßparametertexte (Versionen) |  |
| COVLP | PI Sheet: Current Variable Values | Herstellanweisung: aktuelle Variablenwerte |  |
| TCOPC | Application-Specific Passwords | Anwendungsspezifische Kennwörter | X |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PISHEET
**Name (en):** PP Process Instruction Sheet
**General Description**
=======================

PI sheets are documents (can also be interactive) generated under process orders lifetime (optionally).  They are used to exchange data between the partially or completely manually operated production level and the SAP system (PP-PI). In manually operated production lines, this usually involves a process operator who uses the PI sheet to transfer production-relevant actual data to the SAP system and receives data from the SAP system.

================================================================================

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Link of Idoc to object |
| Name (de) | Verknüpfung Idoc zu Objekt |
| ET Business Object | CA\_CROSSREF  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CA60CROSSREF) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| SRRELROLES | Object Relationship Service: Roles | Object Relationship Service : Rollen |  |
| SMZB\_BINREL | Object Relationship Service: Generic Binary Links | Object Relationship Service: generische binäre Verknüpfungen |  |

Check, if table IDOCREL from BO CA\_IDOCREL should be included into this BO.

**Data model:**

If you create your BO like this, you need to add IDOCREL and an additional view for table SRRELROLES.

**ET Business Object:** CA\_CROSSREF  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CA60CROSSREF)
**Name (en):** Link of Idoc to object
**General Description**
=======================

Links are written in the ALE and IDoc environment, resulting in entries in the tables IDOCREL and SRRELROLES. These links are required for the IDoc monitoring of the document trace and the ALE audit.

The business objects that are involved in a link are stored in the table SRRELROLES.

================================================================================

title: SD Shipments
page_id: 124944451
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124944451
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SD Shipments/Transportation |
| Name (de) | SD Transporte |
| ET Business Object | SD\_SHIPME  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60SHIPME) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| VTTK | Shipment Header | Transportkopf | X |
| VTTP | Shipment Item | Transportposition |  |
| VTTS | Stage of Shipment | Transportabschnitt |  |
| VTSP | Stage of Transport / Item Allocation | Transportabschnitt/Position Zuordnung |  |
| VTPA | Shipping Partner | Partner zum Transport |  |
| TSEGH | Time Segment Header | Terminsegment Kopf |  |
| TSEGE | Time Segment Events | Terminsegment Vorgänge |  |
| TSEGR | Time Segment Relationships Between Events | Terminsegment Anordungsbeziehungen zwischen Vorgängen |  |
| TSEGEADR | Time Segment Address for Event | Terminsegment Adresse zum Vorgang |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_SHIPME  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60SHIPME)
**Name (en):** SD Shipments/Transportation
**General Description**
=======================

Transportation planning involves all the activities that must be performed before the shipment leaves the shipping point. These activities include:

Acquiring the services of a forwarding agent

Organizing the means of transport

Specifying the route for the shipment

Defining the stages for covering this route in the most efficient way.

There are different types of shipment document types to deal with the various forms of transportation (for example, individual shipments or collective shipments). The shipment documents are used as a basis for carrying out transportation planning. This includes, among other things, the execution of the above activities.

================================================================================

title: FSCM Collections Management Parallel Processing
page_id: 283904968
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/283904968
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM Collections Management Par. Processing |
| Name (de) |  |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_COLL\_PR |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_COLL\_PR |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_COLL\_PR |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UDM\_PR\_HEAD | Parallel Program Runs | Parallelisierte Programmläufe | X |
| UDM\_PR\_INTV | Intervals for Parallel Processing of Program Runs | Intervalle zur Parallelisierung von Programmläufen |  |
| UDM\_PR\_JOBS | Jobs for Parallel Processing of Program Runs | Jobs bei der Parallelisierung von Programmläufen |  |
| UDM\_PR\_SEL\_OPT | Selection Options for Parallel Processing | Selektionsoptionen zur Parallellisierung |  |
| UDM\_PR\_SEGMENTS | Block Table for Segment | Sperr-Tabelle für Segment |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_COLL\_PR
**Name (en):** FSCM Collections Management Par. Processing
**General Description**
=======================

You can use this function to display runs for a selected parallelisable program. The processing status is displayed for each run. A run consists of individual packages. Via Display run you can see the associated packages with their respective processing status and an indication of possible errors during data processing. You can view these errors in detail by selecting the corresponding protocol.

You can execute the following functions for a run:

**Stop:** You can stop a parallel run.

**Restart:** If a run has been cancelled or stopped, then you can restart it.

**Release:** If you release a stopped run for the Create worklist programme, the changes will be activated until it is cancelled.

**Delete:** You can delete the monitor data for a run.

If you delete a run for the Generate worklist programme, the generated worklists are also deleted.

If you use the integration with SAP NetWeaver Business Intelligence, deleting a worklist from the monitor for parallelisable program runs also deletes the corresponding data in the BI system.

================================================================================

title: SD Dynamic Sales Pricing / Resource-Related Billing
page_id: 120124301
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120124301
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SD Dynamic Sales Pricing / Resource-Related Billing |
| Name (de) | SD aufwandsbezogene Faktura |
| ET Business Object | SD\_DYNPR  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60DYNPR) |
| S/4 Business Object | SD\_DYNPR\_SDT  (till ET 2021 FP2 SDS4DYNPR) |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| VPKHEAD | Sales Pricing Header Data | Verkaufspreiskalkulation Kopfdaten | X |
| VPKSDITEM | Sales Pricing Header Data (Specific to SD) | Verkaufspreiskalkulation Kopfdaten (Speziell SD) |  |
| VPKDLI | Sales Pricing Dynamic Items | Verkaufspreiskalkulation Dynamische Posten |  |
| AD01DLI | Dynamic items (DI) | Dynamische Posten (DP) | X |
| AD01DLIEF | DI flow: Individual flow | DP-Fluß: Einzelfluß |  |
| AD01DLISF | DI Flow: Totals Flow | DP-FLuß: Summenfluß |  |
| AD01DLISFR | Resource-related billing: DLI flow totals revenue by rev el | Aufwandsb. Fakt.: DEP-Fluß Summensätze Erlöse nach Erlösart |  |
| KONV/PRD\_ELEMENTS | Conditions | Konditionen |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_DYNPR  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60DYNPR)
**Name (en):** SD Dynamic Sales Pricing / Resource-Related Billing
**General Description**
=======================

The prices for customer-specific services are not always defined in a contract as fixed prices, nor can they always be determined using standard pricing. This is the case if, for example, no empirical values exist for specific services, and therefore the services cannot be calculated adequately before conclusion of a contract. Typical examples of this are:

* `Make-to-order production`
* `External plant maintenance in the service company`
* `Specific services such as consulting`

You carry out resource-related billing for these orders. The customer receives a billing document verifying individual materials, internal activities, and costs. The basis for creating a billing document is the billing request.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CS\_TP
**Name (en):** CS functional locations
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the functional locations. A functional location is an organizational unit within Logistics, that structures the maintenance objects of a company according to functional, process-related or spatial criteria. It represents the place at which a maintenance task is to be performed.

================================================================================

title: RE-FX Rental accounting
page_id: 323133172
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/323133172
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | RE-FX rental accounting |
| Name (de) | RE-FX Mietbuchhaltung |
| ET Business Object | RREFX\_RENT\_ACCT |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | REFX\_RENT\_ACCT |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | REFX\_RENT\_ACCT |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VIRAADVPAYCFREF | Link Between AP and Cash Flow | Link zwischen VZ und Finanzstrom |  |
| VIRAADVPAYEXT | Advance Payment - Legacy Data Transfer | Vorauszahlung - Altdatenübernahme |  |
| VIRAADVPAYPROC | Advance Payments per Settlement | Vorauszahlungen pro Abrechnung |  |
| VIRAADVPAYSPLIT | Split Advance Payments | Unterjährige Vorauszahlung |  |

**ET Business Object:** RREFX\_RENT\_ACCT
**Name (en):** RE-FX rental accounting
**General Description**
=======================

#### This BO links advance payments to the cash flow of a real estate contract. **- What is an advance payment?**

###### A payment made before the actual service is provided – e.g., rent for the next quarter.

  
**- What is cash flow in RE-FX?**  
A structured representation of all expected and actual cash flows for a contract.  
  
**Important fields**  
- Contract number  
- Payment reference (e.g., payment for January)  
- Link to cash flow entry  
- Payment status (planned, posted, canceled)

================================================================================

title: Capacity
page_id: 119275541
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/119275541
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Capacity |
| Name (de) | Kapazität |
| ET Business Object | PP\_CAPA |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| KAKO | Capacity Header Segment | Kapazitaet Kopfsegment |  |
| KAKT | Capacity Description | Kapazitaet Bezeichnung |  |
| KAPA | Shift Parameters for Available Capacity | Kapazität Schichtwerte |  |
| KAPE | Capacity unit of measure allocation | Kapazität Basiseinheit Zuordnung |  |
| KAZY | Interval of Available Capacity | Kapazität Angebotsintervalle |  |
| CRCIFOBJ | Object IDs of changed capacities | Objekt IDs geänderter Kapazitäten |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_CAPA
**Name (en):** Capacity
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains all tables required to migrate PP Capacity data. The structure contains a Header table, and several child tables grouped underneath via a Table Key Relationship (TKR). This BO has not direct relationship to other BO’s in terms of Hierarchy. 

Capacity planning is done to balance the load at the work center. You need to check the capacity load on the work center. If any work center is overloaded, you need to move orders to different work centers.

Tables in this BO contains all related information to the Capacities.

================================================================================

**Name (en):** Service Type
**General Description**
=======================

A standard service catalog (SSC) is a general, standardized catalog of text modules used to describe services. 

In contrast to the Service Master the SSC contains standard texts that only yield complete and unique service descriptions when the are put together in various combinations. 

A SSC can be used in the service master.

================================================================================

| ET Business Object | PP\_CAPPROC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60CAPPROC) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the tables required to migrate the Capacity Standard Times calculation Processes. The structure of the BO is made up of a Header table, and some child tables underneath linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR). The BO has not direct relationship to other BO’s. 

Calculating the capacity requirements of an operation for manufacturing a material takes place in scheduling using formulas from the work center. The formulas which you can maintain in Customizing contain formula parameters.
**Tables**
==========

| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FI\_FEB |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FEBIP | Posting Parameters for Postprocessing | Buchungsparametern für Nachbearbeitung |  |
| FEB\_ACCNT\_SAVE | Saving of Posting Lines for Postprocessing (Account Assign.) | Sichern von Buchungszeilen bei Nachbearbeitung  (Kontierung) | X |
| FEB\_AVIR\_EXTD | Advice Note Subitem Enhancement (Bank Statement Postproc.) | Erweiterung Avisunterposition (Kontoauszugnachbearbeitung) | X |
| FEB\_ITEM\_SAVE | Saving of Posting Items for Postprocessing (On Account) | Sichern von Buchungszeilen bei Nachbearbeitung (Akonto) |  |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_FEB
**Name (en):** Electronic bank statement general
**General Description**
=======================

SAP does not generate batch input folders for the electronic account statement, but only reads them in without batch input or ‘immediate posting’. However, this can be completely reworked manually with suggestions from the user exit and search patterns. We use the FEB\_ACCNT\_SAVE and FEB\_ITEM\_SAVE tables to set what was determined in the user exit. If matching documents were found in the user exit via the reference number search, they are also displayed in the ‘Assigned’ tab.

---
title: Kopie von CO - Profit Center Accounting (EC-PCA)
page_id: 307369451
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307369451
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | PCA-Profit Center Accounting |
| Name (de) | PCA-Profit Center Accounting |
| ET Business Object | CO\_PCACC |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | CO\_PCACC |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | CO\_PCACC |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GLPCT | EC-PCA: Totals Table | EC-PCA: Summentabelle |  |
| GLPCA | EC-PCA: Actual Line Items | EC-PCA: Ist-Einzelposten |  |
| GLPCC | EC-PCA: Transaction Attributes | EC-PCA: Bewegungsattribute |  |
| GLPCO | EC-PCA: Object Table for Account Assignment Elements | EC-PCA: Objekttabelle Kontierungselemente |  |
| GLPCP | EC-PCA: Plan Line Items | EC-PCA: Plan-Einzelposten |  |
| GLSPC | Profit Center Accounting: Line Items | Profit Center Rechnung  Einzelposten |  |
| GLTPC | Profit Center Accounting | Profit Center Rechnung |  |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_PCACC
**Name (en):** PCA-Profit Center Accounting
**General Description**
=======================

Profit Center Accounting (EC-PCA) supports the calculation of an internal operating profit for profit centers according to the period accounting method or the cost-of-sales method.

A profit center is a management-oriented organizational unit, which means that it is used for internal controlling.

The division of the company into profit centers forms the basis for controlling internal areas of responsibility.

Note the effects on EC-PCA when changing over master data or organizational data. Tables in PCA are object-based. An object number stands for certain constellations. These are contained in the object number tables GLPCO. There is an internal cbs correction program that adapts the object numbers in the table and in the totals table to changes in, for example, organizational units. (Report in SHC tree).

**GLPCA**
---------

Table GLPCA contains profit center actual line items. Some fields are grouped together to form a specific object number that can be found on tables GLPCO and GLPCC:

The five fields from RBUKRS to RSCOPE are assigned to a unique object number in table GLPCO while the 14 fields from RACCT to RMVCT are assigned to a unique object number in table GLPCC.

**GLPCO**
---------

The GLPCO object number can be found in GLPCT field ROBJNR. The five fields form a unique index, i.e. each object number consists of a unique combination of these five fields. If you map any of these fields, you have to do unique index mapping for GLPCO-OBJNR.

**GLPCC**
---------

The GLPCC object number can be found in GLPCT field COBJNR. The 14 fields form a unique index, i.e. each object number consists of a unique combination of these 14 fields. If you map any of these fields, you have to do unique index mapping for GLPCC-OBJNR.

**GLPCT**
---------

Though GLPCT-ROBJNR links to GLPCO and GLPCT-COBJNR links to GLPCC, the corresponding fields can be found in GLPCT as well. So all three tables have to be consistent. So if you create unique index mappings for object numbers from GLPCO or GLPCC. you have to convert GLPCT using the same unique index mappings created during the conversion of GLPCO / GLPCC. Or you might have to re-build GLPCT based on GLPCA (and GLPCP).

Note that also GLPCT-SOBJNR is linked to GLPCO.

================================================================================

---
title: CO - Object Numbers
page_id: 124965445
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124965445
---
General Description
===================

We have heard of the object number so many times and yet, what is it for? What are the OBJNR? Why do we need to collect OBJNR? How do we collect them and what do we do with the information?

The concept of the OBJNR in SAP:

The OBJNR are widely spread around the system, they are data carriers and often represent the technical link between different sources.

In projects, we need to pay attention to the OBJNR because of:

* Selection (no other way to identify the OrgUnits in scope, so we need to use the OBJNR)
* Mapping (if we convert the system, the OBJNR may be affected, or they can help us map other fields)

To start with, there are different OBJNR in SAP and to differentiate and handle them correctly, we should pay attention to the difference (manifested in their domain).

The most used ones are:

There are the J\_OBJNR, e.g. in COSP, COEP, AUFK, ANLA etc.

There are the GOBJNR, e.g. in GLPCT, GLFUNCT, COFIT etc.

There are the RKEOBJNR, e.g. in CE1\*, BSEG, COEP etc.

This article is handling the J\_OBJNR domain.

Usage of OBJNR
--------------

Where do we find J\_OBJNR mostly? E.g. they are widely spread in CO, just a look at the CO totals tables reveals their role/uniqueness as they are part of the table key:

There might be OBJNR and PAROB/USPOB/PAROB1 etc (partner, source OBJNR etc.)

There are different object types = different OBART (in German OBART = Objektart = object type). Depending on the type, the OBJNR will be constructed differently (as per shown below, see table TBO00).

OBJNR technical tables
----------------------

The OBJNR are used across the system, you can find them in multiple tables and fields. But each unique OBJNR is stored in some technical tables, in addition:

* ONR00 – containing all existing OBJNR (attention: most customer systems are not consistent, so some OBJNR will be either missing or obsolete here)
* ONRXX- for some OBART available (e.g. table ONRAN for AN\*, ONRKL for KL\* etc.)
* Some OBJNR are also found in the logistic status tables JEST, JCDO, JSTO, JCDS etc.
* Some OBJNR are also stored in tables COBRA and COBRB

In table TBO00 you can find the definition of each OBART.

TBO00 reveals the following important points:

* Source/entity table per OBART (TABNAME)
* Is there an index ONRXX table for the particular OBART
* How is the OBJNR composed – see fields KEY01-KEY07
* Do we have internal numbering for the OBART

Example, AN:

AN\* are assigned to assets, they are composed from a concatenation of BUKRS, ANLN1, ANLN2 and all unique AN\*OBJNR can be found in table ANLA. Technically, there is an index table ONRAN, in addition to ONR00.

How does it work for the most OBART?

Example:

AN – definition of AN\* numbers is ruled by entity table ANLA. In table ANLA are “created” all unique AN\* OBJNR that will exist in the system:

(Grouped by OBJNR NE empty)

In table ONR00 and ONRAN there is the exact same amount (here 2427):

Note: very often in the customer systems the data is not consistent, which leads to different amount of OBJNR in the ONRXX tables. The truth is usually in the entity table!

Note: you will find the AN\* OBJNR all across the system, e.g. settlement rules:

Here we talk about the USAGE of the OBJNR as a link/data carrier, not of its unique definition.

================================================================================

============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | Arbeitsplatzhierarchie |
| ET Business Object | PP\_WRKHIE |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| CRHH | Hierarchy Header Data | Hierarchie Kopfdaten | X |
| CRHS | Hierarchy Structure | Hierarchie Struktur |  |
| CRID | CIM Resource - Entities | Production resource - entities |  |
| CRTX | Text for the Work Center or Production Resource/Tool | Arbeitsplatz/ Fertigungshilfsmittel Kurzbezeichnung |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_WRKHIE
**Name (en):** Work Center Hierarchy
**General Description**
=======================

Work center hierarchies are used in capacity planning to cumulate available capacity and capacity requirements. Work centers can be grouped into a work center hierarchy . Apart from the top of the hierarchy - the hierarchy root - every work center in the hierarchy is subordinate to another work center. One work center can exist in several work center hierarchies. A work center hierarchy can include several plants. 

This BO contains the required tables to migrate Work Center Hierarchy. The structure is simple, a Header table, with 2 child tables. The BO has no direct relationship to any other BO.

================================================================================

| Name (de) | FI Konsolidierungs-Objekte |
| ET Business Object | FI\_CONS\_CAPABLE |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FI\_CONS\_CAPABLE |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FI\_CONS\_CAPABLE |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FILCA | FI-LC: Actual journal entry table with 2 object tables | FI-LC: Ist-Einzelpostentabelle mit 2 Objekttabellen |  |
| FILCC | FI-LC: Transaction-related fields for table FILCT | FI-LC: Transaktionsabhängige Felder für Tabelle FILCT |  |
| FILCO | FI-LC: Acct assign.fields (Object-TrPartner) for table FILCT | FI-LC: Kontierungsfelder (Objekt-Partner) für Tabelle FILCT |  |
| FILCT | FI-LC: Global totals table with 2 object tables | FI-LC: Globale Summentabelle mit 2 Objekttabellen |  |
| MCDX | Compressed Consolidation transaction data | Verdichtete Bewegungsdaten Konsolidierung |  |
| T851 | Changes in Investments | Beteiligungsentwicklung |  |
| T871 | Changes in investee equity | Kapitalentwicklung |  |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_CONS\_CAPABLE
**Name (en):** FI Consolidation Capable
**General Description**
=======================

Equity consolidation  
Consolidation method used for associated companies and subsidiaries or joint ventures that are not fully or proportionately consolidated.

The individual financial statement data of a company consolidated at equity are not taken into account in the consolidated financial statements. For this reason, they do not have to be recognised in the consolidation system.

Only the changes in the equity of these companies are taken into account and, in particular, influence the recognition of the investment in the consolidated balance sheet.

This is therefore also referred to as a valuation method.

================================================================================

---
title: CREF Implementation & Understanding - In construction!!!
page_id: 131532336
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/131532336
---
Overview
========

Mapping using the "CREF" table enables the handling of complex mappings which are not possible ~~at least at the moment of writting this article~~ , using the standard ET mapping tools. It is mostly used for:

* Mapping of gl accounts / cost elements, that requires complex derivation rules of Organizational Units (from now OOUU)
* Supporting the group currency implementation
* Mapping year and period fields in a Fiscal Year Variant conversion
* Supporting Functional Area derivation

However, at the moment of writting this article, this "tool" is not part of the standard however the plan is it will be part of it. Theis means we have to bring into our project:

1. The related function modules in the case of accounts mapping
2. The creation of the ZCBS\_CREF\_TAB table

RE20240729 - added   
Please also check this contribution. As this is still always a Z function adjusted to project specific needs you shall not rely that a version from project X will work at project Z at an instant!

================================================================================

| JBDKOPO | Condition Items | Konditionspositionen |  |
| JBDKUMS | Turnovers for Business Partner Accounts | Umsätze für Geschäftspartnerkonten |  |
| JBDZSKO | Cash Flow Header | Finanzstromkopf | X |
| JBDZSTR | Cash Flow | Finanzstrom |  |
| JBDZSVO | Cash Flow (Volume Information) | Finanzstrom (Volumeninformationen) |  |
| JBDZSZT | SAP Banking: Cash Flow (Interest and Repayment Schedule) | SAP Banking: Finanzstrom (Zins- und Tilgungsplan) |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_INTREST
**Name (en):** Interest rate and commodity curve
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP, interest rates are managed through a combination of configuration, transaction-based adjustments, and integration with market data. These rates can be used for various purposes, including calculating interest on financial instruments, managing asset retirement obligations, and performing interest calculation runs. This business object is used to migrate not only interest rates but also commodity curves. Commodity curves are crucial for valuation and risk management related to commodities. They allow for calculating the present value of commodity-related financial instruments and can be used for commodity pricing.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** WM\_TRORD  (till ET 2021 FP 2 WM60TRORD)
**Name (en):** Transfer Order
**General Description**
=======================

The transfer order contains all the information required to execute the physical transfer of materials into the warehouse, out of the warehouse, or from one storage bin to another storage bin within the warehouse. In addition, it is also used for executing logical stock transfers. The tables in this BO contain the data of all the Transfer Orders existing in the Warehouse.

title: FSCM TRM Asset master data for securities
page_id: 307366250
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307366250
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | MD FSCM TRM Asset master for securities |
| Name (de) | MD FSCM TRM Gattungsstammdaten |
| ET Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VWP |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VWP |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VWP |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VWPAKTI | Stock, subscription rights, investment certificates | Aktien, Bezugsrechte, Investmentzertifikate |  |
| VWPANAN | Asset master/asset master relationship | Anlagestamm/Anlagestamm-Beziehung |  |
| VWPANLA | Asset master for securities | Anlagestamm für Wertpapiere |  |
| VWPANLE | Interest-bearing securities | Verzinsliche Papiere |  |
| VWPBDEP | Bank Securities Account Position | Bankdepotbestand |  |
| VWPBONO | Securities listing | WP-Börsennotierung |  |
| VWPBUKR | Company Code Position | Buchungskreisbestand |  |
| VWPCSPREAD | Credit Spread per Security | Bonitätsspread pro Wertpapier |  |
| VWPDEPO | Securities Account Position | Depotbestand |  |
| VWPTERM | Warrants | Optionsscheine |  |
| VWPZUSA | Securities additional information | Wertpapier-Zusatzinformation |  |
| VWPT\_FREE\_ATTR | Free Security Attributes | Freie Wertpapierattribute |  |

**ET Business Object:** MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VWP
**Name (en):** MD FSCM TRM Asset master for securities
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP Treasury Management, securities transactions are processed in the Transaction Manager. The transactions include the management of securities classes, the creation and processing of securities transactions, the posting of movements and the provision of evaluations and reports.The information system offers, for example, evaluations of the securities portfolio and portfolio development.

Master data specific security:

* **Securities Classes:** Master data for securities, including basic data, conditions, and notice data, is managed through securities classes.

* **Business Partner Data:** Business partner data, including ratings and limit information, is also part of the master data and requires appropriate security measures. (This data is migrated via another business object).
* **Securities Accounts:** Master data for securities accounts, including company code, account details, and depository bank, needs to be secured.
* etc.

The business object MD FSCM TRM Asset master for securities is therefore used to transfer relevant master data on a table basis. The associated transaction data is migrated via another business object.

================================================================================

title: S/4 New and Changed Fields and Domains - TO BE INTEGRATED in "VIEWS/CLASSES/TABLES"
page_id: 48925698
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/48925698
---
Overview
========

| Field | Change | Migration relevant action | Source of Information |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ACTIV | BTTYPE in ACDOCA |  | SNote 1976487 |
| RVERS | is obsolete |  | SNote 1976487 |
| DOCNR | is obsolete |  | SNote 1976487 |
| COST\_ELEM | is obsolete RACCT is the only field |  | SNote 1976487 |
| LOGSYS | is AWSYS in ACDOCA |  | SNote 1976487 |
| Optional fields (for example, ZZHOART) | have a different name in the table ACDOCA. The mapping is implemented in the method GET\_ACTIVE\_OPT\_FIELDS of the class CL\_FINS\_FI\_UTILITY |  | SNote 1976487 |
| MUVFLAG | MVFLG (Domain Change) |  |  |
| BSTAT | The NewG/L compatibility views like FAGLFLEXA expose all ACDOCA records except for correction records created by the migration of balances (BSTAT = C in periods > 0). This includes items migrated from COEP and correction items (MIG\_SOURCE = R) | Data Model of S/4HANA Finance Data Migration  Attachment to note 2408083 | SNote 2408083 |
| MIG\_SOURCE | GL  The NewG/L compatibility views like FAGLFLEXA expose all ACDOCA records except for correction records created by the migration of balances (BSTAT = C in periods > 0). This includes items migrated from COEP and correction items (MIG\_SOURCE = R)    AA  The asset line items normally refer to a FI document, but – e.g. for depreciation postings, retirements etc. – do not refer to a single FI line item Therefore, all FI-AA line items (mapped to SLALITTYPE) will be added to the document with MIG\_SOURCE ‘A’ additionally to the FI-lines of MIG\_SOURCE ‘F’ or ‘G’. To correct the duplicate balance of the document, these lines will get reversal lines (MIG\_SOURCE ‘R’).  For missing FI documents (RABUCH postings, AS91 line items or archived FI documents),only FI-AA data will be migrated as MIG\_SOURCE ‘A’ items. If needed, G/L balances will be corrected by the migration of balances. | Data Model of S/4HANA Finance Data Migration  Attachment to note 2408083 | SNote 2408083 |
| RACCT | FOR AA  The G/L account (ACDOCA-RACCT) is derived from the asset + SLALITTYPE |  |  |
| INTRA\_REL | new Fields in VBRK and EKKO: relevance of document for Intrastat - reporting | check, if fields have to be filled with X by migration (if DB-based migration), depending if the process is an import/export process between EU members |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

This table contains an overview of which fields in ECC change in the S4 systems. First column has the ECC fields or structrures, the second one contains the specific change.

| VTB2TRD | Link Between Financial Transaction Flow and Distributor Flow | Verknüpfung von FinGeschäftsbewegung und Verteilerbewegung |  |
| VTBKORES | Correspondence Data | Korrespondenzdaten |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_TRANS
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM VTB Transaction
**General Description**
=======================

The Transaction Manager supports you in managing your financial transactions and portfolios. This includes trading, settlement and the link to financial accounting.

It also...

* helps you to utilise existing rationalisation potential and enables you to automate typical processes.
* provides you with flexible reporting and evaluation structures for analysing your financial transactions, holdings and portfolios.
* enables you to directly measure the impact of financial transactions on liquidity and interest rate risk through integration in Treasury and Risk Management.

The Transaction Manager can be used in companies both in asset management areas and in traditional treasury departments.

================================================================================

---
title: CO-Sum Totals S4 specific handling
page_id: 193086430
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/193086430
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| Name (de) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_SUM\_SDT  [~~CO\_COSP\_SDT~~](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)(deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  ~~[CO\_COSS\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)~~ (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [~~CO\_SUM\_COSPD\_SDT~~](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COSPD  ) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  ~~[CO\_SUM\_COSSD\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)~~ ( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COSSD  ) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_SUM\_COST\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)  ( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COST ) |

If CO line items have been archived in the ECC source system, tables COSP and COSS need to be migrated into their \*\_BAK equivalents in the target system.  
Directly after the CO document import, the CO\_TOTALS report will use these tables to ensure that the CO line items and CO totals in the ACDOCA match.

In LU ONE, these two tables are included in CO\_SUM\_SDT. In a project environment, it probably makes sense to break down this BO into smaller pieces (single tables) for performance reasons.  
Please keep in mind: COSP and COSS were replaced by CDS views in S4 and are not directly writeable. Their target tables are, respectively, COSP\_BAK and COSS\_BAK.

As an example, here are those two tables being migrated in their own BOs:

A selection excluding WRTTYP = '04' and '11' is typical for these two BOs, but the selection should be created according to project guidelines.
**Tables**
==========

**Name (en):** CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE)

================================================================================

title: CO - Activity Type Master Data
page_id: 117445193
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445193
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Activity Type Master Data |
| Name (de) | Activity Type Master Data |
| ET Business Object | CO\_ACTTYP |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_ACTTYP\_SDT |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| CSLA | Activity master | Leistungsartenstamm |  |
| CSLT | Activity type texts | Leistungsartentexte | CSLA |
| ONRKL | Object Number Index, Cost Center/Activity Type | Objektnummernindex Kostenstelle/Leistungsart | CSLA |
| ONR00 (not for SDT) | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer | ONRKL |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_ACTTYP
**Name (en):** Activity Type Master Data
General Description
===================

The activity type of master data is uses to control specific activities and can apply to one or more cost centers. In addition, the activity type of the master data classifies the activities that are to be performed by one or more cost centers within a company.

Note the object numbers of the activity types! If you change the activity type (number) or convert the controlling area, you must change the object numbers KL\* in all relevant tables. Do not forget to migrate or delete the corresponding entries in tables ONR00 and ONRKL during migration or carve out.

================================================================================

title: Message Status
page_id: 153368689
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/153368689
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Message Status |
| Name (de) | Nachrichtenstatus |
| ET Business Object | CA\_NAST |
| S/4 Business Object | no seperate BO S/4 anymore |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NAST | Message Status | Nachrichtenstatus | X |
| OCE\_ADD\_EMAIL | Output Control Additional Emails | Nachrichtensteuerung für zusätzliche E-Mails |  |
**General Description**
=======================

Output determination is the process to determine the "media” such as printouts, faxes, e-mails, or EDI that are sent from one business to any of its business partners. Condition records store this preferred mode of communication. They are stored in the B\* tables and table NACH. This is master data.

Processed messages, for example in purchase orders, can be found in table NAST.
**Tables**
==========

| table | Description | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| NAST | Message Status | Nachrichtenstatus |  |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_NAST
**Name (en):** Message Status

================================================================================

**Name (en):** PLM Calculation Parmeters
**General Description**
=======================

You use process parameters to specify the process flow in greater detail. Process parameters can be, for example, boiler pressure, oven temperature, energy, time, and velocity.

You can assign process parameters to the following objects:

Recipe

Equipment requirement

Process

Stage

Operation

Action

Trial

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TRLT\_THM
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Threshold Result data
**General Description**
=======================

In the context of SAP, "treasury threshold" generally refers to a financial limit or boundary used to control spending, manage risk, and monitor transactions within the Treasury and Risk Management module (TRM). It's a way to ensure compliance, prevent excessive risk, and maintain budgetary control.

| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSIP | Express Delivery Company: Product Codes | Expressdienst: Produktcodes |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSIPT | Express Delivery Company: Product Code Description | Expressdienst: Produktcode Bezeichnung |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSIS | Small Parcel Carrier: Special Processing (Service Code) | Expressdienst: Sonderabwicklung -> Service Code |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSISP | Express Delivery Company: Delivery Priority -> Service Code | Expressdienst: Lieferprio -> Servicecode |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSISR | Small Parcel Carrier: Route -> Service Code | Expressdienst: Route -> Service Code |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSISV | Small Parcel Carrier: Shipping Condition (Service Code) | Expressdienst: Versandbedingung -> Service Code |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSITS | Express Delivery Company: Service Codes | Expressdienst: Service Codes |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXSITST | Small Parcel Carrier: Service Codes Description | Expressdienst: Service Codes Bezeichnung |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VRTCO | Routing Info per Carrier/Country/Location | Routinginfo pro Spediteur/Land/Ort |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VRTCP | Routing Info per Carrier/Country/Zip Code | Routinginfo pro Spediteur/Land/PLZ |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VRTCPR | Routing Info per Carrier/Country/Zip Code Area | Routinginfo pro Spediteur/Land/PLZ-Bereich |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXTRKS | Tracking Status Details | Tracking Status Detail |
| SD\_EXPRMD | VXTRKST | Tracking Status Details | Tracking Status Detail |
| SD\_EXPRMD2 | VXSIFNT | Small Parcel Carrier: Function Module Description | Expressdienst: Beschreibung des Funktionsbausteines |
| SD\_EXPRMD2 | VXSIQE | Small Parcel Carrier: Conversion into External Qualifier | Expressdienst: Umschlüsselung in externe Qualifier |
| SD\_EXPRMD2 | VXSITDNSO | Number Range Sub-object | Nummernkreis Subobjekt |
| SD\_EXPRMD2 | VLBLTD | Express Delivery Company's Carrier Assignment | Expressdienst Spediteurszuordnung |
| SD\_EXPRTR | VTRKH | Tracking Data - Header | Trackingdaten Kopf |
| SD\_EXPRTR | VTRKP | Tracking Data - Item | Trackingdaten Position |
| SD\_EXPRTR | VTRDI | Shipment Planning Index | Transportdispositionsindex |
| SD\_EXPRTR | VLBL | Express Delivery Company's Data Field | Expressdienst Datenfelder |
| SD\_EXPRTR | VTDSTC | Condition of Deliveries for Freight Planning by Fwdng Agent | Zustand von Lieferungen bei Frachtplanung durch Spediteur |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_EXPRMD  SD\_EXPRMD2  SD\_EXPRTR  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60EXPRMD, SD60EXPRMD2, SD60EXPRTR)
**Name (en):** SD Express Service (Company code)  SD Express Service (Company Code - Remnant)  SD Express Service (Tracking)
**General Description**
=======================

Express delivery companies are distinguished by the following:  
•Quick transportation of goods from one location to another  
•Parcel tracking

You can assign one express delivery company ID to a carrier for each export indicator.

The Express Delivery Company Cockpit is the central location in the system where you can make all settings that have to do with express delivery companies.

Express delivery companies often offer additional services (see also service code and product code) such as:  
•Various outbound-delivery speeds (overnight, same-day, economy)  
•Guaranteed delivery  
•Weekend pick-up / delivery  
•Cash on delivery (COD), letter-of-credit processing  
•International processing  
•Dangerous goods deliveries; live animal shipment  
•Pick-up service  
•Delivery to carrier's warehouse or depot  
•Proof of delivery, recipient ID, signature available as a graphic  
•Recipient notification

Please note:

The abbreviations "KEP" and "XSI" are found in data elements, tables and programs:

KEP is the abbrieviation for the German term "Kurier-Express-Paketdienst" (courier/express/parcel service)

XSI stands for eXpress Shipping Interface

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_BP
**Name (en):** Credit management: BP extension
**General Description**
=======================

With the new credit management in S4, the information no longer depends on the customer but on BP. A credit segment is customised for this purpose, which then stores all important information, such as credit limit, risk class, etc., in the corresponding BP roles. The transfer of master data from the old world to the S4 world is carried out with this BO

**With the implementation of the OIE, it is now possible to transfer the sub-accounts of a credit account to the new BP relationship structures in Table BUT050**

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COMMIT
**Name (en):** TD CO Commitments
**General Description**
=======================

Commitments are liabilities that arise due to contractual obligations.

Commitments are only passed to other budget control applications for purchase requisitions and purchase orders that have been assigned to an account.

Commitment management is used for early detection and analysis of commitments in terms of their cost and financial impact.

Complex for reassigning the individual fields, since the dependencies cannot be uniquely assigned!

================================================================================

title: SD Conditions (Price) A*Tables
page_id: 120116828
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120116828
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SD Conditions |
| Name (de) | SD Konditionen |
| ET Business Object | *(SD\_COND*  *SD\_KONH*  *(till ET 2021 FP2 SD60COND,  SD60KONH))* Business Object will be created with  BO Status 97 |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| KONH | Conditions (Header) | Konditionen (Kopf) | (X) |
| KONP | Conditions (Item) | Konditionen (Position) |  |
| KONW | Conditions (1-Dimensional Value Scale) | Konditionen (Wertstaffel 1-dimensional) |  |
| KONM | Conditions (1-Dimensional Quantity Scale) | Konditionen (Mengenstaffel 1-dimensional) |  |
| A\*\*\* | \* | \* |  |

**ET Business Object:** (SD\_COND*  *SD\_KONH*  *(till ET 2021 FP2 SD60COND,  SD60KONH))* Business Object will be created with  BO Status 97
**Name (en):** SD Conditions
**General Description**
=======================

The term pricing is used broadly to describe the calculation of prices (for external use by customers or vendors) and costs (for internal purposes, such as cost accounting). Conditions represent a set of circumstances that apply when a price is calculated. For example, a particular customer orders a certain quantity of a particular product on a certain day. The variable factors here - the customer, the product, the order quantity, the date - determine the final price the customer gets. The information about each of these factors can be stored in the system as master data. This master data is stored in the form of condition records .

The Condition Technique in Pricing

The condition technique refers to the method by which the system determines prices from information stored in condition records. In Sales and Distribution, the various elements used in the condition technique are set up and controlled in Customizing. During sales order processing, the system uses the condition technique to determine a variety of important pricing information. For example, the system automatically determines which gross price the customer should be charged and which discounts and surcharges are relevant given the conditions that apply.

See also

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PROCESSORD
**Name (en):** Process Order
**General Description**
=======================

Process orders are used for the production of materials or provide services in a certain quantity and on a certain date. They allow resource planning, process order management control, and account assignment and order settlement rules to be specified.

A process order can be identified by the order category: AUFK-AUTYP = 40.

The structure is comprised of a Header table, plus several child tables linked underneath by their respective Table Key Relationships (TKR).

This BO acts as a super BO to several other BO’s.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_COLL\_WL
**Name (en):** FSCM Colllections Management Worklists
**General Description**
=======================

A worklist is automatically created daily for each claims handler (see process Data synchronisation and creation of worklists ). The receivables clerk calls up their worklist in the Edit receivables list app. The worklist contains all customers to be contacted in a prioritised order. The prioritisation of the customers on the worklist (worklist entries) was determined by the collection strategy assigned to the customer.

================================================================================

---
title: Transformation of totals & balances
page_id: 113096917
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/113096917
---
Overview
========

...

Creation of totals and balances (FAGLFLEXT)
-------------------------------------------

In the new general ledger, a new totals table is used for reporting. Within a new GL transformation, table FAGLFLEXT must be set up. There are several options of creating the totals, depending on the scenario of the project. The following options are available:

| Report | Description | Dependencies | Functionality | Scenario |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_IMP | Import totals from temporary table | Existing FAGLFLEXT | In a first step, the existing FAGLFLEXT has to be exported and imported in structure /CBSGMBH/A\_FLEXT.  A mapping of G/L accounts and other dimensions is possible in this case.  In a second step the Report will transfer the values from /CBSGMBH/A\_FLEXT to the FAGLFLEXT. | System merge (FAGLFLEXT does exist in source system) |
| [/CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=31594079) | Build up FAGLFLEXT from scratch | Existing GLT0 | The Report updates the FAGLFLEXT with values of tables GLT0, GLFUNCT and GLPCT.  The update can be performed by period and in addition for the carryforward. | Conversion |
| [/CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_UPDATE\_TOTALS](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=31594082) | Build up totals from line items | Existing FAGLFLEXA | The Reports adds up the line items of table FAGLFLEXA and updates the FAGLFLEXT.  Only possible, if all line items are available (no archived documents). | Conversion, System merge |
| /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_CONV | Convert totals table FAGLFLEXT | Existing FAGLFLEXT |  | System merge |
| /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_PLAN\_TOTALS | Build up totals for plan data | Existing FAGLFLEXP | The Reports adds up the line items of table FAGLFLEXP and updates the FAGLFLEXT. | Conversion, System merge |

### /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_IMP

Just to complete the article, we used this program in a project, so we will like to include how we used it:

In the Westfalen AG project we had a system conversion where a harmonization of the chart of accounts, inclusion of the second currency in FI and creation of NEW GL was done.

Due to some reasons with the exchange rate used in the project, they asked us to make some adjustments in some accounts, modifying the total amount in the second currency fields (KSLXX). This program  is able to create/modify lines in the FAGLFEXT.

As I see it, “Best Practice” I would only use the program to create new lines (not to modify them), so it will be easier to have them located in case an auditor asks where this information comes from, as well as avoiding touching lines of the FAGLFLEXT because it is a risky table with many fields and therefore it would be more complicated to validate.

The first thing we must do, is to fill the table /CBSGMBH/A\_FLEXT with the data we need. This table is a copy of the FAGLFLEXT table, including the ‘Legacy Unit’.

As we know, the FAGLFLEXT has fields called OBJNRXX. These object numbers are filled with a number. Each object number is associated to a different table, where we can see that each number is associated to a combination of fields.

Here we can see an example of how an OBJNR of the FAGLFLEXT table works:

The program takes into account all OBJNRs, and in case the combination on the table has an associated OBJNR, it includes it, and in case such a combination does not exist, it creates it, so no OBJNR search is necessary.

Here we have a screenshot about how we filled the table /CBSGMBH/A\_FLEXT in our costumer:

* We have included all OBJNR number “0” except one that is a counter, so the key is not repeated.
* Changes were agreed on 2020, in the period 15, and with some default data (Profit center, segment…) so we include there the values.
* We include in the field LOGSYS a text called “MIG-KSL15”. This field is part of the OBJNR01 so, as we inluded a value that was never on the tables, the program will create a new OBJNR and with this, we will be sure that all the lines that we will create are completely new.

Once we have the CBS table filled, we will only need to execute the program. Here we have a screenshot about /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_IMP:

We have to execute ledger by ledger and the data will be transformed and moved from the CBS table to FAGLFLEXT.

In our project we had some problems with the program summing up some data but in case you faced them as we had, I’m sure that a developer will be able to be solved easily. In our case we didn´t have a developer and the timeline was 2 days so we executed account by account an worked.

Here we can find a screenshot after executing the program where we can see the line that we have in our CBS table, and the line created on the FAGLFLEXT, were the OBJNRs were checked and also the OBJNR that was created due to the change in the LOGSYS (ONBJR = ‘22’):

Balance Carryforward (BCF)
--------------------------

Basically the carryforward within a transformation will be executed via SAP-standard-transaction FAGLGVTR (report SAPFGVTR), except for the partial transformation of history. There are more transformation steps to be considered:

The following 3 Approaches might be required:

| **Approach** | **Report** | **Description** | **Data source** | **Result** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Complete History (with or without dummy split) | SAPFGVTR | Transfer year-end totals into the new fiscal year | FAGLFLEXT | Opening balance per account |
| Partial history transformation with initial year (without split) | /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT  SAPFGVTR | Transfer of totals from last archived fiscal year  Transfer year-end totals into the new fiscal year (not archived fiscal years) | GLT0/GLFUNCT/GLPCT  FAGLFLEXT | Opening balance per account |
| Partial history transformation with document splitting | /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT  ZFCBS\_FAGLFLEX\_CORRECT (see Wacker Chemie)  ZFCBS\_RAGITT\_ALV01 (see Wacker Chemie)  ZFCBS\_RM07MMFI (see Wacker Chemie)  SAPFGVTR  FLEXT\_PRCTR (to be developed) | Transfer of totals from last archived fiscal year  Summation of profit center information of open items and previous documents. Enrichment of empty lines with a defined default profit center.  Summation of profit center information of asset reconciliation accounts (only available, if new asset Accounting is active)  Summation of profit center information of inventory/stock accounts  Transfer of year-end totals into the new fiscal year (not archived fiscal years)  Transfer of fix or percential opening Balance values stored in a Z-table. | GLT0/GLFUNCT/GLPCT  FAGLFLEXA    ANLC / ANLZ  MARC / MBEW  FAGLFLEXT  Mapping table | Opening balance per account and profit center/segment |

There are further Topics to be clarified by customer:

1. Transfer of Transaction types to FAGLFLEXT?
2. Transfer of trading partner to FAGLFLEXT?
3. Transfer of profit centers?

Trading Partner, Transaction type and profit center information is stored in the general ledger (BSEG) and in the Special Ledgers (GLFUNCT / GLPCT). As in most cases archiving is active, it is not possible to gain the ninformation out of the line items (BSEG). In this case, the Information has to be taken out of the Summation tables of the Special Ledgers.

First you have to compare table GLT0 (Ledger 00) with the relevant Special Ledgers (e.g. 0F, 8A). For this pupose you can use Transaction GCAC (ledger comparison).

If there are no differences, you can use Report /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT to Transfer the relevant Information out of the Special ledger tables into table FAGLFLEXT.

If there are differences, the carryforward based on the relevant Information has to be created by customer. We do have the possibility to update table FAGLFLEXT with the Information delivered by the customer.

### Complete History (with or without split)

Transformation of the complete history means that there are no archived FI documents and all documents from all fiscal years have been transformed into table FAGLFLEXA. In this case SAP-standard-report SAPFGVTR is used to transfer the year-end balances into the new fiscal year.

When document splitting is performed for all documents over the whole history, report SAPFGVTR can also be used.

### Partial history transformation with initial year (without split)

If there are archived documents, it is in general not possible to create entries in FAGLFLEXA for all line items. In this case the definition of an initial year is required. The initial year is the first year, in which all documents are available in table BSEG.

The carryforward from the last archived year to the initial year will be done by report **/CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT**. The report will transfer the carryforward of table GLT0 (incl. GLFUNCT and GLPCT) to the FAGLFLEXT.

The carryforward in the following years will be done by SAP-standard report SAPFGVTR.

### Partial history transformation with document splitting

As mentioned in chapter XXX, the implementation of document splitting is only recommended for the current and the previous fiscal year.

To ensure a correct processing of open items, all open items and previous documents of a due date defined will be splitted in addition.

As the result should be a segmented opening balance in the current fiscal year, the following steps have to be processed:

| Step No. | Description | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Carryforward to initial year by report /CBSGMBH/DMF\_NEWGL\_TOTALS\_INIT | Opening balance of initial year per account |
| 2 | Summation of profit center information of open items and previous documents and transfer to  opening balance of initial year by report ZFCBS\_FAGLFLEX\_CORRECT (developed at Wacker Chemie) | Segmented opening balance of initial fiscal year for **open item managed accounts**  **Non-open item managed accounts** are **partially segmented** (due to previous documents) |
| 3 | Carryforward of **open item managed accounts** to the current fiscal year by report SAPFGVTR | **Segmented** opening balance of current fiscal year for **open item managed accounts**,  **partially segmented** opening balance for **non-open item managed accounts** |
| 4 | Setup of a mapping table with opening balance values per **non-open item managed account** and profit center (consider zero balance!!).  This can be done percental or with correct values (see example below).  Report ZFCBS\_FAGLFLEX\_CORRECT (developed at Wacker Chemie) will transfer the content of the mapping table into the carryforward  column of table FAGLFLEXT. | **Segmented** opening balance of current fiscal year for **open item managed accounts**, **partially**  **segmented** opening balance for  n**on-open item managed accounts.** |

| Non-open item managed account | profit center | percentual |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 100000 | PC1 | 40 |
| 100000 | PC2 | 40 |
| 100000 | PC3 | 20 |

| Non-open item managed account | profit center | value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 100000 | PC1 | 2000 |
| 100000 | PC2 | 2000 |
| 100000 | PC3 | 1000 |

================================================================================

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| FAGL\_011GC\_XBRL | Fin. Stmt Vers.: Assignmt of FS Items to Asset Hist.Sht Grps | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Anl.Gittergruppen |  |

| FAGL\_011PC | Fin. Statement Structure: Items in Fin. Statement Structure | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011PC\_T | Fin. Statement Version: Items (Without Internal Node IDs) | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen (ohne interne Knoten IDs) |  |
| FAGL\_011PC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Items (Without Internal Node IDs) | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen (ohne interne Knoten IDs) |  |
| FAGL\_011PCI | F/S Structure: Texts of 1st Item of F/S Structure | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte der 1. Position der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011PCI\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Items in Financial Statement Version | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011QT | Fin. Statement Structure: Text for Fin. Statement Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011QT\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Structure: Text for Fin. Statement Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011SC | Fin. Statement Struct.: Assignment Fin. Stmnt Item: Setname | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Position - Setname |  |
| FAGL\_011TC | Fin. Statement Structure: Timestamp for Fin. Stmt Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zeitstempel zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011VC | Fin. Statement Structure: Contra Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Saldowechselpositionen |  |
| FAGL\_011VC\_XBRL | Finanical Statement Version: Contra Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Saldowechselpositionen |  |
| FAGL\_011ZC | Fin. Statement Structure: Assignment FS Items - G/L Account | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Hauptbuchkonten |  |
| FAGL\_011ZC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Assignment of FS Items to G/L Accts | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Hauptbuchkonten |  |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_STMT\_Version
**Name (en):** Financial Statement Versions
**General Description**
=======================

Financial Statement Versions are used to display Balance Sheets and Profit & Loss Statements according to different accounting principles. G/L accounts need to be added or re-assigned from time to time. For this reason, this is not always considered to be FI customizing, but sometimes also considered to be FI master data.

Financial Statement Versions can be found in the FAGL\_011\* tables. They are migrated via business object FI\_STMT\_VERSION:

There are more (filled) FAGL\_011\* tables than the ones shown in the screenshot above. So, please make sure to add all the ones that are relevant for your project.

================================================================================

| S/4 SDT Business Object | n/a |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description EN** | **Beschreibung DE** |
| TC71 | Digital Signature | Elektronische Unterschrift |
| TC82 | Short Text for Authorization Group for Digital Signatures | Kurztext Berechtigungsgruppe für die digitale Signatur |
| TC84 | Short Text for Individual Signature | Kurztext zur Einzelsignatur |
| TC86 | Short Text for Signature Strategy | Kurztext zur Signaturstrategie |
| TC71D | Signed Document for Signature | Unterzeichnetes Dokument zur Unterschrift |
| TC85 | Signature Strategy | Signaturstrategie |
| TC72 | Digital Signature for Engineering Change Order | Elektronische Unterschrift zum Änderungsauftrag |
| TC73 | Digital Signature for Inspection Lot | Elektronische Unterschrift zum Prüflos |
| TC74 | Digital Signature for PI Sheet | Elektronische Unterschrift zur Herstellanweisung |
| TC75 | Digital Signature for Batch Record | Elektronische Unterschrift zum Chargenprotokoll |
| TC76 | Digital Signature for Object Management Record | Digitale Signatur zum Objektverwaltungssatz |
| TC77 | Digital Signature for Document | Digitale Signatur zum Dokument |
| TC78 | Digital Signature for Physical-Sample Drawing | Digitale Signatur zur Probenahme |
| TC81 | Authorization Group for Digital Signatures | Berechtigungsgruppe für die digitale Signatur |
| TC83 | Individual Signature | Einzelsignatur |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_DSG
**Name (en):** Digital signatures
**General Description**
=======================

Bo for the migration of digital signatures. With the digital signature, the SAP system provides you with a tool to sign and approve digital data. The digital signature ensures that the signatory of a digital document can be identified unambiguously and his or her name is documented together with the signed document, the date, and the time. You can use the digital signature to approve documents or objects in all applications that are set up for its use.

================================================================================

page_id: 120126818
space: unknown

============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | SD Verkaufsaktivitäten |

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| VBKA | Sales Activities | Kontakte | X |
| VBPA | Sales Document: Partner | Vertriebsbeleg: Partner |  |
| VBPA2 | Sales document: Partner (used several times) | Vertriebsbeleg: Partner (Mehrfachverwendung) |  |
| VBPA3 | Tax Numbers for One-Time Customers | Steuernummern für CPD-Kunden |  |
| VBKPA | SD index: Sales activities by partner function | Vertriebsindex: Kontakte zu Partnerrollen |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_SAKIV  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60SAKIV)
**Name (en):** SD Sales Activity
**General Description**
=======================

Partner information such as contact person, partner function and customer class can be taken into account in the selection process. When you specify a customer and a contact person as selection criteria, the system selects sales activities which contain both the customer and the contact person.

Address lists can also be included in the selection process

You can define dynamic selections in addition to the selection criteria in the selection report. For the dynamic selection, you can select any number of fields from the following sources and define a value for them:

* Sales activity

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FI\_MD\_BANK
**Name (en):** Bank Master Data
**General Description**
=======================

The bank master data can be:

* migrated directly via the BO,
* migrated by attaching the tables to the objects for customers/vendors (advantage: only bank master data that is really relevant is migrated).
* created by using the bank details in the customer/vendor master to create new bank master data entries (precondition: bank details must exist in the target, and mapped values must be used)

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| BLPK | Document log header | Belegprotokollkopfsatz | **X** |
| BLPP | Document log item | Belegprotokollposition |  |
| AFWIS | Table of Reprocessing Records Already Reposted | Tabelle der bereits nachgebuchten Nachbearbeitungssätze |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_DOCLOG  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60DOCLOG)
**Name (en):** Document log
**General Description**
=======================

In Repetitive Manufacturing, various documents are created for all the backflushing transactions. 

The data of a document log is saved in various tables. These include:

* BLPK (document log header record)

  BLPP (document log item)

But also:

* MKPF (document header - material document)

  MSEG (document segment - material)

* AFRU (backflush list)

* PZPE (single-lines - reporting point update)

* AFFW (postprocessing individual record)

* AFWIS (postprocessed individual postprocessing record)

================================================================================

---
title: Planned Orders
page_id: 124955406
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124955406
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Planned Orders |
| Name (de) | Planauftrag |
| ET Business Object | PP60PLDORD |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| PLAF | Planned Order | Planauftrag |  |
| MDSM | Simulative dependent requirements | Simulationssekundärbedarf |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP60PLDORD
**Name (en):** Planned Orders
**General Description**
=======================

A planned order is a suggestion from MRP that we should begin producing the particular material that is being suggested. We can then manually convert the planned order into a production order to begin the production of the material. Planned orders can also be automatically converted into production orders. Once a planned order is converted - production of that material is ready to begin. Planned orders specify what material, quantity, and when it should be produced. These quantities can always be changed before conversion.

More details can be found here:

<https://help.sap.com/viewer/85d3fce10e264972a0155c8b46ecf93b/6.17.17/en-US/b898b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4.html>

================================================================================

page_id: 117445342
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445342
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | MD Profit Center |
| Name (de) | MD Profit Center |
| ET Business Object | CO\_PRTCTR  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PRCTR) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_PRTCTR  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PRCTR) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| CEPC | Profit Center Master Data Table | Stammdatentabelle von Profit Centern |  |
| CEPCT | Texts for Profit Center Master Data | Profit-Center-Stammdaten Texte | CEPC |
| CEPC\_BUKRS | Assignment of Profit Centers to a Company Code | Buchungskreiszuordnung von Profit Centern | CEPC |
| CMDT\_PC | Generic Master Record: Profit Center Enhancements | Generischer Stammsatz Profit-Center-Erweiterungen | CEPC |
| CMDT | Generic Master Record: General Header | Generischer Stammsatz Allgemeiner Kopf | CMDT\_PC |
| CMDT\_ADR | Generic Master Record: Address | Generischer Stammsatz Adresse | CMDT\_PC |
| CMDT\_PC\_BUKRS | Generic Master Record: Company Code Assign. for Profit Cent. | Generischer Stammsatz Buchungskreiszuordnung Profit Center | CMDT\_PC |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_PRTCTR  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PRCTR)
**Name (en):** MD Profit Center
General Description
===================

The profit center master data are the classification terms according to which the data relevant for a profit center invoice can be posted and then evaluated.

CEPC\_BUKRS acts as a filter, that is, if a profit center is to be assigned to all company codes, there is no entry in CEPC\_BUKRS. Entries in CEPC\_BUKRS mean that the profit center is not assigned to all company codes, but only to these company codes.

================================================================================

---
title: Ledger-specific fiscal year variant
page_id: 113096914
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/113096914
---
Overview
========

Ledger specific fiscal years are needed for e.g. Indian company codes using a shifted fiscal year for reporting. There is the possibility in the new general ledger to create an own fiscal year variant for non-leading ledger per Indian company code.

The fiscal year variant will be the Standard-SAP-variant V3, with starting of fiscal year in April. Thus the following shifted periods have to be implemented:

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Standard periods (K4)** | **Indian periods (V3)** |
| 04 | 01 |
| 05 | 02 |
| 06 | 03 |
| 07 | 04 |
| 08 | 05 |
| 09 | 06 |
| 10 | 07 |
| 11 | 08 |
| 12 | 09 |
| 01 | 10 |
| 02 | 11 |
| 03 | 12 |

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** REFX\_TERM
**Name (en):** RE-FX Term
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP RE-FX, a **termination** refers to ending a rental or lease contract. This process is essential for properly managing contracts, stopping payments, and keeping accounting clean.

In this BO, all relevant tables for a termination are selected.

**ET Business Object:** VC\_VARTAB
**Name (en):** Variant tables
**General Description**
=======================

Variant tables are used to store combinations of values for different characteristics - for example, you can only have a gray car interior if the car’s paintwork is blue. The system uses the table to infer and check values. If the interdependencies between characteristics change, you change the table, not the dependencies.

================================================================================
