**ET Business Object:** CO\_DOC
**Name (en):** CO Documents
**General Description**
=======================

CO- line- items are created automatically in Controlling if a document is relevant to costs or revenues. A Controlling object, for example: customer output items, internal orders and cost centers, is assigned to the document when it is posted.

All primary costs can be evaluated immediately in the cost accounting.

The CO-line- items worked in a single- circuit system

================================================================================

title: Interest calculation FINT
page_id: 295233003
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/295233003
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | New Interest Calculation |
| Name (de) | Neue Zinsrechnung |
| ET Business Object | FI\_INTEREST\_NEW |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FI\_INTEREST\_NEW |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FI\_INTEREST\_NEW |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| INTITFX | Fixed Interest Amounts per Invoice | Verzinsungs-Festbeträge pro Rechnung |  |
| INTITHE | Until When Was Interest Calculated for Item? | Bis wann wurde der Posten verzinst? |  |
| INTITIT | Interest Calculation Details per Item | Verzinsungs-Details pro Posten |  |
| INTITPF | Interest Data per Form and Posting | Zins-Daten pro Formular und Buchung |  |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_INTEREST\_NEW
**Name (en):** New Interest Calculation
**General Description**
=======================

The interest calculation of an account is controlled by the interest indicator in the master record. This indicator determines, among other things, whether you want to charge interest on open or cleared items, whether you want to post the interest and whether you want to print an interest letter.

Interest is always calculated using the debit interest rate defined for the interest indicator. In exceptional cases, namely when interest is charged on items that were paid before the due date, the credit interest rate is used.

================================================================================

| LIPSPO | LIPS Reference Data Purchase Order | LIPS-Referenzdaten Bestellung |  |

| DLFLD | Non-Changeable Delivery Fields | Nicht änderbare Lieferungsfelder |  |
| VBSS | Collective Processing: Sales Documents | Vertriebsbelege eines Sammelgangs |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_DELIVE
**Name (en):** SD Delivery ( Outbound)
**General Description**
=======================

In its role as central object of the goods issue process, the outbound delivery supports all shipping activities including picking, packing, transportation and goods issue. During the outbound delivery process, shipping-planning information is recorded, status of shipping activities is monitored and data accumulated during shipping processing is documented. When the outbound delivery is created, the shipping activities, such as picking or delivery scheduling, are initiated, and data that is generated during shipping processing is included in the delivery.

An outbound delivery can be created as follows:

With reference to a sales order

With reference to a stock transport order

With reference to a subcontract order

With reference to a project

Without any reference

================================================================================

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Forecasts |
| Name (de) | Prognosen |
| ET Business Object | PP\_FRCAST  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60FRCAST) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| PROW | Forecast Values | Prognosewerte |  |
| PROP | Forecast parameters | Prognoseparameter |  |
| LISR | Planning Object Index for Forecast | Planungsobjekt-Index für Prognose |  |
| PROH | Basic Values (for Forecast): Consumption or Actual Number | Basiswerte (Grundlage für Prognose): Verbrauch oder Istzahl |  |
| MAPR | Material Index for Forecast | Materialindex für Prognose | X |
| MPRP | Forecast Profiles | Prognose-Profiles | x |
| PROF | Forecast Errors | Prognosefehler | x |
| PRON | Postprocessing of forecast errors and exception messages | Nachbereitung der Prognosefehler und -ausnahmemeldungen | x |
| T441A | Forecast profiles | Prognoseprofile | x |
| T441T | Texts for Material Forecasting Profiles | Texte zu den Profilen der Materialprognose | x |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_FRCAST  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60FRCAST)
**Name (en):** Forecasts
**General Description**
=======================

A forecast is a decision-making tool used by many businesses to estimate future demand. This demand is used to procure materials and produce the finished good on time so that we can successfully meet demand. Forecasts are generally built with 3 years of past sales history. 

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the PP forecast data. The BO structure contains a header table, and its child tables linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR). This BO has no direct dependency to any other BO, it can be set up on its own. Forecast based planning is based on material consumption. It operates based on historical values and forecast values and future requirements are determined via the integrated forecasting program. All the Forecast related information is contained in the tables of this BO.

================================================================================

title: FSCM TRM Securities account master data
page_id: 281379878
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/281379878
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM TRM Securities account master data |
| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Depotkonto-Stammdaten |
| ET Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_TWD |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_TWD |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_TWD |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TWD01 | Treasury: Securities Account | Treasury: Depotkonto-Stammdaten |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_TWD
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Securities account master data
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP Treasury and Risk Management (TRM), a securities account is a record-keeping tool used to manage and track a company's holdings in various securities, such as stocks and bonds. It acts as a central repository for all transaction details related to these securities, ensuring accurate reporting and efficient management of investment portfolio.

**Key Functions of a Securities Account in SAP TRM:**

* **Record Keeping:** Securities accounts are used to log all transactions involving securities, including purchases, sales, and other related activities.
* **Position Management:** They provide a mechanism to manage and value a company's security positions, offering a snapshot of the current holdings.
* **Transaction Tracking:** Each transaction is linked to a specific security account, enabling easy tracking and monitoring of individual securities and their associated activities.
* **Reporting:** Securities accounts facilitate the generation of reports related to securities holdings, transaction history, and valuation, which are crucial for financial reporting and analysis.
* **Integration:** Security accounts are integrated with other components of SAP TRM, such as the Transaction Manager, enabling seamless flow of information and streamlined processes.

Imagine a company has a portfolio of bonds. In SAP TRM, they would create a securities account to manage these bonds. This account would store information about the specific bond types, quantities held, purchase and sale dates, and related transaction details. The account would also be linked to the relevant company code and the bank where the bonds are held. 

The business object mentioned above is used to migrate the associated data.

================================================================================

=======================

title: Kopie von CO - Table COKEY
page_id: 307368364
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307368364
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | table cokey |
| Name (de) | table cokey |
| ET Business Object | CO\_COKEY |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | CO\_COKEY |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | CO\_COKEY |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| COKEY | CO key sub-numbers | CO-Key Subnummern |  |
| COKEY2 | CO Key Subnumbers Enhancement Table | CO-Key Subnummern Erweiterungstabelle | COKEY |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COKEY
**Name (en):** table cokey
**General Description**
=======================

The table cokey is a standard sap table used to store controlling key entrepreneur data and is available within R/3 SAP systems depending on version and release level.

The field HRKFT in the tables COEP, the summary record tables COS\* and in the AUAS refers   
to the COKEY2 table via the COKEY table and there it is checked whether the segments from the   
COKEY2 table correspond with those from the object numbers.  
If, in the cost object (sales orders, WBS elements, orders, cost centers, reconciliation object)   
the profit center is changed and consequently also the segment assignment, then in COEP, COS\* and   
AUAS, the HRKFT field must be changed so that the segments from COKEY2 match those of the cost objects from the account assignment.   
In COEP, the totals record tables and AUAS, the profit center is not changed over,   
because the profit center is not contained in these tables, but in these tables there is the field   
CO subkey (HRKFT) which refers to the segment.  
Below is a description of the case where the segment is changed due to the change in the profit center.

================================================================================

---
title: Production version
page_id: 124947776
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124947776
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Production Version |
| Name (de) | Fertigungsversion |
| ET Business Object | PP\_PRDVER |
| S/4 Business Object | [S/4 specific handling](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=124947783) |
| Transactional Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| MKAL | Production Versions of Material | Fertigungsversionen zum Material |  |
| MKAL\_AEND | Change History of Production Version (Recipes Only) | Änderungshistorie der Fertigungsversion (nur Rezepte) |  |
| CIF\_PPM\_CHANGED | PPM: Change Transfer - Changed Production Versions | PPM:Änderungsübertragung - Geänderte Fertigungsversionen |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PRDVER
**Name (en):** Production Version
**General Description**
=======================

The production version is the link between a product BOM (Bill of Material) and the process Routing. It determines which alternative BOM is used together with which routing to produce a material or plan a material.   
Production versions are used in material requirements planning (MRP), Sales and Operations Planning (SOP), process order creation, and product costing to select the most suitable task list or recipe and the corresponding material list.

The BO PP\_PRDVER contains the tables required to migrate the Production Versions. The structure is very simple, a header and only 1 child table linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR).

================================================================================

title: FSCM TRM Standing instructions
page_id: 281380112
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/281380112
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM TRM VTB Standing instruction |
| Name (de) | FSCM TRM VTB Standing instruction |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_STAND |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_STAND |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_STAND |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VTBSTA1 | Allocation of Payment Details as Standing Instructions | Zuordnung Zahlungsverbindungen als Standing Instruction |  |
| VTBSTA2 | Standing Instructions for Correspondence | Standing Instructions für Korrespondenzen |  |
| VTBSTA3 | SI for Business Partner: Transaction Authorizations | SI für Geschäftspartner: Geschäftsberechtigungen |  |
| VTBSTA4 | SI for Business Partner: Derived Flows | SI für Geschäftspartner: Abgeleitete Bewegungen |  |
| VTBSTC1 | Components of Payment Details for Standing Instruction | Komponente Zahlungsverbindung für Standing Instruction |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_STAND
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM VTB Standing instruction
**General Description**
=======================

 The business partner in the role of counterparty (TR0151) is a contract partner in a financial transaction. To create a contract for a specific business partner, the company's bank details are entered on the “Payment Details” tab, which are part of this business object.

The “Payment Details” tab (transaction FTR\_EDIT) contains details on payment transactions with the corresponding business partner. This data is not entered separately for every flow, but applies generally for the entire financial transaction. 

An ID with the direction and the currency, as well as a possible restriction to the validity and flow type, is used to assign the payment details to the flows. If several different payment details are possible for a flow, the payment detail with the most precise ID is selected.

================================================================================

title: Material Layer (FIFO)
page_id: 113113394
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/113113394
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FIFO Results Table |
| Name (de) | FIFO Ergebnistabelle |
| ET Business Object | MM\_LAYER2  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60LAYER2) |
| S/4 Business Object |  |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| MYMFT | FIFO Results Table | FIFO Ergebnistabelle |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_LAYER2  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60LAYER2)
**Name (en):** FIFO Results Table
**General Description**
=======================

FIFO valuation is a method that enables you to valuate the stocks of a material as realistically as possible. FIFO (first in, first out) stands for the assumption that the first stocks of a material to be received are the first to be consumed. The value of the stock is therefore calculated based on the last stocks received.

================================================================================

**Name (en):** Product Cost Collector: Master Data/Processing
General Description
===================

The product cost collector (abbreviation: PCC) is used as a cost object in the product cost by period scenario.

A product cost collector collects the actual costs incurred in each period to produce a material. In the process, the product becomes the main cost.

================================================================================

---
title: Maintenance Strategies
page_id: 118230234
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/118230234
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (EN) | Maintenance Strategies |
| Name (DE) | Wartungsstrategien |
| ET Business Object | PM\_MAINTSTRAT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PM60MAINTSTRAT) |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Bezeichnung (DE)** | **Main Table** |
| T351 | Maintenance strategy | Wartungsstrategie | X |
| T351P | Maintenance packages | Wartungspakete |  |
| T351T | Maintenance strategy texts | Wartungsstrategietexte |  |
| T351X | Maintenance strategy package texts | Pakettexte zur Wartungsstrategie |  |

**ET Business Object:** PM\_MAINTSTRAT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PM60MAINTSTRAT)
**Name (en):** Maintenance Strategies
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the maintenance strategies. A maintenance strategy defines the rules for the sequence of planned maintenance work. It contains general scheduling information and can therefore be assigned to as many maintenance task lists (PM task lists) and maintenance plans as required. A maintenance strategy contains maintenance packages in which the following information is defined:

* The cycle in which the individual work should be performed (for example, every two months, every 3,106.86 miles, every 500 operating hours);

* Other data which affects scheduling.

Only the maintenance strategies are migrated through this BO, not the maintenance strategies assigned to the maintenance plans.

================================================================================

---
title: TOA
page_id: 120097108
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120097108
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Archive link tables (TOA) |
| Name (de) | Archive link Tabellen (TOA) |
| ET Business Object | CA\_TOA ( [untill ET 2021 FP 2 : CA60](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/Business+Objects)TOA )  CA\_TOA\_ADD ( since ET 2022 FP2) |
**General Description**
=======================

ArchiveLink is a service integrated in the SAP Web Application Server for linking documents to application documents  in the SAP system (new archiving is using ADMI\_\* tables instead of TOA\*).

The content links created in this way guarantee you permanent access to the documents. You can display a document directly from a related application document.

The following documents can be stored in a storage system using ArchiveLink:

* Incoming documents
* Outgoing documents
* Print lists
* Archive files

Incoming and outgoing documents are linked to the corresponding business objects. The link is written in an ArchiveLink link table (TOA01, TOA02, TOA03 or customer specific one) while the system is running.

Helpful transactions for configuration:

OAC0, OAC3, OACT
**Tables**
==========

**ET Business Object:** CA\_TOA ( [untill ET 2021 FP 2 : CA60](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/Business+Objects)TOA )  CA\_TOA\_ADD ( since ET 2022 FP2)
**Name (en):** Archive link tables (TOA)

================================================================================

**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CKMLCRWIP | Material Ledger: WIP Period Records (Values) | Material-Ledger: Periodensätze WIP (Werte) |  |
| CKMLLACRWIP | ML Activity Types: Period/ Currency Records WIP (Values) | ML-Leistungsarten: Perioden-/Währungssatz  WIP (Werte) | X |
| CKMLLAPP | ML Activity Types: Period Records | ML-Leistungsarten: Periodensatz |  |
| CKMLLAPPWIP | Ml Activity Types: Period Records WIP (Quantities) | ML-Leistungsarten: Periodensätze WIP (Mengen) | X |
| CKMLPPWIP | Material-Ledger: Period Records WIP (Quantities) | Material-Ledger: Periodensätze WIP (Mengen) |  |
| MLWIPHD | WIP Document in Material Ledger - Header | WIP-Beleg im Material Ledger - Kopf | X |
| MLWIPIT | WIP Document in Material Ledger - Lines | WIP-Beleg im Material Ledger - Zeilen | X |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_ML\_WIP
**Name (en):** CO ML WIP
**General Description**
=======================

In the transaction-based production cost posting, the total values for work in process (WIP) are calculated for cost objects based on standard prices.

If you want to valuate WIP at actual prices in actual costing, you must run the job Transaction-based posting - WIP quantity posting for revaluation of actual costing to create WIP quantity documents that are used in the material ledger for this purpose.

This BO collects the documents and values for this purpose

================================================================================

---
title: Migration Approach
page_id: 166633156
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/166633156
---
Overview
========

The target of the CO-PA transition is to display all relevant FI/CO data in a standardized data structure. The separate CO-PA data pool (CE\* tables) is transferred into Universal Ledger (ACDOCA). Different technical approaches are used to ensure that this is completely realized:

If possible, manual postings are completely avoided. If necessary, changes are made to existing documents so that the margin analysis has all relevant information (inbound migration). Depending on the CO-PA record type and functionality, new documents must be created based on the old CO-PA tables (table-based migration).

Inbound migration Procedure
---------------------------

Whenever possible, the Inbound Migration procedure is applied, to ensure the characteristics (PAOBJNR) from costing-based CO-PA are furthered into the new universal ledger correctly. The basic idea here is to manipulate the existing documents in a way, that Margin Analysis can display all data.

First step is to create profitability segments in target system for the newly maintained or copied operating concern. As a migration prerequired CE4\* tables must be built up according to predefined mapping, before the real migration can begin.

During the normal ACDOCA build up from one or different migration sources, FI and CO line items are being matched. For all CO-PA relevant documents, the profitability segment is already attached to the existing CO line-item. Important is, that the object number is adjusted correctly.

As a last step, characteristic in ACDOCA should be enriched. This can be done directly during migration or as separated steps afterwards.

Table based migration
---------------------

For some record types and functionalities, the old CO line-items do not include all relevant information and they can not be reused in an inbound migration procedure. In those cases migration will exclude this documents during ACDOCA built up and individually migrate the documents afterwards, based on CO-PA line item level.

First step is to collect the relevant documents, then bring them into the necessary structure, transport to target system, prepare and migrate into ACDOCA.

Postings
--------

As a third and final option, documents can be excluded from normal migration and be posted in the target system. This can be done manually, via LSMW or ET supported batch input. Obviously, the effort of this approach is comparably high and should be avoided whenever possible.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** MM\_BATCH  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60BATCH)
**Name (en):** Batch
**General Description**
=======================

A batch contains the quantity or partial quantity of a certain material that has been produced according to the same recipe, and represents one homogenous, non- reproducible unit with unique specifications. The batches must be uniquely identifiable via the batch number. Depending on the batch level which is predefined in the customizing the batch number is unique for one material in one plant or in all plants.

================================================================================

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | Service Meldungen |
| ET Business Object | CS\_NOTIFI  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CS60NOTIFI) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| table | Description | Bezeichnung DE | header table |
| IHPA | Plant Maintenance: Partners | Instandhaltung: Partner |  |
| ILOA | PM Object Location and Account Assignment | Standort und Kontierung zum IH-Objekt |  |
| JCDO | Change Documents for Status Object (Table JSTO) | Änderungsbelege zum Statusobjekt (Tabelle JSTO) |  |
| JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) |  |
| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| MCIPMIS | PMIS: Master data characteristics for PMIS before image | PMIS: Stammdatenmerkmale für PMIS before image |  |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |
| **QMEL** | **Quality Notification** | **Qualitätsmeldunq** | X |
| QMFE | Quality notification - items | Qualitaetsmeldung - Positionen |  |
| QMIH | Quality message - maintenance data excerpt | Qualitaetsmeldung - Abschnitt Instandhaltungsdaten |  |
| QMMA | Quality notification - activities | Qualitaetsmeldung - Aktionen |  |
| QMSM | Quality notification - tasks | Qualitaetsmeldung - Maßnahmen |  |
| QMUR | Quality notification - causes | Qualitaetsmeldung - Ursachen |  |
| QNAOD\_AOBJECT | Assigned Objects for Notification Item | Zugeordnete Objekte zur Meldungsposition |  |

**ET Business Object:** CS\_NOTIFI  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CS60NOTIFI)
**Name (en):** Service Notification
**General Description**
=======================

Means with which customer notifications are created and managed in the areas of Plant Maintenance and Customer Service. The following notification types are predefined in the standard system:

* Customer notification

  Informing a customer of a malfunction or damage that occurs
* Service request

  Request for service to be performed
* Activity report

  Documentation of activities that have been performed

In addition to these standard notification types, you can also define your own user-specific notification types.

If a customer device has to be repaired or maintained, the contact person on site at the customer can enter a service notification directly in the SAP system, or inform the customer service department of the problem by telephone, fax or mail. For the latter, the customer service employee for the device vendor or manufacturer enters the notification in the system.

A service notification consists of a notification header and one or more items.

Notification header data is information that is used to identify and manage the notification. It is valid for the whole notification.

Depending on the notification type, the notification items contain data describing in greater detail the problem or malfunction that occurred, or the activity that was performed.

check other BO

is handling the same table

title: SOP
page_id: 124968075
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124968075
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Sales and Operation Planning |
| Name (de) | Sales and Operation Planning |
| ET Business Object | PP\_SOP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60SOP) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

40%

| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| S076 | Sales & Operations Planning | Absatz- und Produktionsgrobpl. | x |
| S076E | S076 - Structural Information | S076 - Strukturinformationen | x |
| PGPL | Sales & Operations Planning (SOP) | Absatz- und Produktionsgrobplanung | x |
| PGPLE | SOP Characteristic Values | Merkmalsausprägungen SOP | x |
| PGMI | Product Group/Member Allocation | Zuordnung Produktgruppe / Mitglied | x |
| PGZU | Product Group/Member Quantity Conversions | Umrechnung Mengen Produktgruppe / Mitglied | x |
| PGAN | Proportional Factors per Planning Object Relation (SOP/LIS) | Anteilsfaktoren je Planobjekt-Beziehung (SOP / LIS) | x |
| P44V | Planning Versions | Planung: Versionen | x |
| P445X | LIS Planning Versions Table | Versionentabelle LIS-Planung | x |
| P445V | Sales and Operations Planning: Versions | Absatz- und Produktionsgrobplanung: Versionen | x |
| P445E | Planning Object Index for Rough-Cut Planning Profiles | Planungsobjekt-Index für Grobplanungsprofile | x |
| P445G | Parameters for PLOB Version\_Key Figure | Parameter zu PLOB-Version\_Kennzahl | x |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_SOP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60SOP)
**Name (en):** Sales and Operation Planning
**General Description**
=======================

Sales & Operations Planning (SOP) is a flexible forecasting and planning tool with which sales, production, and other supply chain targets can be set on the basis of historical, existing, and estimated future data. Rough-cut planning can also be carried out to determine the amounts of the capacities and other resources required to meet these targets. In standard S&OP, you can plan **individual materials** or a group of materials (known as a **product group**).

This BO contains the tables required to migrate the Sales and Operation Planning Data. The BO does not have any relationship to other BO’s. The structure is made of a group of several tables, which don’t have any direct relationship to one another. Each one can be treated on its own for the filters required.

================================================================================

title: SIS: SD Infostructures

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SIS: SD Infostructures |
| Name (de) | SIS: SD Infostructures |
| ET Business Object | (CA\_SIS  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CA60SIS))  Business Object will be created with  BO Status 97 |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**General Description**
=======================

The information systems in logistics are flexible tools for collecting, aggregating and analyzing data from the operative applications (Purchasing, Sales, Production, Inventory Controlling, Plant Maintenance, Quality Management/Inspection Processing). They thus enable you to continually control target criteria and to react in time to exceptional situations.

**ET Business Object:** PP\_KANBAN  (formerly PP\_CTRCYC)  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60CTRCYC)
**Name (en):** Control Cycle
**General Description**
=======================

Control cycle is being use to define for your plant the [production storage bin](https://help.sap.com/viewer/34fc810a607e4ae5a287b6e233b8566f/6.18.13/en-US/0390c95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html) in which a certain material is to be staged for production and with which staging type, within a [production supply area](https://help.sap.com/viewer/34fc810a607e4ae5a287b6e233b8566f/6.18.13/en-US/2fcabd53e3acb64ce10000000a174cb4.html) .

Using the control cycle, you can support several types of material staging in the WM-PP interface. You define the type of material staging for each material in the material master using the Material Staging Indicator:

* [Pick parts](https://help.sap.com/viewer/34fc810a607e4ae5a287b6e233b8566f/6.18.13/en-US/f48fc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html)

For pick parts, you can define one control cycle per production supply area for all materials. This control cycle is then valid for all of the materials in the production supply area, for which you have not defined an individual control cycle.

* [Crate parts](https://help.sap.com/viewer/34fc810a607e4ae5a287b6e233b8566f/6.18.13/en-US/f78fc95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html)
* [Release order parts](https://help.sap.com/viewer/34fc810a607e4ae5a287b6e233b8566f/6.18.13/en-US/29cabd53e3acb64ce10000000a174cb4.html)
* Manual material staging

You execute manual staging without reference to a production order. The material is staged in the production storage bin that you have defined in the corresponding control cycle.

For manual staging, you can define one control cycle per production supply area for all materials. This control cycle is then valid for all the materials in the production supply area, for which you have not defined an individual control cycle.

* No WM staging

Materials with this indicator cannot be staged via the WMS.

In additional, you clearly define in the control cycle the production storage bin , in which the particular material is to be staged. For this, you must have defined a WM-managed production supply area in the material master or in the BOM.

================================================================================

---
title: CO - Totals Records
page_id: 117445356
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445356
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| Name (de) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| ET Business Object | CO\_SUM  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60SUM) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_SUM\_SDT  CO\_COSP\_SDT (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_COSS\_SDT (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_SUM\_COSPD\_SDT ( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COSPD  ) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_SUM\_COSSD\_SDT ( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COSSD  ) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_SUM\_COST\_SDT   ( until ET 2021 FP2 COS4SUM\_COST ) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE | General Object Number |  |  |
| COSB | CO Object: Total Variances/Results Analyses | CO-Objekt: Summen Abweichungen/Abgrenzungen | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSBD | CO Object: Total of variances/accruals, of which settled | CO-Objekt: Summen Abweichgen/Abgrenzgen - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSL | CO Object: Activity Type Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Leistungsarten | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSLD | CO Object: Activity Type Totals - of Which Settled | CO-Objekt: Summen Leistungsarten - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSP | Generated Table for View | Generated Table for View | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPD | CO Object: Settled Primary Cost Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Kosten extern - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPP | Transfer of the Order in the COSP Table to the Project | Übernahme der COSP-Tabelle Auftrag auf Projekt | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPR | CO Objekt: Enhancement to COSP for Dependency Planning | CO-Objekt: Erweiterung der COSP für Rezeptplanung | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSR | CO Object: Statistical Key Figure Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Statistische Kennzahlen | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSS | Generated Table for View | Generated Table for View | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSSD | CO Object: Settled Secondary Cost Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Kosten intern - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSSP | Transfer of the Order COSS Table to the Project | Übernahme der COSS-Tabelle Auftrag auf Projekt | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_SUM  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60SUM)
**Name (en):** CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE)
General Description
===================

CO totals tables are named COS\*, so for instance COSP, COSS, COSPR or COSLD.

This object contains the CO totals records. Totals records can be reconstructed using report RKACOR04. The program compares the CO summary files COSP and COSS with the line item file COEP. There are also some other analyses / correction reports, e.g. for [comparison with settlement data](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/Tables+of+actual+settlement+and+analysis).

The period value of the totals records is adjusted to the total of the corresponding line items.   
When you start the report, you can decide whether only actual costs (value types 04 and 11) and parked documents and/or commitment values are to be included

All totals records for which a difference is found in a period between the totals record and the total of the corresponding line items are listed.   
If you start the report with update, the totals records with errors are corrected. However, totals values of a period for which no more line items exist are not changed, since the line items may already have been reorganized.  
If you convert/harmonize organizational units or master data, it is therefore necessary to delete the totals records for the data concerned from the totals table beforehand!   
The program works object by object. According to the selection criteria, all valid objects are determined from the /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE and the adjustment is then carried out (individually) for these. For this reason, you should restrict the object numbers to objects that actually have costs and where errors are suspected. There are cases in which it makes sense to pack the summary tables into a separate BO for each table. You select the object numbers using transaction /CBSGMBH/DMF\_SEL\_OBJNR (for a detailed explanation, see: Dependencies in the Pre-Selector (/CBSGMBH/DMF\_SEL\_OBJNR). The relevant object numbers are selected via table /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE. This then serves as a selection table for the totals tables.

The COSR is the totals table for statistical key figures. As can be seen in the Customizing table for statistical key figures (TKA03), however, there are two types of statistical key figures: Total values and fixed values.  
Total values must be added up, but not fixed values. The only way I can see how to do this with the SHC Framework / ET is to make a separate selection for statistical totals and fixed values in a separate BO.

All mappings that affect summary tables must not contain any superfluous mapping, that is, source value = target value. This is because all values contained in the mapping are added up - even if this is not desired with superfluous mapping. This error is known to ET development, but has not been fixed so far, because the functionality actually "works as designed". The mapping of cost elements, cost centers (contained in object numbers) and other CO totals key fields had to be checked and unnecessary mapping removed before the conversion! Therefore, an OIE FuBa was used in many projects, which only adds entries for which a field in the key has changed. A current version of this footbas is available at Continental: \*OIE\_CO\_SUM\_TAB. The OIE is to flow back into the ET shortly. This makes the following point superfluous:  
If there are cases in which no key field is mapped, then a separate BO must be created and this imported WITHOUT "add totals" - in the hope that records with this key do not yet exist in the target.

Backups via BACK\_\*

CO Totals status quo needs to be backed up before importing the totals tables in order to preserve the data. In case the import doesn't go smoothly / the results are not useful, the backup BOs can be imported with no mapping. But first a cleansing of the totals tables needs to be performed, see below

Deletion via CO60DEL\_CO\* (not implemented in LU ONE)

There are several possibilities in case CO Totals need to be corrected/reversed to the status quo.

For this purpose it is advisable to cleanse the CO totals and reimport the data as it was before the migration. For the cleansing, deletion BOs are created which resemble the backup BOs but the programs for their execution are different.

In step 1 the deletion BOs are executed (anaylsis and deletion)

In step 2 the backup BOs are imported (their export should have been performed before the start of the migration/conversion, no changes to the export to be made!)

This scenario delivers good results in case no postings have been done meanwhile (in the time between the export of the backups and now). If there are postings, the totals have been changed accordingly and a totals backup import won't set up the totals to a realistic state.

At this point, please use an alternative if applicable. The import using "add totals" that needs to be corrected can be "reversed" via new import using reverse algebraic sign.

================================================================================

All Tables

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_IHC
**Name (en):** FSCM Inhouse Cash Payment
**General Description**
=======================

Used to record a payment transaction. An IHC payment order documents the payment from a bank account to a recipient account and comprises an ordering party item and a recipient item. The IHC payment order serves as a bracket for these payment items, but does not actually represent a posting.

================================================================================

space: unknown

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | PLM Process |
| Name (de) |  |
| ET Business Object | PLM\_PROCESS |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| /PLMB/RPR\_HDR | Process |  | X |
| /PLMB/RPR\_PE | Process Element |  | X |
| /PLMB/RPR\_EQR | Equipment Requirements |  |  |
| /PLMB/RPR\_PP\_M | Recipe Process Parameter Master Data |  |  |
| /PLMB/RPR\_PP\_VAL | Process Parameter Values |  |  |
| /PLMB/RCP\_DESCR | Descriptions |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** PLM\_PROCESS
**Name (en):** PLM Process
**General Description**
=======================

PLM Process bring all the related processes, actions and stages within the recipe. Once the PLM recipe is upgraded or transfer to productive, these processes are converted on the Routings.

This also includes the process parameters

In order to obtain all the descriptions for the parameters and the elements, the table /PLMB/RCP\_DESCR needs to be used more than once within the BO Hierarchy.

3 views were created to be able to link with the different tables in the BO

ZCBS\_V\_RCP\_DESC  
ZCBS\_V\_RCP\_DESCM  
ZCBS\_V\_RCP\_DESCP

Details for the views

Long texts are very important since they include additional information relevant for the processes.

================================================================================

**Name (en):** CO Header without WRT 4 and 11
**General Description**
=======================

This BO migrates all CO Header entries that do not contain value type 4 or 11.  
These headers are not necessarily relevant for the structure of the ACDOCA, but if there are mixed documents (CO/FI WRT 4 and 11 and pure CO documents WRT not equal to 4 and 11), it must be ensured that the headers from the ACDOCA structure are not overwritten and therefore import type 4 for the COBK

**ET Business Object:** CS\_EQUIPM

**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the equipment. An equipment represents a physical object which can be independently maintained in the industry. It is an asset that can be moved from one place to another. Examples of equipment can be transportation utilities, measurement devices, PC systems, machine spare parts, etc.

================================================================================

---
title: 6. Retired Assets migration via LSMW
page_id: 151102933
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/151102933
---
Overview
========

Given an S4 mid-of-year Assets migration hybrid approach where master data is created via DB update and the values via idocs, the migration runs smooth until the customer adds into the scope the retired assets during the current fiscal year. The transactions in scope were ~~let's say~~ challenging to be posted by using idocs. For this reason an LSMW approach was proved to work, safe & sound.

But to make this happen, we had to follow some steps that this article will describe. For instance, LSMW was used only to post the retirements, hence, the cumulative APC + depreciations of the asset had to be there already, which means we have to "split" the idoc and take the retirement transactions out of it.

This article will cover the over all process and also include function modules needed, the LSWM project, etc.

*This approach should be done also in LTMC.*

*\*: Migration of legacy asset values can be performed in two approaches:*

* ***Start-of-year migration**: Which migrates the cumulative values of APC + depreciation at the beginning of the FY. (This is less complex).*
* ***Mid-of-year migration**: Which includes all the transactions until the transfer date.*

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_DIFT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Persistent flow selectors
**General Description**
=======================

The persistent flow types are migrated based on the DIFT\_POS\_IDENT table. In SAP Treasury, a persistent flow type represents the basic classification of financial flows within the system. These flow types are used to categorize different types of cash flows, such as interest payments, principal repayments, or fees, and are crucial for managing and analyzing financial transactions in the Treasury and Risk Management module.

================================================================================

---
**Overview**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Quantity Ledger |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TRQT |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TRQT |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TRQT |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TRQT\_BUSTRANS | Treasury: Business Transactions | Treasury: Geschäftvorfälle |  |
| TRQT\_FLOW | Treasury: Flow Data Quantity Ledger | Treasury: Bewegungsdaten Mengenbuch |  |
| TRQT\_FLOWGROUP | Treasury: Transaction Flow Groups | Treasury: Geschäftsbewegungsgruppen |  |
| TRQT\_POSFLOW | Treasury: Position Flows | Treasury: Bestandsbewegungen |  |
| TRQT\_POSITION | Treasury: Quantity Ledger Position | Treasury: Mengenbuchbestand | X |
| TRQT\_SUBTOTAL | Treasury: Table with Quantity Ledger Position Trans. Figures | Treasury: Tabelle mit Mengenbuchbestandsverkehrszahlen |  |
| TRQT\_TRAFLOW | Treasury: Transaction Flows | Treasury: Geschäftsbewegungen | X |
| TRST\_CLASFLO | CFM: Class Flows in Securities Account | CFM: Klass. Bewegungen in Depot | x |
| TRST\_CLASFLO\_REV | CFM: Reversed Class Flows in Securities Account | CFM: Stornierte Depotgattungsbestandsbewegungen |  |
| TRST\_CLASPOS | Treasury: Class position in Securities Account | Treasury: Klass. Position in Depot |  |
| TRST\_SECACC\_BANK | Payment Details for Securities Account | Zahlungsverbindungen für Depot |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TRQT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Quantity Ledger
**General Description**
=======================

The Treasury Quantity Ledger serves as a repository for granular information related to treasury transactions, including position components like units, nominal amounts, acquisition values, and book values. 

* **Key Features:**

  + **Position Values:** Stores the numerical values associated with each treasury position, such as the number of units, the nominal amount, etc.
  + **Position Components:** Holds detailed information about different components of a position, such as hedge adjustments, effective amounts, and ineffective amounts.

The data stored in the Treasury Quantity Ledger is used for various reporting and analysis purposes, such as preparing treasury reports, assessing risk exposure, and managing financial positions. The associated data is migrated on the basis of this business object.

================================================================================

| PKEK | Error Log for Kanban (Event-Driven KANBAN) | Fehlerprotokoll für Kanban (Ereignisgesteuertes Kanban) |  |
| PKPS | Control Cycle Item / Kanban | Regelkreisposition / Kanban |  |
| PKSD | Control Cycle Item/Additional Data for Kanban With Delivery | Regelkreisposition / Zusatzdaten für Kanban mit Lieferung |  |
| PKCH | Batches and Quantities in KANBAN | Chargen und Mengen in Kanban |  |
| PKEN | Dummy Table for Creating a Block.Object for Sec.Key Tab.PKHD | Dummy-Tab. zur Anlage eines Sperrobj. f. |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_KANBAN  ( till ET 2022 FP 0 MM\_KANBA )  ( till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60KANBA)
**Name (en):** PP - Kanban
**General Description**
=======================

The KANBAN method for controlling production and material flow is based on the actual stock quantity in production. Material that is required on a regular basis is continually provided in small quantities in production. Replenishment or the production of a material is only triggered when a higher production level actually requires the material. 

KANBAN replenishment is controlled by various different replenishment strategies. The replenishment strategies determine in which way the kanban quantity is produced or procured.

================================================================================

---
title: Kopie von CO - Object Numbers
page_id: 307369473
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307369473
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) |  |
| Name (de) |  |
| ET Business Object | N/A |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | N/A |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
**General Description**
=======================

We have heard of the object number so many times and yet, what is it for? What are the OBJNR? Why do we need to collect OBJNR? How do we collect them and what do we do with the information?

The concept of the OBJNR in SAP:

The OBJNR are widely spread around the system, they are data carriers and often represent the technical link between different sources.

In projects, we need to pay attention to the OBJNR because of:

* Selection (no other way to identify the OrgUnits in scope, so we need to use the OBJNR)
* Mapping (if we convert the system, the OBJNR may be affected, or they can help us map other fields)

To start with, there are different OBJNR in SAP and to differentiate and handle them correctly, we should pay attention to the difference (manifested in their domain).

The most used ones are:

There are the J\_OBJNR, e.g. in COSP, COEP, AUFK, ANLA etc.

There are the GOBJNR, e.g. in GLPCT, GLFUNCT, COFIT etc.

There are the RKEOBJNR, e.g. in CE1\*, BSEG, COEP etc.

This article is handling the J\_OBJNR domain.

**Usage of OBJNR**
------------------

Where do we find J\_OBJNR mostly? E.g. they are widely spread in CO, just a look at the CO totals tables reveals their role/uniqueness as they are part of the table key:

There might be OBJNR and PAROB/USPOB/PAROB1 etc (partner, source OBJNR etc.)

There are different object types = different OBART (in German OBART = Objektart = object type). Depending on the type, the OBJNR will be constructed differently (as per shown below, see table TBO00).

**OBJNR technical tables**
--------------------------

The OBJNR are used across the system, you can find them in multiple tables and fields. But each unique OBJNR is stored in some technical tables, in addition:

* ONR00 – containing all existing OBJNR (attention: most customer systems are not consistent, so some OBJNR will be either missing or obsolete here)
* ONRXX- for some OBART available (e.g. table ONRAN for AN\*, ONRKL for KL\* etc.)
* Some OBJNR are also found in the logistic status tables JEST, JCDO, JSTO, JCDS etc.
* Some OBJNR are also stored in tables COBRA and COBRB

In table TBO00 you can find the definition of each OBART.

TBO00 reveals the following important points:

* Source/entity table per OBART (TABNAME)
* Is there an index ONRXX table for the particular OBART
* How is the OBJNR composed – see fields KEY01-KEY07
* Do we have internal numbering for the OBART

Example, AN:

AN\* are assigned to assets, they are composed from a concatenation of BUKRS, ANLN1, ANLN2 and all unique AN\*OBJNR can be found in table ANLA. Technically, there is an index table ONRAN, in addition to ONR00.

How does it work for the most OBART?

Example:

AN – definition of AN\* numbers is ruled by entity table ANLA. In table ANLA are “created” all unique AN\* OBJNR that will exist in the system:

(Grouped by OBJNR NE empty)

In table ONR00 and ONRAN there is the exact same amount (here 2427):

Note: very often in the customer systems the data is not consistent, which leads to different amount of OBJNR in the ONRXX tables. The truth is usually in the entity table!

Note: you will find the AN\* OBJNR all across the system, e.g. settlement rules:

Here we talk about the USAGE of the OBJNR as a link/data carrier, not of its unique definition.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** WM\_DOC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 WM60DOC)
**Name (en):** WM Documents
**General Description**
=======================

Every time a stock movement is performed to and from the Warehouse, the details of the movement are captured in the tables contained in this BO. 

Any time a movement is performed to move stock in/out of Warehouse, the information is stored in the table contained in this BO.

===================

They can be used at the beginning of the planning phase, but also in the design phase, or by the planning of new products.

With base planning, existing material cost estimates can also be changed.

| TRFT\_ATTR\_L | Values for Long attributes | Werte für lange Attribute | X |
| TRFT\_ATTR\_L\_T | Texts for Long Attribute Values | Texte für Werte für lange Attribute |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TRFT\_ATT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Attributes
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP Treasury and Risk Management (TRM), TRFT\_ATTR\*-tables are used to store attributes associated with financial instrument types. Having this attribute allows for a more granular view of instruments, facilitating detailed risk analysis, reporting, and adherence to financial regulations.

Relevant data is therefore migrated with the help of this business object.

================================================================================

After the data has been collected accordingly with the /CBSGMBH/DMF\_OP\_EXP [Opening Balance on Database level (cbs-consulting.de)](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=260246559&moved=true)  programme,

documents are now created from this data in this BO.

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Service Package  Model Service Specifications |
| Name (de) | Service Leistungspaket  Muster-Leistungsverzeichnis |
| ET Business Object | MM\_SRVPAC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60SRVPAC)  MM\_MSSPECS ( since ET 2023 FP1) |
| S/4 Business Object | S4 specfic |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| ESLH | Service Package Header Data | Leistungspaket Kopfdaten | X |
| ESUH | Ext. Services Management: Unpl. Service Limits: Header Data | Dienstleistungsabw. unpeplante Leistungslimits Kopfdaten |  |
| ESLL | Lines of Service Package | Zeilen des Leistungspakets |  |
| ESKL | Account Assignment Specification: Service Line | Kontierungszuordnung: Leistungszeile |  |
| ESUC | Ext. Services Management: Unplanned Limits on Contract Item | Dienstleistungsabw. Ungeplante Limits auf Kontraktpos. |  |
| ESUP | Ext. Services Management: Unpl. Limits on Service Package | Dienstleistungsabw. ungeplante Limits auf Leistungspakete |  |
| ESUS | Services Management: Unplanned Limits on Service Types | Dienstleistungsabw. Ungeplante Limits auf Leistungsbereiche |  |
| TMP\_SPEC | Model Service Specifications | Muster-Leistungsverzeichnis |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_SRVPAC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60SRVPAC)  MM\_MSSPECS ( since ET 2023 FP1)
**Name (en):** Service Package  Model Service Specifications
**General Description**
=======================

When creating service POs (item category 9, EKPO-PSTYP = ‘9’) each PO line item has a package number. This package number contains detailed information about the service itself. Same is true for purchase requisitions and service entry sheets.

Table ESLH has 1 entry for the outline and 1 entry for the service package. The field **ESLH-HPACKNO** groups this information together. When you use the view **ML\_ESLL** you can display service lines together with their package header data.

For example a PO item can have just services, or services and limits. The ESLH package number stands for the subpackage number (**ESLL-SUB\_PACKNO**), which contains the services.

You can find in the tables ESLL and ESKL the details to your service package with the packno from ESLH which is different from the HPACKNO.

You can have different outline levels in your service specification.

You define the assignment of the services to the individual outline levels via the subpackage number.

================================================================================

title: Storage Units
page_id: 286005120
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/286005120
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Storage Units |
| Name (de) | Lagereinheiten |
| ET Business Object | WM\_STORAGE |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| LEIN | Storage unit header records | Lagereinheiten-Kopfsätze |  |

**ET Business Object:** WM\_STORAGE
**Name (en):** Storage Units
**General Description**
=======================

Storage unit (SU) management in the Warehouse Management (WM) application component makes it possible to optimize warehouse capacity and control material flow using storage units within the warehouse.

A storage unit is a logical grouping of one or several amounts of material such as a pallet or a container that can be managed within a warehouse as a unit that belongs together. Storage units can be either homogeneous (containing one material item only) or mixed (containing two or more material items).

All storage units, whether the materials are stored on standard pallets, wire baskets or other containers, are assigned an identifier a number which is maintained in the system as the storage unit number. Therefore, it is possible at any given time to know where each storage unit is located in the warehouse complex, the amount of material contained in it, and which operations have been processed or planned for it.

When SU management is not active in a storage type, all stock is managed as separate quants at the storage bin level. With SU management, stock is managed at the pallet or storage unit level. A storage bin can have one or more storage units. Similarly, each storage unit can consist of one or more quants.

================================================================================

| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |

**ET Business Object:** VC\_KONF
**Name (en):** Configuration data IBase tables
**General Description**
=======================

Variant configuration is for manufacturing complex products. The manufacturer is always having to offer new variants of its products. Often, new variants are created by modifying existing product designs as you process the order. You do not need to create a separate material for each variant of a product in your company. You can use one configurable material to cover all variants.

The configuration data of an object is stored in some IB\* tables. The storage is called iBase.

The IBase is not only used for variant configuration but also for PM installations (PM\_IBASE). Only entries for IBase category '02' (IBIB-IBTYP) are relevant for variant configuration.

================================================================================

| DRAD | Document-Object Link | Verknüpfung Dokument-Objekt |  |
| DRAD\_LAST\_CHANGE | DMS: Last Changes to Object Link | DVS: Letzte Änderungen an Objektverknüpfung |  |
| DRAO | Originals for documents | Originale für Dokumente |  |
| DRAOZ | Additional Files for DMS Originals | Zusatzdateien zu Originalen Dokumentenverwaltung |  |
| DRAP | Document Log File | Protokolldatei Dokument |  |
| DRAT | Texts for Document Info Records | Kurztexte für Dokuinfosätze |  |
| DRAW | Document Info Record | Dokumentinformationssatz | X |
| DRAW\_LAST\_CHANGE | DMS: Last Changes to Document | DMS: Letzte Änderungen am Dokument |  |
| DRAZ | Table for Additional Files for DMS Originals | Tabelle für Zusatzdateien zu Original Dokumentenverwaltung |  |
| ESTUSRTEMPLSET | WWI Table for Template and User Settings | WWI-Tabelle für Vorlagen und Benutzereinstellungen |  |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |
| ONRDO | Object number index for documents | Objeknummernindex für Dokumente |  |

**General Description**

Documents carry information for users/individuals or between systems. This information may be kept in the form of text, graphics, programs, or technical drawings. A complete object will use all of this information to describe its content. The term document info record is used in reference to the SAP System master data record that stores the business information within the document. Metadata for a document is stored within the document info record while the original file maintains the genuine information in the document. The actual document may be located in a location other than the SAP System, such as an email attachment on the users PC. Document info record links the outside attachment with the SAP System.

Information contained within the document info record is as follows:

* Character descriptive data
* Control function
* Original application file data
* Automatically copied system data

Document key fields such as: Document number, Document type, Document part, and Document version are used to identify document within SAP systems

**ET Business Object:** PLM\_DOCINF     PLM\_DOCINF\_DRAD    PLM\_DOCINF\_DRAW  (ET 2023 FP2 or higher)
**Name (en):** Document Info Record

================================================================================

title: Additionals: Documents
page_id: 274578215
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274578215
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Additionals: Documents |
| Name (de) | Verkaufshilfsmittel: Belege |
| ET Business Object | RET\_ADDIT  ~~Z\_RET\_ADDIT~~ |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| WTADAB | Additionals: Documents | Verkaufshilfsmittel: Belege |  |
| WTADABT | Additionals: Documents: Supplementary texts | Verkaufshilfsmittel: Belege: Zusatztexte |  |
| WTADDI | Additionals for a material | Verkaufshilfsmittel eines Materials |  |
| WTADFMCU | Additional: External/Internal Procedure for Additionals | Verkaufshilfsmittel: Externe/interne Anbringungsmethode |  |
| WTAD\_SUP\_FM | Additionals: Vendor-specific Procedure for Additionals | Verkaufshilfsmittel: Anbringungsmethoden eines Lieferanten |  |

**ET Business Object:** RET\_ADDIT  ~~Z\_RET\_ADDIT~~
**Name (en):** Additionals: Documents
**General Description**
=======================

### Retail (IS-R)

Additionals are assigned to materials for sale to ensure effective sales presentation or to support logistics as a service.

An additional has a separate material master and represents either a material (for example, ticket) or a service (for example, pressing clothing for display).

#### Example

* Materials

+ Tickets (price labels for customers)
+ Care labels
+ Security tags
+ Swing tickets
+ Clothes hangers
+ Packaging, in particular for private labels

* Services

+ Pressing clothing for display
+ Arranging clothing on hangers
+ Pre-packing by a vendor as a supplementary logistics service for floor-ready merchandise

These tables belong to the **“Additionals Management”** (German: *Verkaufshilfsmittelverwaltung*) component in **SAP Retail**.  
They are used to manage **supplementary items** or **add-on materials** that are associated with a main article — for example, **hangers, price tags, packaging materials, or promotional labels** that accompany a product when it is delivered or sold.

---

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Explanation / Functional Role** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **WTADAB** | Additionals: Documents | Stores **documents or records** that define which additionals (sales aids) are assigned to a specific article, site, or vendor. Each record can represent a document linking main article and its additional(s). |
| **WTADABT** | Additionals: Documents – Supplementary Texts | Contains **language-dependent descriptive texts** or notes for the documents stored in `WTADAB`. Used for user-facing information in Retail maintenance or printing. |
| **WTADDI** | Additionals for a Material | Core table that defines **which additional materials** (e.g., hangers, tags, polybags) are linked to a **main material**. Each entry describes the relationship between the main article and its required sales aids. |
| **WTADFMCU** | Additionals: External/Internal Procedure for Additionals | Stores the **method of attachment or handling** for each additional — e.g., *externally applied by vendor* or *internally applied in DC or store*. Controls logistics and cost responsibility. |
| **WTAD\_SUP\_FM** | Additionals: Vendor-specific Procedure for Additionals | Defines **vendor-specific handling procedures** for additionals. For example, a certain vendor may deliver garments already on hangers, while another ships them flat-packed. This allows differentiation per supplier. |

---

### 🔶 **Functional Summary**

* “Additionals” are *auxiliary items* required for sale, logistics, or presentation of a main article.
* These tables collectively determine:

  1. **What** add-ons are needed (`WTADDI`),
  2. **Who** applies or delivers them (internal vs. external, `WTADFMCU`, `WTAD_SUP_FM`),
  3. **Where** they are documented (`WTADAB`), and
  4. **How** they are communicated (texts in `WTADABT`).

They are key in **fashion and hard goods retail**, where logistics and presentation standards differ per vendor and site.

---

### 🔶 **In Business Process Terms**

1. **Article Master Maintenance (MM41/MM42)** → define additionals per article (`WTADDI`).
2. **Vendor Collaboration** → vendor-specific procedures (`WTAD_SUP_FM`).
3. **Replenishment / Purchase Order Creation** → system checks whether required additionals must be ordered or provided.
4. **Inbound Logistics** → determines whether goods arrive with or without additionals based on `WTADFMCU`.

---

### 🔶 **S/4HANA Retail & Fashion Transformation**

* The concept of *Additionals Management* remains, but is streamlined:

  + Data exposed via **Fiori apps** (*Manage Additionals*, *Display Article Master (Retail)*).
  + Integrated with **Article Master Business Object (Retail Article API)** and **Material BOMs** (for structured packaging relationships).
  + CDS Views: `I_AdditionalMaterial`, `I_AdditionalProcurementProcedure`.
* **Clean Core recommendation:** do not enhance these tables directly — use BAdIs or APIs for additional logic.
* In **SAP Fashion**, similar concepts apply under *Hanger/Flatpack management*.

================================================================================

title: Supply Area
page_id: 124951890
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124951890
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Supply Area |
| Name (de) | Produktionsversorgungsbereich |
| ET Business Object | PP\_SUPARE  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60SUPARE) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| PVBE | Production Supply Area | Produktionsversorgungsbereich | X |
| PVKT | Supply Area Short Text | PVB Kurztext |  |

40%

**ET Business Object:** PP\_SUPARE  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60SUPARE)
**Name (en):** Supply Area
**General Description**
=======================

In Kanban processing, production supply areas (PSAs) divide the plant. The components required for production are stored in these PSAs and and also the information which number of components are or have to be available (for example, three full containers). If a container is empty, it is set to "empty" and replenishment is triggered. Various work centers take what they need from them. A Kanban control cycle is defined to specify how a material should be obtained within a PSA.

The BO PP\_SUPARE contains the tables required to migrate the Supply Areas. The structure is very simple, a header and only 1 child table linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR). The BO does not have any direct relationship to any other BO.

================================================================================

---
title: Replenishment
page_id: 274578193
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274578193
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Replenishment |
| Name (de) | Nachschub |
| ET Business Object | RET\_REPL  ~~Z\_RET\_REPL~~ |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| WRPD | Replenishment: documents created | Nachschub: erzeugte Belege |  |
| WRPE | Replenishment: Error messages | Nachschub: Fehlermeldungen |  |
| WRPL | Replenishment: quantities per customer/material | Nachschub: Mengen pro Kunde/Artikel |  |
| WRPP | Replenishment: parameters at material/req. group level | Nachschub: Parameter auf Material/Bedarfsgruppen-Ebene |  |
| WRPR | Replenishment: replenishment runs | Nachschub: Nachschubläufe | X |
| WRPT | Replenishment: movement data | Nachschub: Bewegungsdaten |  |

**ET Business Object:** RET\_REPL  ~~Z\_RET\_REPL~~
**Name (en):** Replenishment
**General Description**
=======================

**Replenishment** is a procedure for the demand-oriented merchandise supply of recipients (stores or external customers). When executing replenishment, the requirements are determined using the stock situation and then follow-on documents (purchase requisitions, purchase orders, sales orders, or outbound deliveries) are generated automatically for the merchandise supply. Store replenishment in SAP Retail is executed using the **Rapid Replenishment** function.

Replenishment for customers is executed within the framework of Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI), to carry out the requirements planning as a service. In that case, the retailer must have access to the customer's stock and sales data. Customer replenishment in SAP Retail is executed using the **Customer Replenishment (VMI)** function.

These tables belong to the **Retail Replenishment (Nachschubsteuerung)** component in SAP Retail.  
They store **master data, control parameters, and runtime results** for automated **store replenishment** — i.e., how stock is replenished from a distribution center or vendor to stores based on demand, sales, and forecast data.

---

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Explanation / Functional Role** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **WRPR** | Replenishment: Replenishment Runs | **Master table** – each replenishment run (batch job or simulation) is stored here. Contains run ID, date/time, processing type (e.g., DC → Store, Vendor → DC), and controlling parameters. It’s the central header for all replenishment activities. |
| **WRPP** | Replenishment: Parameters at Material/Requirement Group Level | Stores **customized control parameters** that influence how replenishment is calculated for each material or requirement group (e.g., safety stock logic, minimum quantities, rounding profiles). |
| **WRPL** | Replenishment: Quantities per Customer/Material | Contains **calculated replenishment quantities** per store (customer) and material from a specific run. This is the operational result of the replenishment calculation. |
| **WRPT** | Replenishment: Movement Data | Holds **historical movement data** (e.g., sales, stock, goods receipt) used as input for replenishment calculation and forecasting. Acts as a transactional reference. |
| **WRPD** | Replenishment: Documents Created | Logs all **follow-on documents** created during a replenishment run (e.g., purchase orders, stock transfer orders, delivery documents). Enables traceability of generated logistics documents. |
| **WRPE** | Replenishment: Error Messages | Contains **error or warning messages** that occurred during replenishment processing (e.g., missing master data, blocked sites, insufficient stock). Used for monitoring and troubleshooting. |

---

### 🔶 **Functional Summary**

Together, these tables represent the **end-to-end Replenishment Framework** in SAP Retail:

1. **WRPR** – defines the replenishment run.
2. **WRPP** – defines parameters (how replenishment behaves).
3. **WRPT** – collects movement and demand data.
4. **WRPL** – calculates replenishment quantities per store/material.
5. **WRPD** – generates and logs follow-on documents.
6. **WRPE** – captures any processing errors.

---

### 🔶 **Transformation / S/4HANA Context**

* The framework is still valid in **S/4HANA Retail and Fashion**, but integrated tighter with:

  + **MRP Areas & Site Replenishment (Fiori Apps)**
  + **SAP CAR Replenishment Planning (CAR-RP)** or **F&R (Forecasting & Replenishment)**
* `WRPR` remains the **core control table**, while transactional and analytical access is handled via **CDS Views** and APIs (`I_ReplenishmentRun`, `C_ReplenishmentOverviewQuery`).

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** MD\_FSCM\_IHC
**Name (en):** FSCM IHC Bank Account Master data
**General Description**
=======================

The account is the central object of the current account system.

Accounts within the current account system can be used for the usual purposes of a current account (sight deposit account). The components of a current account, such as balance-oriented balancing and the processing of payment transactions, are supported.

Time deposits (fixed-term deposits, call money, deposits at notice), savings bonds (normal interest savings bonds) and savings deposits with an agreed or 3-month notice period are also supported.

================================================================================

| EKETH | Scheduling Agreement Schedules: History Tables | Lieferplaneinteilungen Historientabelle |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_PURDOC  MM\_PURDOC\_K
**Name (en):** MM Request for quotation MM Purchase order MM Contract MM Scheduling agreement
**General Description**
=======================

Purchasing documents are external documents which are used by Purchasing to procure materials or services.

This BO contains all kind of external purchasing documents (excluding purchase requisitions which are only for internal purposes).

This BO no longer contains contracts as its selection is different. Also, the table are different: table EKAB (release documentation) is only relevant for MM\*PURDOC\_K.

title: FSCM TRM Hedge Accounting

space: unknown

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | Hedge Accounting |

| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_VTV\_HEDGE |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_VTV\_HEDGE |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VTVBAR | NPVs of OTC transactions | Barwerte von OTC-Geschäften |  |
| VTVBAR\_HEDGE | Key Figures for Hedge Accounting | Kennzahlen für Hedge Accounting |  |
| VTVSZWPKUR | Scenario Database: Security Prices | Szenariodatenbank: Wertpapierkurse |  |
| VTVSZWPKUV | Scenario Database: Security Price Volatilities | Szenariendatenbank: Wertpapierkursvolatilitäten |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_VTV\_HEDGE
**Name (en):** Hedge Accounting
**General Description**
=======================

SAP's Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) module includes comprehensive capabilities for hedge accounting, allowing businesses to manage and report on financial instruments used to mitigate risk. This includes features for hedge accounting under various accounting standards like IFRS and US-GAAP. The TRM module facilitates the entire hedge accounting process, from identifying exposures to managing hedging instruments and recording the accounting implications. By integrating hedge accounting with SAP TRM's exposure management and trading functions, companies can more efficiently manage their risks and hedge their exposures. 

The associated data is migrated using the FSCM TRM Hedge Accounting business object.

================================================================================

Detailed Business Partners Information

Here you can find very detailed Business Partner information

[Business Partner Concept](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCMDM/Business+Partner+Concept) incl. detailed Q&A page [Business Partner Q&A](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=88772540) .

**ET Business Object:** MD\_BP\_CP  MD\_BP\_CUST  MD\_BP\_VEND  MD\_BP\_CRM\_STATUS  MD\_BP\_COMMON  MD\_BP\_SETTING
**Name (en):** Business Partner  Contact Person  Vendors  Customers
**General Description**
=======================

An organization (company, subsidiary), person or group of people or organizations in which your company has a business interest.

You can enter and manage your business partner centrally with regard to various business transactions.

This is particularly relevant if a business partner has various roles for a company, such as sold-to party and ship-to party.

A business partner can be created in various business partner roles and can take on additional business partner roles over the course of the business relationship. It is not necessary to reenter general data for a business partner, which is not dependent on their function or on application-specific enhancements, meaning that entry and storage of redundant data can be avoided.

Structure
---------

The following basic elements are available in the business partner data:

Business partner concepts

| Concept | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| Business partner category | Data that is available for your business transactions is also dependent on the business partner category. Depending on the category (organization, person or group) different fields are mandatory and available in the BP. |
| Business partner role | A business partner can therefore adopt various business partner roles, which allow different views of the business partner according to the business function involved. |
| Business partner relationship | A relationship links two business partners and is defined by business partner relationship categories. |

Relationship Customer/Vendor-BP-Contact Person
----------------------------------------------

Helpful views are

**V\_CVI\_CUST\_CT\_LI**  
**V\_CVI\_CUST\_LINK**  
**V\_CVI\_VEND\_CT\_LI**  
**V\_CVI\_VEND\_LINK**

because the CVI\*LINK tables does not contain the PARTNER number but the PARTNER\_GUID only

Basic Entity Relationship Diagram
---------------------------------

see also
