---
title: Extended Forecast Functions
page_id: 274578208
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274578208
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Extended Forecast Functions |
| Name (de) | Erweiterte Prognose Funktionen |
| ET Business Object | RET\_DISPO  ~~Z\_RET\_DISPO~~ |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| MAHD | Alternative Required Time Series for the Forecast | Alternative Bedarfszeitreihen für die Prognose |  |
| MAHD\_OVAL | Original Values for Alternative RPM Time Series for Forecast | Originalwerte alternative Bedarfszeitreihen für Prognose |  |
| MDRD | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Belieferungsbeziehungen: Abnehmer pro Werk und Material |  |
| MDRD\_EXTWG | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Belieferungsbeziehungen: Abnehmer pro Werk und Material |  |
| WAHD | Alternative Required Time Series for the Forecast | Alternative Bedarfszeitreihen für die Prognose |  |
| WAHD\_OVAL | Original Values for Alternative RPM Time Series for Forecast | Originalwerte alternative Bedarfszeitreihen für Prognose |  |
| WDRD | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Belieferungsbeziehungen: Abnehmer pro Werk und Material |  |
| WDRD\_EXTWG | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Belieferungsbeziehungen: Abnehmer pro Werk und Material |  |

**ET Business Object:** RET\_DISPO  ~~Z\_RET\_DISPO~~
**Name (en):** Extended Forecast Functions
**General Description**
=======================

These tables belong to the **Replenishment and Forecasting framework** in **SAP Retail**, particularly for **Requirements Planning (RPM)** and **Supply Relationships** between **distribution centers (DCs)** and **stores (sites)**.  
They control **forecast demand series** and **delivery relationships** that drive automated replenishment and stock transfer proposals.

---

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Explanation / Functional Role** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **MAHD** | Alternative Required Time Series for the Forecast | Holds **alternative demand (requirement) time series** for forecasting in **Material Requirements Planning (MRP/RPM)**. Used to simulate or substitute the standard demand profile with an alternative one (e.g., seasonal, promotional). |
| **MAHD\_OVAL** | Original Values for Alternative RPM Time Series for Forecast | Stores the **original unadjusted values** of the alternative demand time series maintained in `MAHD`. Enables auditability and comparison between adjusted and original forecast data. |
| **WAHD** | Alternative Required Time Series for the Forecast | Functionally identical to `MAHD`, but in the **Retail layer (W-tables)** — used for **Retail-specific replenishment runs**. Contains forecasted quantities per article/site/time bucket (often daily or weekly). |
| **WAHD\_OVAL** | Original Values for Alternative RPM Time Series for Forecast | Retail equivalent of `MAHD_OVAL`, storing **raw forecast values** before Retail-specific adjustments (e.g., store closing days, promotions). |
| **MDRD** | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Defines **which sites (recipients)** are supplied by which **distribution center (supplying plant)** for a given material. Core table for **site-to-site replenishment logic**. Used by replenishment and allocation processes. |
| **MDRD\_EXTWG** | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material (Extended Article Group) | Extension of `MDRD` that manages **delivery relationships at material group level** (e.g., all beverages from DC1000 to stores in region North). Reduces maintenance effort for large assortments. |
| **WDRD** | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material | Retail-layer equivalent of `MDRD`. Contains **the active delivery network** used in SAP Retail Replenishment — defines which stores receive goods from which DC for each material. |
| **WDRD\_EXTWG** | Delivery Relationships: Recipients per Plant and Material (Extended Article Group) | Retail-specific extension table to manage **delivery relationships per material group** (e.g., category-level sourcing from DCs). Used in Replenishment and Allocation Management. |

---

### 🔶 **Functional Summary**

* **MAHD/WAHD** tables → define **demand forecasts** and **time series** used to plan replenishment.
* **MDRD/WDRD** tables → define **supply relationships** between DCs and stores, driving where replenishment proposals are sourced from.
* The “\_EXTWG” tables extend these relationships to **material group level**, simplifying maintenance for large assortments.

---

### 🔶 **In Process Flow**

1. **Forecasting (MAHD / WAHD):**  
   Demand time series calculated (based on historical sales, promotions, seasonality).
2. **Replenishment Planning:**  
   Uses forecast data to determine store-level requirements.
3. **Source Determination (MDRD / WDRD):**  
   Determines supplying DC or plant for each requirement.
4. **Execution:**  
   Generates stock transfer or purchase proposals.

---

### 🔶 **S/4HANA Transformation Context**

* Functionality continues under **S/4HANA Retail & Fashion** with integration into:

  + **SAP F&R (Forecasting & Replenishment)** or **SAP CAR Replenishment Planning (CAR-RP)**.
  + Tables are often **replicated into CAR or embedded in CDS Views** for simulation and analytics.
* **Key replacements/enhancements:**

  + `I_ForecastTimeSeries`, `I_ReplenishmentRelationship` CDS Views.
  + Fiori apps: *Manage Replenishment Relationships*, *View Forecast Profiles*.
* **Best practice:** treat MAHD/WAHD and MDRD/WDRD as **transition-layer tables** — don’t extend directly; instead use APIs or integration into CAR.

================================================================================

| COOI | Commitments Management: Line Items | Obligoverwaltung: Einzelposten |  |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COMMIT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COMMIT)
**Name (en):** TD CO Commitments
General Description
===================

Commitments are liabilities that arise due to contractual obligations.

Commitments are only passed to other budget control applications for purchase requisitions and purchase orders that have been assigned to an account.

Commitment management is used for early detection and analysis of commitments in terms of their cost and financial impact.

Complex for reassigning the individual fields, since the dependencies cannot be uniquely assigned!

================================================================================

| Name (de) | CS Wartungspositionen ohne Pläne |

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| table | Description | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| ILOA | PM Object Location and Account Assignment | PM Object Location and Account Assignment |  |
| MPOS | Maintenance item | Maintenance item | X |
| OBJK | Plant Maintenance Object List | Plant Maintenance Object List |  |
| SER00 | General Header Table for Serial Number Management | General Header Table for Serial Number Management |  |
| COBRA | Settlement Rule for Order Settlement | Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung |  |
| COBRB | Distribution Rules Settlement Rule Order Settlement | Aufteilungsregeln Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung |  |

**ET Business Object:** CS\_MAITEM (till ET 2021 FP 2 CS60MAITEM)
**Name (en):** CS Maintanance Item w/o plans
**General Description**
=======================

**Maintenance item(s)**

A maintenance item describes which preventive maintenance tasks should take place regularly at a technical object or a group of technical objects.

A maintenance plan automatically always contains a maintenance item. You can create additional maintenance items directly in the maintenance plan, or assign existing maintenance items which have not yet been assigned. Exceptions to this rule include maintenance plans for service procurement and maintenance plans with reference to an outline agreement. These plans have only one maintenance item.

================================================================================

**General Description**

Definition
----------

It allows user to define groups and process the grouped activities for e.g.: outbound deliveries or transfer requests collectively (create all transfer orders for the entire group in one step and print out all of the transfer orders for the group).

It can also be used for two-step picking for e.g.: to group together picking-relevant outbound deliveries or transfer requirements.

**ET Business Object:** WM\_WMGRUP
**Name (en):** WM Groups

**ET Business Object:** CO\_SET
**Name (en):** CO Sets
**General Description**
=======================

Sets are flexible structures for organizing hierarchies and quantities. Values can be grouped or value intervals can be specified in one or more records in a hierarchy.

This is not included in our standard migration content. Talk to your project manager for specific scope description in order to include or exclude sets from data migration.

\* mapping of values in table SETLEAF

\* Mapping Object determination is dependend on SETLEAF-SETCLASS.

\* Values are only mapped if both are same.

\* It is not possible to guess - if values

\* differentiate - what value may be the right one

\* necessary additional tasks

\* - maintain /CBSGMBH/C\_MDD; add SETHEADER-TABNAME/FIELDNAME to

\* corresponding MO

\* - maintain /CBSGMBH/C\_MA; add references to main BO’s for

\* busines object CO\_SET for all used MO’s.

================================================================================

title: Material stock
page_id: 113104956
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/113104956
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Stock |
| Name (de) | Bestand |
| ET Business Object | MM\_STOCK |
| SDT Business Object | MM\_STOCK\_STEP1\_SDT and  MM\_STOCK\_STEP2\_SDT  MM\_STOCK\_VALUATION\_SDT |
| S4 to S4 Business Object | MM\_STOCK\_VALUATION\_S4  MM\_STOCK\_S4 |
| NONSAP / BAPI / IDOC Business Object | STOCK\_POSTING  (LU NONSAP\_ERP) |
| IDOC Business Object | MM\_STOCK\_IDOC MBGMCR |
**General Description**
=======================

Material stock can have different characteristics. It can be valuated or non valuated. There can be special stocks like consignment, subcontracting  and various types of a company’s own special stocks.

In general there are three types of stock in Inventory Management.

Each of these stock types indicates whether the material concerned can currently be used. Withdrawls for consumption can only be posted from unrestricted use.

* Unrestricted-use stock
* Quality inspection stock
* Blocked stock

At the time of goods receipt, you decide the type of stock to which a given quantity is to be posted.
**Tables**
==========

**ET Business Object:** MM\_STOCK
**Name (en):** Stock

================================================================================

---
title: FSCM TRM Valuation
page_id: 307366641
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307366641
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Valuation |
| Name (de) | Bewertungen |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TLVT |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TLVT |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TLVT |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BCKTLVT\_VAL\_FLOW | Backup: Treasury: Valuation Flow | Sicherung: Treasury: Bewertungsbewegung |  |
| TLVT\_MANUAL\_VAL | Book Values for Manual Valuation of Positions | Buchwerte für manuelle Bewertung von Beständen |  |
| TLVT\_VAL\_FLOW | Treasury: Valuation Flow | Treasury: Bewertungsbewegung |  |
| TLVT\_VAL\_STEP | Treasury: Valuation Step | Treasury: Bewertungsschritt |  |
| TLVT\_VAL\_STEPLOG | Treasury: Valuation Step Additional Log | Treasury: Zusatzprotokoll für Bewertungsschritt |  |
| TLVT\_VAL\_TRANS | Treasury: Valuation Business Transactions | Treasury: Bewertungs-Geschäftsvorfälle | X |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TLVT
**Name (en):** Valuation
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP's Corporate Finance Management (CFM) module, valuation in the Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) area involves assigning values to positions, typically based on market prices, net present values, or other relevant factors. This valuation process is crucial for financial reporting, risk management, and overall financial analysis within the Treasury function. 

Key Aspects of Valuation in SAP CFM TRM:

* **Valuation Areas:**

  Positions are tracked and valued within specific valuation areas, which can be parallel or separate entities for different currencies or legal entities.
* **Valuation Currency:**

  Each valuation area has a designated valuation currency, which is the currency in which the positions are managed and reported.
* **Position Valuation:**

  Positions are broken down into components, allowing for independent valuation of different parts of a transaction, such as the security value, foreign currency valuation, or index valuation.
* **Key Date Valuation:**  
  Valuation is performed on a specific key date, which can be a regularly scheduled valuation date or a specific date for a particular transaction.
* **etc.**

Example:  
Imagine a company holds a bond in USD, but its valuation area is in EUR. The system would use the current exchange rate to convert the bond's USD value to EUR for valuation purposes. If the exchange rate has changed since the bond's purchase, a foreign currency valuation adjustment would be made, potentially resulting in a write-up or write-down.

The associated data is migrated accordingly with the help of the business object FSCM\_TRM\_TLVT.

================================================================================

---
title: FSCM TRM Risk factor
page_id: 307374269
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307374269
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM TRM Risk factor |
| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Riskofaktoren |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_*TRM\_*ATRF |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_*TRM\_ATRF* |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_*TRM\_ATRF* |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ATRF | Risk factor | Risikofaktor | X |
| ATRFBETA | Risk factor description beta factors | Risikofaktorbeschreibung Betafaktoren |  |
| ATRFKORR | Risk factor description correlations | Risikofaktorbeschreibung Korrelationen |  |
| ATRFT | Risk Factors - Definition of Risk Factor Name | Risikofaktoren - Definition Risikofaktorname |  |
| ATRFVOLA | Descriptions of Risk Factors for Volatility Names | Beschreibung der Risikofaktoren für Volatilitätsnamen |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_*TRM\_*ATRF
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Risk factor
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP TRM (Treasury and Risk Management), an abstract risk factor represents a general category of risk that can be assigned to financial transactions, rather than a specific, concrete risk. These abstract factors act as placeholders for more detailed risk assessments, allowing for flexibility and customization within the system. Here's a more detailed explanation: 

* **Categorization:**  
  Abstract risk factors allow for the grouping of similar financial risks. For example, instead of defining specific interest rate risks for each transaction, you might use a broader "interest rate risk" abstract factor.
* **Flexibility:**  
  This approach allows for adaptable risk management strategies. As market conditions or business needs change, you can adjust the specific risks associated with an abstract factor without having to modify individual transactions.
* **Centralized Management:**  
  Abstract risk factors can be managed centrally within the system, making it easier to maintain and update risk parameters across various financial instruments and transactions.

The associated master data is transferred using the business object FSCM\_TRM\_ATRF.

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

Derived business transactions supplement Operative Business Transactions. They are generated on the basis of the operative business transactions and are dependent on the valuation area. This means that the derived business transactions can be differ according to valuation area even if they stem from the same operative business transactions.

title: FSCM TRM Derivatives transactional data
page_id: 307370576
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307370576
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM TRM Derivatives |
| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Derivate |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TPMT |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TPMT |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TPMT |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TPMTVMFL | Position management variation margin flows at sec. account | Bestandsführung Variation Margin Flows am Depot |  |
| TPMT\_DEDOC\_HDR | Derivatives Document: Document Header | Derivate Beleg: Belegkopf | X |
| TPMT\_DEDOC\_LOTPO | Derivatives Document: Document Item Per Lot | Derivate-Beleg: Belegposition je Lot |  |
| TPMT\_DEDOC\_POS | Derivatives Document: Document Item | Derivate Beleg: Belegposition |  |
| TPMT\_DEPAL\_FLOWS | Listed Futures: Price Gain Records | börsennotierte Futures: Kursgewinnsätze |  |
| TPMT\_LOT\_HEADER | Single Position Master Data for TR Position Management | Einzelbestandstammdaten für TR Bestandsführung | X |
| TPMT\_POS\_IND\_DE | Allocation of Acct Assignment Reference to Position | Zuordnung der Kontierungsreferenz zu Bestand |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TPMT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Derivatives
**General Description**
=======================

SAP TRM (Treasury and Risk Management) supports the management of various derivative instruments for hedging and risk mitigation. It allows for the entry, processing, and valuation of different types of derivatives, including options, interest rate swaps, cross-currency swaps, futures, and more. SAP TRM integrates with trading platforms and exchanges, enabling seamless connectivity for trade execution and capture. When you create or modify a derivative transaction in TRM, the system may automatically trigger the derivation process based on predefined rules. The derivation process uses data from various sources, including the transaction itself, other transactions, market data, and user-defined rules to derive the values of specific characteristics. 

The following graphic shows how the various derivatives components relate to one another:

For example, the table TPMT\_DEDOC\_HDR stores the header information of a derivation document. It contains details like the transaction number, the derivation strategy used, the date and time of the derivation, and the user who performed the derivation.

The associated data is migrated with the business object FSCM\_TRM\_TPMT.

================================================================================

---
title: Allocation (material)
page_id: 274578158
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274578158
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Allocation Document  Allocation Rule (material) |
| Name (de) | Aufteilerbelege  Aufteilregel (material) |
| ET Business Object | RET\_AUFT  RET\_ALL\_RULE  ~~Z\_RET\_AUFT~~  ~~Z\_RET\_ALL\_RULE~~ |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| AUBF | Document Flow SAP Retail (Allocation Table, Promotions...) | Belegfluss R3/Retail (Aufteiler, Aktionen, ...) |  |
| AUBSI | Worklist for PO (Follow-on Document Generation) | Arbeitsvorrat für Bestellung (FBG) |  |
| AUFG | Allocation Table Sub-Item, Store Group | Aufteilerunterposition Filialgruppe |  |
| AUFI | Allocation Table, Document Sub-item, Stores | Aufteilerbelegunterposition Filialen |  |
| AUKAI | Worklist for Sales Orders (FBG) | Arbeitsvorrat für Kundenaufträge (FBG) |  |
| AUKO | Allocation Table Document Header | Aufteilerbelegkopf | X |
| AULW | Delivery Phases | Lieferwellen |  |
| AUPO | Allocation Table Document Item | Aufteilerbelegposition |  |
| AUSB | Plant Status Table | Tabelle Betriebsstatus |  |
| AUUMI | Worklist for Stock Transport Orders (Follow-on Doc. Gen.) | Arbeitsvorrat für Umlagerungsbestellungen (FBG) |  |
| AUVDI | Worklist for Deliveries (Follow-on Doc. Generation) | Arbeitsvorrat für Lieferung (FBG) |  |
| AUVT | Shipping-Specific Data of Plants for Delivery Generation | Versandspez. Daten der Werke für Lieferungsgenerierung |  |
| AUVW | Delivery Phase for Distribution Center | Lieferwelle Verteilzentrum |  |
| AUVZ | Distribution Center | Verteilzentrum |  |
| AUWEI | Index, Adjust Alloc Tbl After Entering Doc. | Index Aufteiler anpassen nach Erfassen Beleg |  |
| SVKO | Allocation Rule / IS-R Allocation | Aufteilregel / SAP-WWS Aufteilung | X |
| SVPOS | Allocation Rule Items / SAP IS-R Allocation | AufteilregelPositionen / SAP-WWS Aufteilung |  |
| SVUP | Allocation Rule Sub-Item (Stores) | Aufteilregel Unterposition (Filialen) |  |

**ET Business Object:** RET\_AUFT  RET\_ALL\_RULE  ~~Z\_RET\_AUFT~~  ~~Z\_RET\_ALL\_RULE~~
**Name (en):** Allocation Document  Allocation Rule (material)
**General Description**
=======================

Diese Tabellen hängen alle mit dem **Aufteiler- bzw. Allocation-Management** in SAP Retail zusammen. Das ist das zentrale Steuerungsobjekt für die **Verteilung von Warenmengen aus Zentraleinkäufen auf Filialen**, häufig im Kontext von **Promotionen, Initialbelieferungen, Umlagerungen oder Nachversorgungen**.  
Hier eine kompakte Einordnung, wie man sie im Transformation- oder Design-Kontext verwenden würde:

---

### 🧩 **Funktionaler Zusammenhang**

Der **Aufteiler (Allocation Table)** bündelt Bedarfe verschiedener Filialen zu einem zentralen Beleg, um daraus automatisiert **Folgebelege** (Bestellungen, Umlagerungsbestellungen, Lieferungen) zu erzeugen.  
Er bildet somit die Brücke zwischen **Merchandise Distribution**, **Einkauf**, **Logistik** und **Promotionmanagement**.

---

### 📘 **Tabellenübersicht & Bedeutung**

| Tabelle | Funktion im Retail-Kontext | Beschreibung / Nutzung im Prozess |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **AUKO** | Aufteiler-Belegkopf | Headerdaten des Allocation Tables (z. B. Promotion, Einkaufskampagne, Gültigkeit, Steuerungsparameter). |
| **AUPO** | Aufteiler-Position | Positionsdaten (Material, Menge, Allokationslogik, Bezug zur Promotion oder zum Order-Item). |
| **AUFG** | Aufteiler-Unterposition Filialgruppe | Gruppierung mehrerer Stores zu „Store Groups“ – ermöglicht Verteilung auf Gruppenebene. |
| **AUFI** | Aufteiler-Unterposition Filialen | Detailaufteilung auf einzelne Stores (Filialmenge, Liefertermin etc.). |
| **AUBF** | Belegfluss (Allocation Table / Promotions) | Verknüpfung der Aufteiler mit Folgebelegen (Bestellung, Lieferung, Wareneingang). |
| **AUWEI** | Index zur Anpassung | Technische Tabelle für Indexierung nach Änderungen im Aufteiler (z. B. Anpassung nach Belegerfassung). |
| **SVKO** | Aufteilregel (Header) | Steuerungsregel für die automatische Verteilung (z. B. prozentuale Zuteilung, Bedarfs- oder Umsatzbasis). |
| **SVPOS** | Aufteilregel-Position | Regeldefinition je Material / Materialgruppe. |
| **SVUP** | Aufteilregel-Unterposition (Filialen) | Regeldefinition auf Store-Level. |
| **AULW** | Lieferwellen | Steuerung von Lieferwellen im Rahmen der Distribution (zeitlich gestaffelte Lieferungen). |
| **AUVW** | Lieferwelle Verteilzentrum | Abbildung der Lieferwelle auf Ebene des Distribution Centers. |
| **AUVZ** | Verteilzentrum | Stammdaten der Verteilzentren im Retail-Kontext. |
| **AUVT** | Werk-/Versandspezifische Daten | Steuerung, wann und wie ein Werk Lieferungen generiert (FBG-Parameter). |
| **AUSB** | Betriebsstatus der Werke | Anzeige, ob ein Store/Werk im Aufteilprozess aktiv oder gesperrt ist. |
| **AUBSI** | Arbeitsvorrat für Bestellung (FBG) | Worklist für generierte Bestellungen aus dem Aufteiler. |
| **AUKAI** | Arbeitsvorrat für Kundenaufträge (FBG) | Worklist zur Generierung von Sales Orders. |
| **AUUMI** | Arbeitsvorrat für Umlagerungsbestellungen (FBG) | Worklist zur Generierung von STOs. |
| **AUVDI** | Arbeitsvorrat für Lieferung (FBG) | Worklist für Lieferungserzeugung nach Aufteilerfreigabe. |

---

### 🧭 **Einordnung im Prozessfluss**

1. **Planung/Promotion (MM/SD-Retail)** → Definition von Artikeln, Mengen, Filialen.
2. **Aufteilererstellung (AUKO/AUPO/AUFI)** → zentrale Verteilentscheidung.
3. **Regelanwendung (SVKO/SVPOS)** → automatische Mengenverteilung.
4. **Folgebeleggenerierung (AUBSI, AUKAI, AUUMI, AUVDI)** → Erstellung von Bestellungen, STOs, Lieferungen.
5. **Logistikabwicklung (AULW, AUVW)** → physische Belieferung und Monitoring.
6. **Belegfluss (AUBF)** → Nachverfolgung über gesamte Kette.

---

### 🧩 **Transformation Management Relevanz**

* Beim **S/4HANA Retail oder Fashion Migration** ist die Prüfung dieser Tabellen wichtig, weil:

  + Datenmodell teils in **Business Partner und MATDOC** überführt wird.
  + Einige Tabellen durch **Fiori Apps** (z. B. „Manage Allocation Tables“) ersetzt werden.
  + Custom Extensions häufig auf AUKO/AUPO/AUFI zugreifen.
  + Für Clean Core-Strategie oft **neue API-basierte Aufteilerlogik** in Betracht gezogen wird.

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

Persistent data for payment requests is transferred via the TRPRT\_PAYMENTS table. The corresponding log entries can be found in the TRPT\_LOG table.

The TRPRT\_PAYMENTS table is also filled by executing transaction TBB1. Transaction TBB1 executes the payment and the posting at the same time (generation of documents for financial accounting in treasury).

| S/4 Business Object | S4 handling |
**Tables**
==========

| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| RKPF | Belegkopf Reservierung | Document Header: Reservation | X |
| FTIND | Fehlteilindex | Missing parts index |  |
| MISTATUS | Statustabelle zur Materialident. auf Mat./Charg.ebene | Status Table for Material Identification on Mat./Batch Level |  |
| RESB | Reservierung/Sekundärbedarf | Reservation/dependent requirements |  |
| REUL | Index für Umlagerungsreservierungen zum Material | Material stock transfer reservation index |  |
| RSPSP | Index Reservierungen zu PSP-Elementen / Kundenauftragspos. | Index: Reservations for WBS elements/Sales order items |  |
| TCNTM05 | Zuordnung Komponente zu Termingruppe | Assignment Components to Groups |  |
| TCNTM06 | Termin zu Komponente, Terminart und Termintyp | Dates for Components, Events and Date Type |  |
| VSHIST\_OK | Übernahmehistorie Materialkomponenten | Transfer history material components |  |
| RSADD | Zusatzfelder zur Reservierung | Additional fields for reservation | X |
| RSDB | Index von der RESB zum Direktbeschaffungselement | Index of the RESB for the Direct Procurement Element | X |
| RSDBS | Index auf Einkaufsbelege zum Einzelbestand | Index of purchasing documents for individual customer stock | X |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_RESERV  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60RESERV)  PP\_RESERV\_2  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60RESERV\_2)
**Name (en):** Reservations
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate reservations.  

Table RESB contains reservations items (requests to the warehouse to reserve a material to be delivered at a later point in time) and dependent requirements (material or product requirements that are generated by superior assemblies and can be planned) used in Logistics. The corresponding header table is RKPF, which contains general information on reservations (creator, transaction type, account assignment). 

The structure is comprised of a Header table, plus several child tables linked underneath by their respective Table Key Relationships (TKR).  

This BO acts as a super BO to several other BO’s.

================================================================================

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| /ECRS/RPHD | Intrastat Declaration Header Data | Intrastat-Meldungskopfdaten | / |

| /ECRS/POIA | Provider of Information | Auskunftspflichtiger | / |
| /ECRS/POIC | Provider of Information (Plants) | Auskunftspflichtiger (WERK) | /ECRS/POIA |

**ET Business Object:** table VEIAV in [SD\_DELIVE](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/SD+Delivery)
**Name (en):** Intrastat declaration S4
**General Description**
=======================

In S/4 the old ECC table VEIAV has been replaced by new tables /ECRS/RP\* for the intrastat declaration.

**Since this is a new BO, please bring back findings from the projects to Confluence.**

================================================================================

space: unknown

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Pre-Allocated Stock |
| Name (de) | Fehlteile |

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| T310 | Pre-Allocated Stock | Fehlteile |  |

**ET Business Object:** WM\_FEHL  (till ET 2021 FP 2 WM60FEHL)
**Name (en):** Pre-Allocated Stock
**General Description**
=======================

Pre-allocation is an action of blocking inventory or pre-ordering an item for a future predicted usage. Preallocated stocks are materials that you urgently need for goods issue, but which are not available in the warehouse, for example materials required urgently by production, materials required by the customer, or quantities in backlog.

**Name (en):** Permits
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the permits. Permits are used to adhere to health and safety regulations and to control order processing; they are assigned to technical objects. Only the permits themselves are migrated through this BO, not the assignments of those permits to technical objects.

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CKBS | Base Planning Object - Unit Costing | Musterkalkulation - Einzelkalkulation |  |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_UNITCO
**Name (en):** Base Planning - Unit Costing
**General Description**
=======================

All base planning objects are controlled by a costing variant.

They can be used at the beginning of the planning phase, but also in the design phase, or by the planning of new products.

With base planning, existing material cost estimates can also be changed.

title: ERS Procedure
page_id: 118230091
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/118230091
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | ERS Procedure: Goods (Merchandise) Movements to be Invoiced |
| Name (de) | ERS-Verfahren: Zu berechnende Warenbewegungen |
| ET Business Object | MM\_ERSINV  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60ERSINV) |
| S/4 Business Object |  |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| EKRS | ERS Procedure: Goods (Merchandise) Movements to be Invoiced | ERS-Verfahren: Zu berechnende Warenbewegungen |  |
| EKRSDC | ERS Procedure: Planned Delivery Costs to be Invoiced | ERS-Verfahren: Zu berechnende geplante Nebenkosten |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_ERSINV  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60ERSINV)
**Name (en):** ERS Procedure: Goods (Merchandise) Movements to be Invoiced
**General Description**
=======================

Procedure for settling goods receipts automatically. When you use Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS), you agree with the vendor that the latter will not submit an invoice in respect of a purchase order transaction. Instead, the system posts the invoice document automatically on the basis of the data in the purchase order and goods receipts. This eliminates invoice variances.

================================================================================

---
title: Number get next -> External numbers example
page_id: 285999509
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/285999509
---
Overview
========

When there is a need to renumber documents by using the "Number Get Next", sometimes there are external numbers which need to be handled accordingly.

This article describes how the mapping was set in a project (Avnet - 2024). Naturally, the approach was agreed with the customer.

The scope of the project went for renumbering FI documents given there was a merge into an existing Company code, but for external numbers, the original number would be kept, but assigned to a newly created Number range Object (93)

Source Nriv:

Target Nriv:

================================================================================

---
title: 5. Insurance values processing for Assets with iDocs
page_id: 140784962
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/140784962
---
Overview
========

Insurance value can be mapped in two ways:

* Option 1: Defining specific information for insuring the complex fixed asset in the asset master record
* Option 2: Managing insurance values in a dedicated depreciation area

More details in this note: <https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/0002398938>

The (option 2) mapping of insurance values to a dedicated depreciation area will be explained in this article. Having this premise, the insurance values are will be migrated to a dedicated depreciation area. Technically speaking, the implementation of insurance values in S4 might vary if:

* **The customer was using already Insurance values**
* **The customer was not using Insurances values**

Both cases will be explained for the Mid of Year approach.

================================================================================

Manuel Müller ,  Tim Beigel, Rudi Michel

* Changes in Migration

  + Carve In  vs. Transformation
  + List of BO's
  + Object specific changes
  + Changes in the sequence of processing
  + Processing Logic in the OIE

* Characteristics
  + Viessmann / Zürich / BSH
  + Checks / Customizing Consistency (before Import)
  + Post Processing
  + Validation - Business - Technical

### S4LT Block 7 - Special Topics - Project Types - Project Execution - Wrap Up

**Presentation 08.02.2019**

**Recording 08.02.2019**

S4LT KT Training Block 7-- Special. . . - Freitag, 8. Februar 2019 16.17.09.mp4

Duration approx: 2,5h

Tim Beigel, Rudi Michel

---

Special Topics

* Business Partner
* Accrual Engine
* COPA
* Credit Management

Project Types

* Playground / Sandpit
* Carve In
* Transformation

Project Types

* Simplification Items
* MDV - Prechecks - PRE MIGRATION/CONVERSION VALIDATION
* Migration - Prechecks - CORRECTION / POST PROCESSING REPORTS AFTER MIGRATION/CONVERSION
* Validation - POST MIGRATION/CONVERSION VALIDATION

Wrap Up

==========

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Header table** |
| ESTRH | Specification Header |  |
| ESTRI | Identifier (Numbers, Names, Chemical Formulas) | ESTRH |
| ESTRL | Identifier - Regulatory List Assignment | ESTRI |
| ESTRR | Reference | ESTRH |
| ESTMJ | Specification-Material Assignment | ESTRH |
| ESTVH | Specification - Value Assignment Type Assignment | ESTRH |
| ESTVA | Value Assignment | ESTVH |
| ESTDF | Additional Information - User-Defined Text | ESTVA |
| ESTDR | Additional Information: Value Assignment Assessment | ESTVA |
| ESTDU | Additional Information: Value Assignment Usage | ESTVA |
| ESTVP | Value Assignment Item | ESTVA |
| ESTDS | Additional Information: Value Assignment Source | ESTVA |
| EST07 | Dangerous Goods Management - Transport Approval | ESTRH |
| EST0B | Dangerous Goods Management - Packaging Code Approval | ESTRH |
| EST0D | Dangerous Goods Management - Risk Classification | ESTRH |
| EST0F | Dangerous Goods Management - Transport Classification | ESTRH |
| CCUNTCARR | Provisions for Carriage | ESTRH |
| CCUNTPACK | Packing Requirements | ESTRH |
| CCUNTSPROV | Special Packing Provisions | ESTRH |

**ET Business Object:** EHS\_SPEC
**General Description**
=======================

A Specification is a description that uses identifiers and value assignments such as physical and chemical properties, medical advice, and transport-relevant information. Is the central data object for mapping substances, agents, packagings, waste codes, and dangerous goods classifications in the SAP component Environment, Health and Safety (EH&S).

It is also a key element for the **Product Lifecycle Management**that uses this component to describe the elements and specifications for the new recipe and / or formulas being develop and passed to production.

| S/4 Business Object | SD\_INVOIC\_SDT  (till ET 2021 FP2 SDS4INVOIC) |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| **VBRK** | **Billing Document: Header Data** | **Faktura: Kopfdaten** | **X** |
| VBUK | Sales Document: Header Status and Administrative Data | Vertriebsbeleg: Kopfstatus und Verwaltungsdaten |  |
| VBPA | Sales Document: Partner | Vertriebsbeleg: Partner |  |
| VBRP | Billing Document: Item Data | Faktura: Positionsdaten |  |
| KONV | Conditions (Transaction Data) | Konditionen (Vorgangsdaten) |  |
| TVFKD | Billing Document: Billing Date by Number Range | Faktura: Fakturadatum pro Nummernkreis |  |
| VBRL | Sales Document: Invoice List | Vertriebsbeleg: Rechnungsliste |  |
| VBSS | Collective Processing: Sales Documents | Vertriebsbelege eines Sammelgangs |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_INVOIC  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60INVOIC)
**Name (en):** SD Invoice/billing document
**General Description**
=======================

During billing processing, you can create, change, and cancel billing documents (invoices, credit memos, debit memos, and so on). You can also post them to financial accounting and output them to a variety of channels (for example, print and email).

You can also create and manage preliminary billing documents as draft predecessors of the final billing documents that are sent to customers for settlement.

You can create billing documents:

* With reference to one or more sales orders
* With reference to one or more delivery documents
* With reference to transactions outside of Sales (including reference to billing document requests that can contain billing data from external sources or other lines of business such as Service)
* With reference to preliminary billing documents

================================================================================

---
title: 7. Transaction types (BWASL) treatment
page_id: 153357877
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/153357877
---
Overview
========

During a mid-of-year approach whithin a legacy values transfer of assets. We have to migrate current year transactions (acquisitions, transfers, reversals, manual depreciations, etc.), those are attached to transaction types. In this article we will list known issues when posting transaction types and how to proceed. Please, these mappings have to be agreed with the customer and tested, we propose them as solution. Every customer has its own particularities and for this reason better to be in a safe place.

================================================================================

| VBREVR | Revenue Recognition: Reference Document Lines | Erlösrealisierung: Refferenzbelegzeilen |  |

| VSVBEP\_CN | Version: Sales document: Schedule line data | Version: Verkaufsbeleg: Einteilungsdaten |  |
| VSVBKD\_CN | Version: Sales document - commercial data | Version: Verkaufsbeleg Kaufmännische Daten |  |

**General Description**

The sales order is a contractual agreement between a sales organization and a sold-to party about delivering products or providing a service for defined prices, quantities and times.

In sales order processing, the system implements various functions:

In the sales order, functions such as pricing and printouts are available. The system checks whether the material is available for the requested delivery date and if necessary, transfers the requirements to materials planning. The system generates shipping dates and points in delivery scheduling.

Another part of this component are the special sales order types - rush orders and cash sales.

* In cash sales the goods are paid for in cash.
* In a rush order transaction, the customer picks up the goods or you deliver the goods on the same day as the order is placed. However, the invoice is created later.

**ET Business Object:** SD\_ORDERS
**Name (en):** SD sales order  SD contracts  SD quotations  SD inquiry

================================================================================

title: Sales configuration engine
page_id: 120097153
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120097153
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Sales configuratin engine (SCE) |
| Name (de) | Sales configuratin engine (SCE) |
| ET Business Object | VC\_SCE  (till ET 2021 FP 2 VC60SCE) |
| S/4 Business Object |  |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**General Description**
=======================

The Sales Configuration Engine (SCE) is a core Business Solutions component in the way that it is implemented to provide support for sales processes with interactive product configuration, for example. The component is in particular suitable for applications that focus on interaction in the configuration process.

It has open architecture, is fully integrated into SAP ERP and uses the existing variant configuration models.

| LL01 | Whse Activity Monitor: Critical Transfer Order Items | Lagerleitstand: Kritische Transportauftragspositionen |  |
| LL02 | Whse Activity Monitor: Critical Transfer Requirement Items | Lagerleitstand: Kritische Transportbedarfspositionen |  |
| LL03 | Whse Activity Monitor: Critical Posting Change Documents | Lagerleitstand: Kritische Umbuchungsbelege |  |
| LL04 | Whse Activity Monitor: Critical Deliveries | Lagerleitstand: Kritische Lieferungen |  |

**ET Business Object:** WM\_LVSLL  (till ET 2021 FP 2 WM60LVSLL)
**Name (en):** Warehouse activity monitor
**General Description**
=======================

The warehouse activity monitor provides a means to notify responsible personnel in case there are delays or errors in the overall system. Helps you to identify and correct warehousing errors or critical processes soon after they occur. Each of the tables in this BO referes to a specific criteria of movement types being monitored.

================================================================================

---
title: Combined FICO
page_id: 254953557
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/254953557
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Combined FICO |
| Name (de) | FICO Kombiniert |
| ET Business Object |  |
| S/4 Business Object | AC\_FICO\_COMBINED\_SDT |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| /CBSGMBH/A\_AWKEY | Object key | Objektschlüssel | X |
| /CBSGMBH/A\_COSEL | preselected data for CO | vorselektierte Daten für CO |  |
| COBK | Accounting Document Header | Belegkopf für Buchhaltung |  |
| COEP | Accounting Document Segment | Belegsegment Buchhaltung |  |
| BKPF | Entry View of Accounting Document for Additional Ledgers | Erfassungssicht Buchhaltungsbeleg für zusätzliche Ledger |  |
| BSEG | CO Object: Document Header | CO-Objekt: Belegkopf |  |

**Name (en):** Combined FICO
**General Description**
=======================

This BO combines the two BOs AC\_FI\_DOC\_SDT and AC\_CO\_DOC\_SDT.  Starting with ET 2023 FP2, report [/CBSGMBH/DMF\_PRESEL\_4\_CO](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=246495559) only selects the value types 04 and 11 from the COEP depending on time and company code as well as the FI documents depending on time and company code. The FI documents and the CO documents in the ACDOCA are linked in one go.

Based on the old ledger accounting in the source, this BO is used with a different OIE. If the new general ledger accounting is already active in the source, please use this BO 

Warning

Report [/CBSGMBH/DMF\_PRESEL\_4\_CO](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=246495559) is used to fill tables /CBSGMBH/A\_AWKEY and /CBSGMBH/A\_COSEL. Apart from the selection criteria of this report, filters on controlling area (KOKRS), company code (BUKRS) or fiscal year (GJAHR) should **not** be used except for doing logical splits. Otherwise, cross company documents might be lost or processed incorrectly.

For the same reason, you should not change the table key relations for /CBSGMBH/A\_AWKEY and /CBSGMBH/A\_COSEL. I.e. do **not** use company code and fiscal year to connect /CBSGMBH/A\_AWKEY and /CBSGMBH/A\_COSEL to the tables below.

In general, the fields in these tables should not be used for any "semantical" processing, like using them in filters or mappings. This is especially important for KOKRS, BUKRS and GJAHR.

================================================================================

**Name (en):** Transfer Requirement
**General Description**
=======================

Transfer requirements form the basis for creation of transfer orders that are used to carry out physical goods movements. You can create transfer requirements automatically by posting a goods movement in Inventory Management or by requesting materials using the Production Planning system. The tables in this BO contain all the properties of all Transfer Requirements existing in the Warehouse.

**General Description**
=======================

An installation groups together the devices, registers, and, if applicable, reference values that belong to the same division and are allocated to the same premise, and that are therefore billed together, such as an on-peak and an off-peak rate meter.

An installation is allocated to one contract only.

---
title: Sites
page_id: 246485727
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/246485727
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) |  |
| Name (de) |  |
| ET Business Object |  |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
|  |  |  |  |
**General Description**
=======================

A site is an organizational unit used to document goods stored in distribution centers and stores for the purposes of inventory management and to model the associated business processes, such as goods receipt, physical inventory, and goods issue.

A site is alreay assigned to a customer (T001W-KUNNR) and optionally also to a vendor (T001W-LIFNR).

The the site category (T001W-VLFKZ) defines wether it is a distribution center or a store.

The T001W entry will not be created in the customizing. Instead, it will be created automatically in the background if a site will be created in the application.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_FTR
**Name (en):** FSCM FTR Position and rates
**General Description**
=======================

This business object is used to transfer data on commodities, among other things. With SAP Commodity Risk Management, you can manage financial transactions such as commodity futures, commodity forwards, commodity swaps, tradable options and OTC options and process the associated master data and market data.

Furthermore, credit line definitions are also transferred within this business object.

================================================================================

| ~~LCABNT~~ | ~~Characteristic descriptions~~ | ~~Texte für Merkmale~~ | moved to CA\_SPECIAL\_CLASS |
| CLSELSTATISTICS | Statistics for Optimizing Object Search | Statistik zur Optimierung der Objektsuche |  |
| CLSELSTATISTICS2 | Statistical AUSP Information for Characteristics | Statistische AUSP Informationen auf Merkmalebene |  |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_CHARACTER  ~~CA\_MERKMAL ( till ET 2025 FP0)~~  CA\_ATINN\_MAPPING
**Name (en):** Characteristics
**General Description**
=======================

In the classification system, characteristics are used to describe the properties of objects (e.g. materials, batches, documents). The individual characteristics of a feature are represented by characteristic values. Example: The characteristic 'color' has the values 'red', 'green' and 'blue'. The color of an object is specified by its characteristic value during classification. 

Characteristics are created centrally, they are then assigned to classes. Characteristics can be adapted for specific classes. When objects are assigned to a class, values are assigned to the characteristics.  

This BO is to migrate characteristics (table CABN) and their values (CAWN) as well as related statistical data.  

The tables in the template BO stand alone, they are not linked to each other via TKR. All of them have ATINN (Internal characteristic number) as key field. You can also implement a TKR as in the example below:

================================================================================

space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120116388
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (EN) | Quality Levels |
| Name (DE) | Qualitätslagen |
| ET Business Object | QM\_QULE  (till ET 2021 FP 2 QM60QULE) |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Bezeichnung (DE)** | **Main Table** |
| QDQL | Quality level | Qualitätslage | X |

**ET Business Object:** QM\_QULE  (till ET 2021 FP 2 QM60QULE)
**Name (en):** Quality Levels
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the quality levels. 

The information in the quality level determines which inspection stage will be used for the sample determination of the next inspection lot. The system automatically updates the quality level at the time of dynamic modification (at lot creation or when the usage decision is made), by checking the conditions for an inspection stage change and, if necessary, storing a new inspection stage for the next inspection.

================================================================================

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| IMAK | Appropriation requests - general data | Investitionsanforderung - Allgemeine Daten |  |
| IMAKA | Approp. request for assets or equipment to be replaced | Maßnahmensanforderung zu ersetzende Anlagen / Equis | IMAK |
| IMAKPA | Approp.req.-cost ctr, bus.area, prof.ctr, w/ perc.distrib. | Maßn.anford. anf. Kostenst./Gsber./Profitctr. mit Proz.auft. | IMAK |
| IMAKPI | Approp. Request- Percentage Dist. by Invest.Reason | Invest.anforderung Aufteilung Investitionsgrund | IMAK |
| IMAKPP | Approp. request - distribution by requesting cost ctr | Invest.anforderung Aufteilung anfordernde Kostenstelle | IMAK |
| IMAKPS | Approp. request - distribution by requesting division | Invest.anforderung Aufteilung anfordernde Sparte | IMAK |
| IMAKPU | Approp. request - environmental inv. reason w/ perc.distrib. | Maßnahmenanforderung Umweltschutzgrund mit Prozentauft. | IMAK |
| IMAKPW | Approp. request - distribution by requesting material group | Invest.anforderung Aufteilung anfordernde Warengruppe | IMAK |
| IMAKT | Appropriation request text | Maßnahmenanforderung Texte | IMAK |
| IMAKZ | Assignment of measure to order or WBS element ... | Zuordnung der Realisierungsobjekte Auftrag/PSP ... | IMAK |
| IMAKZS | Approp. request assignment table- alternate requests... | Maßnahmenanforderung Zuordnungstabelle Alternativen ... | IMAK |
| IMAV | Appropriation request variant | Variante Maßnahmenanforderung | IMAK |
| IMAVC | Calculations Copied from Variants to WBS Elements | Kalkulationen, die von Varianten auf PSP übernommen wurden | IMAV |
| IMAVT | Texts for appropriation request variant | Maßnahmenanforderungsvariante Texte | IMAV |
| IMAVZ | Assignment of approp. request variants to plan versions | Maßnahmenanforderung Variantenzuordnung zu Planversionen | IMAV |
| IHPA | Plant Maintenance: Partners | Instandhaltung: Partner | IMAK |
| ANIA | Depr. simulation for invest. projects | Investitionsprojekte AfA-Simulation | IMAK |
| ANIB | Investment projects depr. simulation depr. areas | Investitionsprojekte AfA-Simulation Bewertungsbereiche | IMAK |

**ET Business Object:** IM\_APPR
**Name (en):** Appropriation request
**General Description**
=======================

The Appropriation Requests component supports the investment process in the phase concerned with planning and making decisions about investments to be implemented.

In a certain sense, appropriation requests serve a similar function as purchase requisitions and maintenance notifications. In the investment process, appropriation requests allow you to represent investment wishes or research and development ideas in the stages before they are actually implemented concretely.

You can plan appropriation requests and carry out preinvestment analysis for them. In the context of capital investment planning, appropriation requests make it possible to include planned investments in cyclical (annual) investment planning using an investment program.

Once the appropriation request has been approved, the information can be transferred to the measure (order or project) that will be used for actually carrying it out.

================================================================================

=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the sampling procedures. 

Sampling procedures contain the rules for determining the sample size for an inspection. The sampling procedure also specifies the type of valuation to use for results recording. A sampling procedure is assigned to each characteristic in an inspection plan to calculate the sample size.

---
title: Sets / Standard Hierarchies (mapping, conversion, re-structuring, migration)
page_id: 19693754
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/19693754
---
Overview
========

Sets are used to group different objects (Cost Center, Profit Center, WBS, Orders, ...) in a hierarchical tree structure. Independent of the object type the identical tables are used to store the sets on the database. A Standard Hierarchy (e.g. for Profit Center) is a special kind of Set where no ranges in leafs are allowed, but besides that there is no difference to other Sets. In addition, the name of the Set entry of such a Standard Hierarchy is used as a reference in the object master data (e.g. field KHINR in table CEPC or CSKS) and in some customizing settings. Each existent master data record in CO must be part of a Standard Hierarchy.

The Sets consists of four relevant tables:

1. SETHEADER - table for all set datasets in the system, no matter on which level of a set they exist
2. SETHEADERT - texts in all languages for each SETHEADER dataset
3. SETNODE - link between a set and all sub-sets existing one level down underneath the respective set
4. SETLEAF - holds the object values (leafs) underneath a respective set.

Always try to avoid the migration of Sets. Due to the structure of the Sets the mapping of the different fields in the Set tables can cause troubles, especially when leave objects need to get mapped in combination of the use of ranges for these set leafs.

**Example:**

Cost Center Set with leaf range 'ABC to ZZZ' in combination with mapping 'ABC → 900', 'ZZZ → 100' and 'ABD → 005'.

Result: Cost Center Set with leaf range '900 to 100' → invalid range and loss of an included Cost Center after mapping!

MIG business object of the Set tables: CO60SET

================================================================================

=======================

This BO contains the tables required to migrate the Capacity Standard Times calculation Methods. The structure of the BO is made up of a Header table, and some child tables underneath linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR). The BO has not direct relationship to other BO’s. 

Calculating the capacity requirements of an operation for manufacturing a material takes place in scheduling using formulas from the work center. The formulas which you can maintain in Customizing contain formula parameters.

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **BO** | **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| CO\_MLHD | MLHD | Material Ledger Document: Header | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Kopf |  |
| CO\_MLHD | MLMST | Material Ledger Document: Costing Run Header Data | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Kalkulationslauf Kopfdaten | MLHD |
| CO\_MLIT | MLIT | Material Ledger Document: Items | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Positionen |  |
| CO\_MLIT | MLPP | Material Ledger Document: Posting Periods and Quantities | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Buchungsperioden und Mengen | MLIT |
| CO\_MLIT | MLPPF | Material Ledger Document: Field Groups (Posting Periods) | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Feldgruppen (Buchungsperioden) | MLIT |
| CO\_MLIT | MLCR | Material Ledger Document: Currencies and Values | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Währungen  und Werte | MLIT |
| CO\_MLIT | MLCRF | Material Ledger Document: Field Groups (Currencies) | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Feldgruppen (Währungen) | MLIT |
| CO\_MLIT | MLCRP | Material Ledger Document: Price Changes (Currencies, Prices) | Material-Ledger-Beleg: Preisänderung (Währungen,  Preise) | MLIT |
| CO\_MLIT | MLKEPH | ML Document: Cost Component Split (Elements) for Values | ML-Beleg: Schichtung (Elemente) für Werte | MLIT |

| CO\_MLIT | MLPRKEPH | ML Document: Cost Component Split (Components)for Prices |  | MLIT |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_MLHD and CO\_MLIT, former CO60ML\_DOC  ~~CO60ML\_DOC~~
**Name (en):** Material Ledger Documents
**General Description**
=======================

Material ledger data is valuation and control data related to a material (possibly with a specific valuation type) in a plant for a specific posting period. In addition, the material ledger data contains the data on movements that are relevant to the valuation of the material: this data is collected during entry in the material ledger. Material ledger data form the basis for actual costing and for keeping valuation prices in multiple currencies and valuations. The material ledger data thus complements the material master records. In addition, the material ledger data can refer to materials on individual customer and project stocks.

These are the documents for the ML postings

================================================================================

| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| WBRK | Settlement Management Document Header | Abrechnungsmanagement-Belegkopf | X |
| WBRP | Settlement Management Document Item | Abrechnungsmanagement-Belegposition |  |
| WBPA | Business Partner Assignment | Geschäftspartnerzuordnung |  |
| WBRL | Agency business: Item in document list | Agenturgeschäft: Position einer Belegliste |  |
| WBRRE | AB-RRLE: Extended Remuneration List Data for External Doc. | AB-RRLE: Erweiterte Vergütungslistendaten zum Fremdbeleg |  |
| WBRF | Document flow vendor billing document/settlement request | Belegfluß Lieferantenfaktura/Regulierungsanforderung |  |
| WBRFN | Document Flow of Reference Document to Agency Document | Belegfluss Referenzbeleg zum  Agenturbeleg |  |
| WBRD | Agency Business: Bank Data Suspense Account | Agenturgeschäft: Bankdaten CPD |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_AGCYBUS  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60AGCYBUS)
**Name (en):** Agency Business
**General Description**
=======================

Use
---

You can use this component to process transactions between third parties (such as brokers and payment agencies).You can offer the parties involved advantages or services (such as del credere guaranties or simplified payment transactions), and perform settlement accounting for commissions in the system.

In pooled payment, the agency negotiates, for example, purchase price conditions and payment processing between the vendor and customer.The agency takes a commission from the customer and/or the vendor for services rendered.Transactions relating to the actual merchandise (for example, purchase orders and deliveries) can be carried out completely independently of the agency.For payment processing and calculating commission, the agency enters incoming invoices and remuneration settlements in agency documents.

**Agency documents have the following common features:**

* There may be no reference in the system to previous transactions
* Payments have to be settled on particular dates
* There is direct contact between the customer and the vendor.

With the Agency Business component, you can enter documents without referring to previous transactions. This means that you can perform settlement accounting for payments and commission, even if the system does not contain any master data or movement data for the articles concerned.

**Incoming Invoices:**

You can enter invoices from vendors and credit memos either as single settlement requests or in the form of a settlement request list.

**Remuneration Settlement**

* You can enter all ad hoc remunerations or charges made to the vendor in the form of a vendor billing document.
* You can also enter expenses for a customer in an expense settlement document , using the same method.
* You can then create follow-on documents from these agency documents to perform settlement accounting for the invoices, credit memos, and commission.

One of the places where those documents can be found is in TM. Also known as "Cost Distribution Documents", are linked to freight orders, mainly purchase documents ( Fiori  App "Cost Allocatin Docs"):

The document marked as "Invoice" in the screenshot above, can be found in table WBRK and related:

================================================================================

The costing run enables efficient costing of a large number of materials in collective processing.

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_BNK\_BATCH
**Name (en):** FSCM Bank Communication Management BATCH
**General Description**
=======================

These tables filled the account statement monitor and shows you the status of the account statements of individual house bank accounts. Information from the external account statement from the bank is compared with internal account information in Financial Accounting and various statuses are determined.

A bank statement that has not arrived or has arrived late can be identified just as quickly with this function as a difference between the bank statement balance and the internal G/L account balance. Unallocated items or posting differences are also recognised in this way.

================================================================================

| VARIT | Variant Texts | Variantentexte | VARI |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_VARI
**Name (en):** Report Variants
**General Description**
=======================

A variant is a set of filters and input paratmeters that can be defined to view specific subsets of data from the report. 

With a report variant, you can specify default values for fields, hide fields or make them unavailable for input on the initial screen of a program. You can use report variants to schedule in the background or to define a report transaction. For technical

reasons, only the display and input availability of the selection criteria can be influenced for the program. The display and input availability of other fields (for example, the parameters for mass processing or the list category) can only be controlled via a

transaction variant. Conversely, the selection criteria cannot be influenced in the transaction variant.

Also, variants are a simple method for passing values to a report program in a background job.

Can be created when saving values in execution mode of a report:

which brings to the variant creation screen:

Also, in transaction SE38 (or SE80) it can be managed:

================================================================================

=======================

The device, device category, and device number in the Utilities Industry (IS-U) component correspond to equipment, material, and the serial number in the standard system.

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_VTB\_LIMITS
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM VTB Limits
**General Description**
=======================

In limit management, functions are available for managing risk via established limits and for monitoring these limits and compliance with them. Any combination of available features ensures flexible limit management. This component is part of the Credit Risk Analyzer.

The focus of the Credit Risk Analyzer is on the measurement, analysis and management of counterparty default risks. In the first expansion stage, the aim is to cover the specific risks for a company's financial transactions. In order to carry out this active controlling, the Credit Risk Analyzer includes risk management through limit specifications and flexible limit management with online monitoring as well as comprehensive reporting. This enables company managers to identify and avoid default risks as they arise.

The corresponding SAP tables are transferred using this business object.

================================================================================

| S/4 Business Object | CO\_COSTELEM\_PRIM\_SDT  CO\_COSTELEM\_SEC\_SDT    (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COSTELEM\_PRIM/ CO60COSTELEM\_ELEM) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| CSKA | Cost Elements (Data Dependent on Chart of Accounts) | Kostenarten (Kontenplanabhängige Daten) |  |
| CSKU | Cost Element Texts | Kostenartentexte | CSKA |
| CSKB | Cost Elements (Data Dependent on Controlling Area) | Kostenarten (Kostenrechnungskreisabhängige Daten) | CSKA |
| SKA1 | G/L Account Master (Chart of Accounts) |  | CSKA |
| SKB1 | G/L account master (company code) |  | SKA1 |
| SKAT | G/L Account Master Record (Chart of Accounts: Description) |  | CSKA |
| SKAS | G/L account master (chart of accounts: key word list) |  | CSKA |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_CECSKA  CO\_CECSKB  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60CECSKA/CO60CECSKB)
**Name (en):** Cost Elements / Cost Element B-Segment
General Description
===================

The cost components of the product cost elements determine how the costed material is valuated.

For each cost element, you specify whether the assigned costs are to be included in further valuations.

Primary costs are, for example material costs or costs of external procurement. In the financial accounting, the primary costs are taken directly from the expense accounts of the income statement.

Secondary costs have no equivalent in financial accounting. The costs are determined via internal activity allocations and assigned to the cost centers. An example of this is the use of internal services.

The cost category is divided into two business objects, because CSKA is dependent on the chart of accounts, but CSKB is dependent on the cost accounting key.

================================================================================

| BKK83 | Bank Condition Determination | Bankkonditionsfindung |  |
| BKK81T | Texts for Bank Conditon | Texte für Bankkondition |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_IHC\_COND
**Name (en):** FSCM Inhouse Cash Conditions
**General Description**
=======================

A condition determines the basis for settlement in the account statement and in the context of payment transactions. Conditions control the calculation of interest, the charging of fees and the value date modalities.

Each condition has a specific condition category. This condition type determines the basic functionality with which a condition is used to calculate charges, interest and the value date. Additional condition categories can be created by the customer, but are not interpreted by the system without extensions to the standard. The condition types are divided into the following groups: Interest, fees and value date.  
This BO adopts the conditions created

================================================================================

| FIBL\_RPCODE\_GREL | Assign Repetitive Codes/Groups | Zuordnung Repetitve Codes /Gruppen |  |
| FIBL\_RPCODE\_GROU | Groups of Repetitive Codes | Gruppen von Repetitive Codes | X |
| FIBL\_RPCODE\_GRTX | Group Names of Repetitive Code | Gruppenbezeichnungen von Repetitive Code |  |
| FIBL\_RPCODE\_TDEF | Default Values for Repetitive Codes | Vorschlagswerte für Repetititve Codes |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_BNK\_PT
**Name (en):** FSCM BNK Repetitve Payments
**General Description**
=======================

A key that designates the payment transfer data that remains unchanged.

This includes the sender bank, sender account, recipient bank, recipient account, payment method, and currency. It is used to reduce the administrative work required for frequently recurring payments with the same payment details. The only details that are different for each payment are the amount and the note to payee. In addition, in the USA, a company can agree a repetitive code with its house bank. The bank then only needs the repetitive code, the amount, and (if required) the note to payee in order to process a payment order.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CA\_JOBS
**Name (en):** Jobs
**General Description**
=======================

Jobs are automated tasks that run mostly in the background without any user interaction. They are scheduled to run at specific times and are useful for executing time-consuming and repetitive tasks such as data archiving, batch processing, and report generation.

================================================================================

==========

All Tables

**ET Business Object:** UTIL\_DM\_POD
**Name (en):** Point of Delivery
**General Description**
=======================

Point to which a utility service is supplied, or for which a utility service can be determined.

A point of delivery can be identified by a unique key, called the point of delivery ID . This key is used for communication with external systems. It ensures that, for example, misunderstandings and incorrect data allocations can be avoided, even if a customer switches utility companies.

title: EHS_DGDS
page_id: 163717876
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/163717876
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | EH&S: Dangerous Goods Master |
| Name (de) | EH&S: Dangerous Goods Master |
| ET Business Object | EHS\_DGDS |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Header table** |
| DGTMD | Dangerous Goods Master |  |
| DGTM2 | Dangerous Goods Master (App. Table) | DGTMD |
| CCDUTCARR | Provisions for Carriage | DGTMD |
| CCDUTPACK | Packaging Code Approvals | DGTMD |
| CCDUTSPROV | Special Provisions | DGTMD |
| CCDUTTRAPPR | Transport Restrictions | DGTMD |

**ET Business Object:** EHS\_DGDS
**Name (en):** EH&S: Dangerous Goods Master
**General Description**
=======================

With Dangerous Goods Management, you can assess unpackaged and packaged dangerous goods, classify them according to dangerous goods regulations, and decide whether transportation of a product is allowed, allowed with restrictions, or forbidden.

To use the components of Dangerous Goods Management , the system requires data from the material master, engineering change management, and phrase management.

As the dangerous goods master is an enhancement for the material master, you create dangerous goods master records for materials that already exist there. The dangerous goods master contains the data required to perform dangerous goods checks and generate dangerous goods documents according to the respective dangerous goods regulations.

================================================================================

title: PPS CAPP standard times calculation: Formula
page_id: 124967898
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124967898
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | PPS CAPP standard times calculation: Formula |
| Name (de) | PP CAPP Vorgabezeitberechnung: Formeln |
| ET Business Object | PP\_CAPFORM  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60CAPFORM) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the tables required to migrate the Capacity Standard Times calculation Fomulas. The structure of the BO is made up of a Header table, and some child tables underneath linked by a Table Key Relationship (TKR). The BO has not direct relationship to other BO’s. 

Calculating the capacity requirements of an operation for manufacturing a material takes place in scheduling using formulas from the work center. The formulas which you can maintain in Customizing contain formula parameters.
**Tables**
==========

**ET Business Object:** PP\_CAPFORM  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60CAPFORM)
**Name (en):** PPS CAPP standard times calculation: Formula

================================================================================

=======================

The BO contains the hazardous material record which can be created out of the Warehouse Management System.

---
title: Generic Hierarchy
page_id: 274578178
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274578178
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Generic Hierarchy |
| Name (de) | Generische Hierarchie |
| ET Business Object |  |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| WRF\_HIER\_ID | Hierarchy Table | Hierarchietabelle | X |
| WRF\_HIER\_ID\_T | Hierarchy Text | Hierarchietext |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_NODEBOX | Node Container | Knotencontainer |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_NODES | Hierarchy Node | Hierarchieknoten |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_NODES\_T | Node Texts | Knotentexte |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_OBJ | Objects of a Hierarchy | Objekte einer Hierarchie |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_OBJBOX | Object Container | Objekt-Container |  |
| WRF\_HIER\_OBJ\_T | Object Descriptions | Objektbeschreibungen |  |

**Name (en):** Generic Hierarchy
**General Description**
=======================

These tables belong to the **SAP Retail hierarchy management framework** — they store the technical and semantic structure for **merchandise hierarchies, store hierarchies, or customer hierarchies** that are used across Retail processes (assortment planning, pricing, promotions, reporting, etc.).  
Here’s what each table does:

---

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Explanation / Functional Role** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **WRF\_HIER\_ID** | Hierarchy Table | **Master table** that defines the hierarchy itself (e.g., “Merchandise Hierarchy Z001”, “Store Hierarchy EU”). It contains header information such as hierarchy ID, type, and validity period. |
| **WRF\_HIER\_ID\_T** | Hierarchy Text | Stores **language-dependent texts** (descriptions and labels) for each hierarchy ID defined in `WRF_HIER_ID`. |
| **WRF\_HIER\_NODEBOX** | Node Container | Technical table that manages **the relationships and container structure** for hierarchy nodes (e.g., how nodes are grouped or buffered in the database). |
| **WRF\_HIER\_NODES** | Hierarchy Node | Contains **the individual nodes** (levels, categories, or branches) within a hierarchy, including their parent-child relationships and sequencing. |
| **WRF\_HIER\_NODES\_T** | Node Texts | Holds the **language-dependent descriptions** of the hierarchy nodes (e.g., “Beverages”, “Men’s Apparel”). |
| **WRF\_HIER\_OBJ** | Objects of a Hierarchy | Links **business objects** (e.g., materials, sites, vendors) to hierarchy nodes. This table defines which entities are assigned to which node. |
| **WRF\_HIER\_OBJBOX** | Object Container | Technical structure that groups or manages objects assigned to hierarchy nodes — used for performance and buffering. |
| **WRF\_HIER\_OBJ\_T** | Object Descriptions | Stores **texts or descriptive names** of objects that belong to hierarchy nodes (e.g., article or store names). |

---

### 🔶 **Functional Summary**

Together, these tables form the **Generic Hierarchy Framework (GHF)** in SAP Retail, used to:

* Maintain **multi-level hierarchies** (product, site, vendor, or custom).
* Enable **assortment and promotion planning** based on hierarchy levels.
* Provide **cross-application structure** for analytics and reporting.
* Replace older, hard-coded hierarchy tables (e.g., `T179`, `T179T`) with a **flexible, extensible model**.

**Analyses**

[ET System analysis](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/ET+System+analysis) will mark the following object as relevant if the following tables are filled.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Object | Identifying table | ET Business Object |
|  |  |  |

================================================================================
