---
title: Carve Out of Company Codes
page_id: 136063494
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/136063494
---
Overview
========

In the age of globalization and reorganizations, companies not only merge and harmonize but sometimes split, sell business units, or separate company parts into a newly established legal entities / companies.

A typical scenario when a business unit is sold is the Company Codes Carve-Out, usually executed into a new System.

\*\*\*This article is focusing on the premises:

* The Company Codes are the basis for the Carve-out
* The Carve-out is bringing data over into a new empty system via Direct Update
* The new system is identical to the source system (Release, Customizing etc.)

The aim of the article is to describe the SLT execution but also to capture and share best experiences on Specific Organizational topics typical for Carve-Out projects.

Additional questions to be clarified that could raise the complexity:

* Is the whole history carved-out or is there a timeline? If so, how are open items to be handled? (consider the impact on the Workflow if only partially migrated)
* Are there mapping objects and which?
* Is there archived data and how does this impact the Carve-out?
* others

\*\*\*The below technical description assumes the simple scenario of a Carve-out

* without mapping
* for the whole history
* no archived data
* no intercompany processes

 

================================================================================

| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_FTBK\_RISK |

**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| KLARP | Default Risk Line Items | Ausfallrisiko-Einzelposten |  |
| KLARPOS | Line Items for Default Risk | Einzelposten für Ausfallrisiko |  |
| KLREP | Risk Line Items | Risiko-Einzelposten |  |
| KLREPOS | Risk Line Items | Risiko-Einzelposten |  |
| KLSDCPARAM | Credit Limit: Controlling External Default Risk Rates (NEW) | Kreditlimit: Steuerung externer Ausfallrisikosätze (neu) |  |
| KLZUEXTOBJ | Assignment of External Administration Key to Object Number | Zuordnung externer Verwaltungsschlüssel zur Objektnummer |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_FTBK\_RISK
**Name (en):** FSCM Risk Management
**General Description**
=======================

Counterparty risk describes the risk that a receivable may lose its value due to a deterioration in the creditworthiness of the business partner. Counterparty risks are divided into credit risk and settlement risk. The existence of these two risks depends on when the transaction is analysed. Credit risks exist over the entire term of the transaction, while settlement risks only arise during the settlement period. Depending on the type of transaction, the credit risk may be a pure counterparty risk or may also include an issuer risk (e.g. in the case of securities forward transactions).

Country risk describes the risk of a receivable losing its value due to a deterioration in the creditworthiness of a country at risk. Country risk measures the possibility that a business partner in the country in question or the country itself will be unable to fulfil its obligations due to political measures or economic problems.

================================================================================

title: SD Handling Unit / Status
page_id: 120124565
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120124565
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SD Handling Unit / Status |
| Name (de) | SD Handling Unit / Status |
| ET Business Object | SD\_HU  SD\_HUSTAT |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Business Object | Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| SD\_HU | VEKP | Handling Unit - Header Table | Handling Unit Kopftabelle | X |
| SD\_HU | VEPO | Packing: Handling Unit Item (Contents) | Verpacken: Handling Unit Position (Inhalt) |  |
| SD\_HU | VEVW | Where-Used List for Handling Units | Verwendungsnachweis für Handling Units |  |
| SD\_HU | HUMSEG | Reference-HU-Item for Material Doc.Item that was Posted Last | Referenz HU-Position zu letzter gebuchter Mat.belegpos. |  |
| SD\_HU | HURES | Tentative Reservation of HU Items | weiche Reservierungen von HU Positionen |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | HUSTOBJ | Information about HU Status Object | Informationen zum HU-Statusobjekt | X |
| SD\_HUSTAT | HUSSTAT | Individual Status for Each Handling Unit | Einzelstatus pro Handling Unit |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| SD\_HUSTAT | JCDO | Change Documents for Status Object (Table JSTO) | Änderungsbelege zum Statusobjekt (Tabelle JSTO) |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_HU  SD\_HUSTAT
**Name (en):** SD Handling Unit / Status
**General Description**
=======================

You can use Handling Unit Management (HUM) to reflect packing-based logistics structures in the SAP System. Using this method, you track the movements of entire handling units and the materials they contain rather than tracking each material individually.

When you base logistics processes on handling units, goods movements processing is made easier, which in turn optimizes all logistics operations. This simplified processing includes the existing packing function in shipping and warehouse processing in the Warehouse Management system, which is expanded here.

Handling units contain all inventory management information of the materials they contain that are maintained in Inventory Management. There are also status messages that you can call up at any time that indicate whether a handling unit is only planned or if the ship-to party has been notified of the arrival of this handling unit, or whether it is in the warehouse or has already been posted to goods issue. The integrated history function also records each business process in the life cycle of each handling unit, meaning that you can track the handling unit’s path and development at any time.

In HU-managed storage locations, all goods movements are executed through the specification of the respective HUs, and Inventory Management is performed through the handling units. If you are not working with HU-managed storage locations, you can use handling units (without stock information) as before in the delivery and in the shipment.

In HU-managed storage locations, materials can be managed in HUs only. Mixed stock (stock made up of packed and non-packed materials within the same storage location) is not supported. HUs can also be managed in interim storage types. Unpacking a material from an HU means that the stock of the material is posted to a storage location that is not HU-managed

In the system, the handling unit (HU) expands on the shipping unit. Handling units can be nested, which means that you can create new handling units from several handling units as often as you like. At the material item level, HUs contain the complete material identification, the quantity, and, in the case of serial numbers , the respective object list. Handling units have unique, scannable identification numbers that can be developed according to standards such as EAN 128 or SSCC.

Click here for more details

Definition
----------

A handling unit is a physical unit consisting of packaging materials (load carriers/packing material) and the goods contained on/in it. A handling unit is always a combination of products and packaging materials. All the information contained in the product items, for example, about batches, is retained in the handling units and is always available.

For more information, see [Packaging Materials](https://help.sap.com/viewer/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/1909.001/en-US/19cccb53ad377114e10000000a174cb4.html).

Handling units can be nested, and you can create a new handling unit from several other handling units.

A handling unit has a unique, scannable identification number that you can construct according to industry standards such as GSI-128 or SSCC.

Fig. 1: Example of a Handling Unit

Structure
---------

The object “Handling Unit” contains the following attributes:

* Identification number
* Dimension
* Weight
* Volume
* Status
* Products and quantities
* Packaging material
* Packaging specification

A handling unit consists of a handling unit header and handling unit items. It receives a unique number that is used for identification purposes.

Handling Unit Header

The header data of a handling unit is divided up as follows:

* Weight/volume/dimension
* General data
* Storage-specific data

For more information about the different detailed information, see [Packing at the Work Center](https://help.sap.com/viewer/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/1909.001/en-US/f2e53a521fea116be10000000a423f68.html).

Status

The handling unit is connected to general status management. This enables you to document the various physical statuses (such as planned or realized) and other attributes (such as, weighed, loaded, blocked, or posted for GI) for a handling unit.

If you want to use your own status, you can store a user schema for the packaging material type.

Handling Unit Item

The items of a handling unit can consist of products, auxiliary packaging materials, or other handling units, which are displayed in an overview. For more information about this overview, see [Packing at the Work Center](https://help.sap.com/viewer/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/1909.001/en-US/f2e53a521fea116be10000000a423f68.html).

================================================================================

**Name (en):** IS-U premise
**General Description**
=======================

The premise is an enclosed spatial unit that is supplied with energy, for example, an apartment or factory.

A premise can have several utility installations. Since the premise is division-independent, you can allocate electricity and water installations to the same premise, for example.

---
title: PLM Recipe
page_id: 184320014
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/184320014
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | PLM Recipe |
| Name (de) | PLM-Rezept |
| ET Business Object | PLM\_RECIPE |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| /PLMB/RCP\_RECIPE | Recipes | Rezepte |  |
| /PLMB/RCP\_DESCR | Descriptions | Beschreibungen | /PLMB/RCP\_RECIPE |
| /PLMB/RCP\_PLNT | Plant Assignment for Recipe | Werkszuordnung für Rezept | /PLMB/RCP\_RECIPE |
| /PLMB/RCP\_ASSIGN | Assignment of Recipe Items |  | /PLMB/RCP\_RECIPE |
| /PLMI/RCP\_EHS | Default Exception Value | Standardausnahmewert |  |
| /PLMI/RCP\_EHSIDL | Define Identification Listing | Identifikationsauflistung definieren |  |

**ET Business Object:** PLM\_RECIPE
**Name (en):** PLM Recipe
**General Description**
=======================

You use this component to create and manage the master data for process manufacturing.

The central object of this component is the [recipe](https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP_SPV/55cf43c74134475f91f84a9ae58758f7/d710b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html?version=6.00.29) . You use it to describe the manufacturing of products or the execution of a process. Recipes comprise information about the products and components of a process, the process steps to be executed, and the resources required for the production.

Recipes are used at different enterprise levels. They serve as a basis for enterprise-wide investment decisions as well as for site-related long-term planning, or the conversion of a process in a plant. The recipe types in Recipe Management enable you to create a general description of the requirements or concrete procedural instructions depending on your needs. By making the data of enterprise-wide recipes more concrete, site- and plant-specific recipes are derived from them.

================================================================================

title: FSCM TRM Trade repository
page_id: 281379792
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/281379792
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | FSCM TRM Trade repository |
| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Handelsverzeichnis |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TARO |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TARO |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_TARO |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TLRT\_TARO\_MMF | Trade Repository Object Administrative Data | Verwaltungsdaten für Transaktionsregisterobjekt |  |
| TLRT\_TARO\_ADM | Mismatched fields reported by trade repository | Vom Transaktionsregister gemeldete nicht abgeglichene Felder |  |
| TLRT\_TARO\_PFIELD | Trade Repository: Protected Fields | Transaktionsregister: Geschützte Felder |  |
| TLRT\_TRADE\_ID | Trade Identifier for Financial Transactions |  |  |
| I73TRM | Treasury |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TARO
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Trade repository
**General Description**
=======================

For reasons of financial market regulation, companies must report their derivative financial transactions to a trade repository. This report is made in addition to the report to the central clearing of derivative financial transactions or to the clearing threshold reporting (CSR). The trade repository reporting functions support you in creating such trade repository reports. Once the process has been completed, your trade repository reports are available in file form on the application server for transmission to the trade repository.

The FSCM TRM Trade repository business object is used, among other things, to transfer data generated as part of this process.

================================================================================

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| table | Description | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| CRFH | CIM production resource/tool master data | CIM Production Resource and Tool Master Data | PP\_PRTFHM\_D PP\_PRTFHM\_M PP\_PRTFHM\_S |
| CRID | CIM Resource - Entities | Production resource - entities |  |
| CRTX | Text for the Work Center or Production Resource/Tool | Arbeitsplatz/ Fertigungshilfsmittel Kurzbezeichnung |  |
| CRVD\_A | Link of PRT to Document | Verknüpfung Fertigungshilfsmittel - Dokument |  |
| CRVD\_B | Link of Document to PRT | Verknüpfung Dokument - Fertigungshilfsmittel |  |
| CRVE\_A | Assignment PRT data - equipment | Verknüpfung FHM-Daten - Equipment | PP\_PRTFHM\_E |
| CRVE\_B | Assignment equipment - PRT data | Verknüpfung  Equipment - FHM-Daten |  |
| CRVM\_A | Link of PRT data to a material | Verknüpfung Fertigungshilfsmitteldaten - Material |  |
| CRVM\_B | Link of material to a production resource/tool | Verknüpfung Material - Fertigungshilfsmitteldaten |  |
| CRVP\_A | Linkage of PRT Data - Measuring Point | Verknüpfung FHM-Daten - Messpunkt | PP\_PRTFHM\_P |
| CRVP\_B | Linkage of Measuring Point - PRT Data | Verknüpfung Messpunkt - FHM-Daten |  |
| CRVS\_A | Relationship of PRT internal number to PRT external number | Verknüpfung Fertigungshilfsmittel ohne Material |  |
| CRVS\_B | Relationship of PRT external number to PRT internal number | Verknüpung Fertigungshilfsmittel ohne Material |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PRTFHM\_D PP\_PRTFHM\_E PP\_PRTFHM\_M PP\_PRTFHM\_P PP\_PRTFHM\_S
**Name (en):** Production resource / tools
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the Production Resource/tools data. The structure contains 1 Header table, and multiple child tables underneath linked by a simple Table Key Relationship (TKR). 

Unlike machines and fixed assets, production resources and tools (PRTs) are movable (not stationary) operating resources that are required to perform an activity and can be used repeatedly. For example, PRTs include documents, engineering drawings, fixtures, and measurement instruments. 

Production Resource Tool categories

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| English | | German | |
| Category | Description | FHM-Art | Bezeichnung |
| D | Document | D | Dolument |
| E | Equipment | E | Equipment |
| M | Material | M | Material |
| O | Miscellaneous | S | Sonstige |
| P | Measuring Point | P | Messpunkt |

================================================================================

| WBHE | Trading Contract: Schedule Lines | Trading-Kontrakt: Einteilungen |  |
| WBHF | Trading Contract: Document Flow | Trading-Kontrakt: Belegfluß |  |
| WBHI | Trading Contract: Item Data | Trading-Kontrakt: Positionsdaten |  |
| WBHK | Trading Contract: Header Data | Trading-Kontrakt: Kopfdaten | x |
| WBHP | Trading Contract: Partner Assignment | Trading-Kontrakt: Partnerzuordnung |  |
| WBRF | Document Flow for Settlement Managment Document | Belegfluss für Abrechnungsmanagementbeleg |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_TRADING
**Name (en):** Trading Contract
**General Description**
=======================

Trading contracts are documents in the SAP System that integrate multiple legal contracts between customers and suppliers. With the trading contract, both sales and purchase orders can be maintained using a single transaction.

The Trading Contract component enables you to implement numerous different business transactions for integrating sales documents and purchase documents. Depending on your business requirements, you can create, for example, the following trading contract types:

* Domestic trading contract
* Import trading contract
* Export trading contract
* Off-shore trading contract (a deal with a third country or region)

<https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7b24a64d9d0941bda1afa753263d9e39/4faa337f118516c7e10000000a42189d.html?locale=en-US>

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** SD\_PACK  ( SD\_PACK\_COND  tables of Business Object SD\_PACK\_COND will be created with  BO Status 97 )
**Name (en):** SD packing
**General Description**
=======================

A packing instruction serves as a template for the creation of a handling unit. In a packing instruction, you define the materials and packaging materials to be packed in a handling unit.

This enables you to reproduce specific customer packaging requirements in the system. You can also automate the packing process by creating a proposal for a handling unit, based on a packing instruction.

================================================================================

title: GOS

| Name (de) | Generische Objektdienste |
| ET Business Object | CA\_GOS  CA\_GOS\_MIG ( data conversions only ) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**General Description**
=======================

The Generic Object Services (GOS) offer functions that are available in various SAP applications. These functions always refer to the application object in use, for example, adding an attachment to the application object and showing linked objects to this one.

E.g. Attachment list to a material:

Program /CBSGMBH/DMF\_GOS\_TRANSPORT offers both export and import functionalities in one report, and the file parameters happen to be the same. The relevant table entries for the GOS objects will be created automatically in the background by function modules which are contained in program /CBSGMBH/DMF\_GOS\_TRANSPORT.
**Tables**
==========

**ET Business Object:** CA\_GOS  CA\_GOS\_MIG ( data conversions only )
**Name (en):** Generic Object Services (GOS)

**ET Business Object:** QM\_ORDER
**Name (en):** QM orders
**General Description**
=======================

General QM orders are defined as order type QL01 in the standard system. This order type has the following properties:

The order is a long-term cost collector

The order accumulates the costs on a lump sum basis for all the materials or inspection lots that are assigned to the order. You cannot, therefore, assign the costs to individual materials or inspection lots.

The costs are settled in the Controlling (CO) component on a periodic basis (for example, once a month).

Features:   
With a general QM order, you can:

Assign several different materials or inspection lots to the order

Confirm the activities for an inspection operation during results recording or when you make the usage decision

Create the order manually using the central maintenance function for creating and assigning QM orders

Use this type of order for goods movements without account assignments (for example, for normal goods receipt movements to warehouse stock)

================================================================================

=======================

**Overview**
============

| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| Name (de) | CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE) |
| ET Business Object | CO\_SUM |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | CO\_SUM\_SDT  [CO\_COSP\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)(deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_COSS\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_SUM\_COSPD\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_SUM\_COSSD\_SDT (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_SUM\_COST\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | CO\_SUM\_SDT  [CO\_COSP\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling)(deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_COSS\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_SUM\_COSPD\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  CO\_SUM\_COSSD\_SDT (deprecated with ET2023 FP1)  [CO\_SUM\_COST\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/193086430/CO-Sum+Totals+S4+specific+handling) |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE | General Object Number |  |  |
| COSB | CO Object: Total Variances/Results Analyses | CO-Objekt: Summen Abweichungen/Abgrenzungen | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSBD | CO Object: Total of variances/accruals, of which settled | CO-Objekt: Summen Abweichgen/Abgrenzgen - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSL | CO Object: Activity Type Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Leistungsarten | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSLD | CO Object: Activity Type Totals - of Which Settled | CO-Objekt: Summen Leistungsarten - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSP | Generated Table for View | Generated Table for View | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPD | CO Object: Settled Primary Cost Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Kosten extern - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPP | Transfer of the Order in the COSP Table to the Project | Übernahme der COSP-Tabelle Auftrag auf Projekt | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSPR | CO Objekt: Enhancement to COSP for Dependency Planning | CO-Objekt: Erweiterung der COSP für Rezeptplanung | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSR | CO Object: Statistical Key Figure Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Statistische Kennzahlen | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSS | Generated Table for View | Generated Table for View | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSSD | CO Object: Settled Secondary Cost Totals | CO-Objekt: Summen Kosten intern - davon abgerechnet | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |
| COSSP | Transfer of the Order COSS Table to the Project | Übernahme der COSS-Tabelle Auftrag auf Projekt | /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_SUM
**Name (en):** CO totals (under /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE)
**General Description**
=======================

CO totals tables are named COS\*, so for instance COSP, COSS, COSPR or COSLD.

This object contains the CO totals records. Totals records can be reconstructed using report RKACOR04. The program compares the CO summary files COSP and COSS with the line item file COEP. There are also some other analyses / correction reports, e.g. for [comparison with settlement data](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/display/CCETSLT/Tables+of+actual+settlement+and+analysis).

The period value of the totals records is adjusted to the total of the corresponding line items.  
When you start the report, you can decide whether only actual costs (value types 04 and 11) and parked documents and/or commitment values are to be included

All totals records for which a difference is found in a period between the totals record and the total of the corresponding line items are listed.  
If you start the report with update, the totals records with errors are corrected. However, totals values of a period for which no more line items exist are not changed, since the line items may already have been reorganized.  
If you convert/harmonize organizational units or master data, it is therefore necessary to delete the totals records for the data concerned from the totals table beforehand!  
The program works object by object. According to the selection criteria, all valid objects are determined from the /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE and the adjustment is then carried out (individually) for these. For this reason, you should restrict the object numbers to objects that actually have costs and where errors are suspected. There are cases in which it makes sense to pack the summary tables into a separate BO for each table. You select the object numbers using transaction /CBSGMBH/DMF\_SEL\_OBJNR (for a detailed explanation, see:[Dependencies in the Pre-Selector (/CBSGMBH/DMF\_SEL\_OBJNR)](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/download/attachments/3877689/Selektion_Preselector_OBJNR.docx?version=1&modificationDate=1417696361752&api=v2). The relevant object numbers are selected via table /CBSGMBH/A\_OBJSE. This then serves as a selection table for the totals tables.

The COSR is the totals table for statistical key figures. As can be seen in the Customizing table for statistical key figures (TKA03), however, there are two types of statistical key figures: Total values and fixed values.  
Total values must be added up, but not fixed values. The only way I can see how to do this with the SHC Framework / ET is to make a separate selection for statistical totals and fixed values in a separate BO.

All mappings that affect summary tables must not contain any superfluous mapping, that is, source value = target value. This is because all values contained in the mapping are added up - even if this is not desired with superfluous mapping. This error is known to ET development, but has not been fixed so far, because the functionality actually "works as designed". The mapping of cost elements, cost centers (contained in object numbers) and other CO totals key fields had to be checked and unnecessary mapping removed before the conversion! Therefore, an OIE FuBa was used in many projects, which only adds entries for which a field in the key has changed. A current version of this footbas is available at Continental: \*OIE\_CO\_SUM\_TAB. The OIE is to flow back into the ET shortly. This makes the following point superfluous:  
If there are cases in which no key field is mapped, then a separate BO must be created and this imported WITHOUT "add totals" - in the hope that records with this key do not yet exist in the target.

Backups via BACK\_\*

CO Totals status quo needs to be backed up before importing the totals tables in order to preserve the data. In case the import doesn't go smoothly / the results are not useful, the backup BOs can be imported with no mapping. But first a cleansing of the totals tables needs to be performed, see below

Deletion via CO60DEL\_CO\* (not implemented in LU ONE)

There are several possibilities in case CO Totals need to be corrected/reversed to the status quo.

For this purpose it is advisable to cleanse the CO totals and reimport the data as it was before the migration. For the cleansing, deletion BOs are created which resemble the backup BOs but the programs for their execution are different.

In step 1 the deletion BOs are executed (anaylsis and deletion)

In step 2 the backup BOs are imported (their export should have been performed before the start of the migration/conversion, no changes to the export to be made!)

This scenario delivers good results in case no postings have been done meanwhile (in the time between the export of the backups and now). If there are postings, the totals have been changed accordingly and a totals backup import won't set up the totals to a realistic state.

At this point, please use an alternative if applicable. The import using "add totals" that needs to be corrected can be "reversed" via new import using [reverse algebraic sign](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/43764843/Inverse+Totals).

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** MM\_INVSAMP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60INVSAMP)
**Name (en):** MM - Inventory Sampling
**General Description**
=======================

Instead of carrying out a physical inventory for all stocks it might be faster and less costly to count only the stock of individual materials and carrying out an extrapolation. 

The inventory sample can be done based on inventory or warehouse management. Inventory sampling can be done periodically or continuously.

================================================================================

title: CO - Cost Center Master Data
page_id: 117445322
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445322
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | cost center master data |
| Name (de) | cost center master data |
| ET Business Object | CO\_COSTCT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COSTCT) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_COSTCT\_SDT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COSTCT) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| CSKS | Cost Center Master Record | Kostenstellenstammsatz |  |
| CSSL | Cost Center/Activity Type | Kostenstelle / Leistungsart | CSKS |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer | CSKS |
| CSKT | Cost Center Texts | Kostenstellentexte | CSKS |
| COKA | CO Object: Control Data for Cost Elements | CO-Objekt: Steuerungsdaten Kostenart | ONR00 |
| ONRKS | Object Number Index, Cost Center | Objektnummernindex Kostenstelle | ONR00 |
| COSC | CO Object: Assignment Original Costing Sheets |  | ONRKS |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COSTCT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COSTCT)
**Name (en):** cost center master data
General Description
===================

**What is a cost center?**

* A cost center is a place in the company where services are performed, and costs are incurred
* Cost centers are a part of components of management accounting
* It´s a physical location where the costs of business transactions are shown and mapped

**When do you need a cost center?**

* You need a cost center, when you have in your company services, which are performed and costs which are incurred

**What should be considered by creating a cost center?**

* The cost center should be an independent and clearly separated area specific validation

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FI\_DOC (classic GL)  FI\_FAGL (new GL)
**Name (en):** FI Documents
**General Description**
=======================

| BO | Description |
| --- | --- |
| FI\_DOC | FI Beleg (klassisch) / FI Accounting Document (classic GL) |
| FI\_FAGL | FI Beleg (neues Hauptbuch) / FI Accounting Document (New GL) |

FI documents consist of a header table BKPF and the position in BSEG. Additionally, the data is stored in the so called inedex table BS\*. BO: **FI\_BKPF**

When NEW Gl is activated, a general ledger view is added to the FI document. The tables FAGFLEX\* need to be migrated as well. Use the BO **FI\_FAGL** instead.

For details in New GL see [New G/L](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/3877686/New+G+L)

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** MM\_VENDEVAL  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60VENDEVAL)
**Name (en):** Vendor evaluation
**General Description**
=======================

Vendor evaluation is the process of checking performance of vendors based on different criteria for giving ranking.

By evaluating vendors, enterprises can improve their competitiveness. The vendor evaluation provides accurate information on prices, and terms of payment and delivery of materials. It’s also possible to evaluate procured services.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CS\_SER
**Name (en):** CS Serial number link
**General Description**
=======================

The serial number is given to an item of material in addition to the material number, in order to differentiate that individual item from all other pieces of this material. The combination of material and serial number is unique.

In general, it's also assigned to an equipment.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** ( MM\_COND  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60COND) )  Business Object will be created with  BO Status 97
**Name (en):** MM Conditions (preise, mat-findung)
**General Description**
=======================

SAP MM pricing conditions are required for determination and calculation of prices in various purchasing documents.  

All these conditions are maintained in tables A001 – A999. They are related to the overall condition table KONH. Do not mix this up with table KONV which is used for transactional conditions. For more information please have a look at the details.

================================================================================

============

| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | RE-FX Kontrakte |
| ET Business Object | REFX\_Contract |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | REFX\_Contract |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | REFX\_Contract |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VIBDOBJASS | Object Assignment | Objektzuordnung |  |
| VIBDCNSUBJECT | Contract Object | Vertragsgegenstand |  |
| VIBDBUPT | Property Tax Data for Building | Grundsteuerdaten für Gebäude |  |
| VICINCN | Contracts | Kontrakte | X |
| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) |  |
| JCDO | Change Documents for Status Object (Table JSTO) | Änderungsbelege zum Statusobjekt (Tabelle JSTO) |  |
| VIBDAO | Architectural Object | Architektonisches Objekt |  |
| VIBDMEAS | Measurement | Bemessung |  |
| VIBDNODE | Higher-Level Architectural Object | Übergeordnetes Arch. Objekt |  |

**ET Business Object:** REFX\_Contract
**Name (en):** RE-FX Contracts
**General Description**
=======================

In **SAP RE-FX (Flexible Real Estate Management)**, a **contract** represents a formal agreement between your company and a business partner regarding the use of a real estate object—such as renting, leasing, or service provision.

Each contract in RE-FX is structured with multiple components:

* **Contract Type**  
  Defines the nature of the agreement, e.g.:

+ Lease-in (your company rents from someone)
+ Lease-out (your company rents out property)
+ Service contracts (e.g. cleaning, maintenance)
+ General contracts (e.g. security deposits, G/L postings)

* **Business Partner Assignment**  
  Identifies the parties involved—tenant, landlord, service provider, etc.
* **Contract Term**  
  Specifies start and end dates, renewal rules, and notice periods.
* **Rental Objects**  
  Links the contract to specific real estate units (e.g. buildings, rooms, land parcels).
* **Conditions**  
  Details the financial terms:

+ Rent amount
+ Payment frequency
+ Advance payments
+ Index-based adjustments

* **Posting Parameters**  
  Determines how and where financial postings are made (e.g. profit center, business area).
* **Cash Flow & Accounting Integration**  
  Automatically generates planned and actual payment flows, integrating with SAP FI/CO for accounting and reporting.

================================================================================

---

============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | FSCM TRM Nebenbuch-Verteiler |
| ET Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_SLDT |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_SLDT |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | FSCM\_TRM\_SLDT |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| SLDT\_TRANSACTION | Subledger Distributor Business Transactions | Nebenbuch-Verteilerereignisse | X |

| SLDT\_EVENT | Subledger Distributor Events | Nebenbuch-Verteilerereignisse |  |
|  |  |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_SLDT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Subledger Distributor
**General Description**
=======================

The business object FSCM\_TRM\_SLDT is used to transfer the subledger distributor data. In SAP Treasury, a subledger distributor is a functionality that enables the distribution of accounting postings, such as deferrals or valuation remnants, to multiple subledger accounts based on specific criteria, like balances or amortization periods. This ensures that the financial impact of transactions is accurately reflected across different subledgers in accordance with defined rules.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** QM\_QPCPD
**Name (en):** Control Plan
**General Description**
=======================

The control plan (CP) describes how products and processes are monitored. It contains actions that are executed for each phase of the process. In addition, the requirements for goods entry and goods issue inspections, for process controls, and for recurring inspections and controls are stored in the control plan so that the process can be controlled.

================================================================================

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Bezeichnung (DE)** | **Main Table** |
| QPRVK | Sample-drawing procedure | Probenahmeverfahren | X |
| QPRVKT | Header for sample drawing procedure texts | Kopf Probenahmeverfahren Texte |  |
| QPRVP | Sample-drawing items | Probenahmeanweisungen |  |

**ET Business Object:** QM\_SADP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 QM60SADP)
**Name (en):** Sample-Drawing Procedures
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate thesample-drawing procedures.

Sample-drawing procedures are used to plan the drawing of physical samples. Together with the information in the inspection plan the information will be used to calculate the physical sample sizes, and to create physical-sample records when an inspection lot is created.

================================================================================

---
title: BP on the Fly
page_id: 274569818
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/274569818
---
General Description
===================

Business Partners for Customers | Vendors | Contact Partners (ECC) can be created directly on the database in S/4 by using the cbs ET Enterprise Transformer. For this purpose you have to run the Business Objects MD2BP\* in a certain sequential order (see [Migration Steplist](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/295223695/Migration+Steplist)). 

### Advantages of Creating Business Partners by "BP on the fly"

* Longer timeline for data cleansing possible because BP can already be validated after migration even if they can not be used yet
* No CVI Customizing in source system necessary

### Disadvantages of Creating Business Partners by "BP on the fly"

* Increased effort on customer side for thorough data cleansing in the source system and for the definition of mappings in Customer and Vendor Master. For a successful Business Partner creation, it is essential to have very high-quality customer and supplier data in the target system before the start of the Business Partner creation by "BP on the Fly". For this, it is necessary to talk to the customer about data quality topics as early as possible. In addition to the standard checks, MDV tool can also be used here.

================================================================================

| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| Tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AFDIST | Distribution Information on Production Order | Verteilungsinformationen zum Fertigungsauftrag |  |
| AFFH | PRT assignment data for the work order | FHM-Zuordnung Werkauftrag |  |
| AFFL | Work order sequence | Werkauftragsfolge |  |
| AFFW | Goods Movements with Errors from Confirmations | fehlerhafte Warenbewegungen aus Rückmeldungen |  |
| AFKO | Order Header Data PP Orders | Auftragskopfdaten PPS-Aufträge |  |
| AFKO\_KAL | Storing calendar selections for Maintenance Order | |  |
| AFPO | Order item | Auftragsposition |  |
| AFRC | Incorrect cost calculations from confirmations | Falsche Kostenberechnung aus Rückmeldungen |  |
| AFRP4 | Table of planned changes to confirmatn: Data transfer to HR | Vormerktabelle der Rückmeldung: Datentransfer zum HR |  |
| AFRU | Order Confirmations | Auftragsrückmeldungen |  |
| AFRU\_TEXT | AFRU Long Text (Mapping) | |  |
| AFVC | Operation within an order | Auftragsarbeitsvorgang |  |
| AFVC\_PS\_ADMIN | Network Activity - Project Systems Administration | Netzplanvorgang - Projektsystemadministration |  |
| AFVC\_TEXT | Order Operation Long Text (Mapping) |  |  |
| AFVU | DB structure of the user fields of the operation | DB-Struktur der Benutzerfelder des Arbeitsvorganges |  |
| AFVV | DB structure of the quantities/dates/values in the operation | DB-Struktur des Arbeitsvorgangs Mengen/Termine/Werte |  |
| AFWD | Deliveries for Confirmation Goods Movements in EWM | Lieferungen zu Rückmeldungs-Warenbewegungen im EWM |  |
| AFWI | Subsequently posted goods movements for confirmations | nachträglich gebuchte Warenbewegungen zu Rückmeldungen |  |
| AUFK | Order master data | Auftragsstammdaten | X |
| AUFK\_TEXT | AUFK Long Text (Mapping) |  |  |
| AUFM | Goods movements for order | Materialbewegungen zum Auftrag |  |
| COBRA | Settlement Rule for Order Settlement | Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung |  |
| COBRB | Distribution Rules Settlement Rule Order Settlement | Aufteilungsregeln Abrechnungsvorschrift Auftragsabrechnung |  |
| FAPW | Index of production-/issuing plant for production order | Index vom Produktions-/Lieferwerk zum Fertigungsauftrag |  |
| MCKALKW | Versions: Cost Itemization | Versionen Kosteneinzelnachweis |  |
| OBJK | Plant Maintenance Object List | Objektliste Instandhaltung |  |
| ORDCOM | Communication control Operation download | Kommunikationssteuerung AVO-Versorgung |  |
| SER00 | General Header Table for Serial Number Management | Allgemeine Kopftabelle für die Serialnummernverwaltung |  |
| SER05 | Document Header for Serial Numbers for PP Order | Belegkopf für Serialnummern zum PP-Auftrag |  |
| AFSPREL | Relation Between Orders in an Order Split | Relation der Aufträge eines Auftragssplits |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_ORDER
**Name (en):** Production Order
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate production order data, as well it contains confirmations, reservations, capacity requirements.

A production order defines which material is to be processed, at which location, at what time and how much work is required. It also defines which resources are to be used and how the order costs are to be settled. A production order can be identified by the order category: AUFK-AUTYP = 10.

When a production order is created the following actions are carried out:

* A routing (PP\_STROUT) is selected, its operations and sequences are transferred to the order
* The bill of materials (PP\_BOM) is exploded and the items in the bill of material are transferred to the order
* Reservations (PP\_RESERV) are generated for bill of material items held in stock
* The planned costs for the order are generated
* The capacity requirements are generated for the work centers (PP\_WRKCTR)
* Purchase requisitions (MM\_PURREQ) are generated for non-stock items and externally-processed operations

The structure is comprised of a Header table, plus several child tables linked underneath by their respective Table Key Relationships (TKR).

This BO acts as a super BO to several other BO’s.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CO\_PRTCTR
**Name (en):** MD Profit Center
**General Description**
=======================

The profit center master data are the classification terms according to which the data relevant for a profit center invoice can be posted and then evaluated.

CEPC\_BUKRS acts as a filter, that is, if a profit center is to be assigned to all company codes, there is no entry in CEPC\_BUKRS. Entries in CEPC\_BUKRS mean that the profit center is not assigned to all company codes, but only to these company codes.

================================================================================

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | Klassen |

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

BO CA\_KLASS

| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CLAENNR | Change number-to-class alloc.: classification effectivity | Änderungsnummer-Klassenzuordnung: Klassifiz. Effectivity |
| CUFM | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Form | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Form |
| CUSD | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner |
| CUSDT | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Descriptions | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Bezeichnungen |
| CUSF | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Frame | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Frame |
| CUSFT | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Frame Desc. | Customizing: Class/Config: Screendesigner Frametext |
| KLAH | Class Header Data | Klassenkopf Daten |
| KLAT | Classes: Long Texts | Klassen: Langtexte |
| KRIF | Richie Riffle | Richie Riffle |
| KSML | Characteristics of a Class | Merkmale zu  Klassen |
| SWOR | Classification System: Keywords | Klassifizierungssytem: Schlagwörter |
| CLSELSTATADM | Administrative Info on Statistics in Classification System | Verwaltungsinformationen zur Statistik im Klassensystem |
| GEN\_TABLES\_MAIN | Generated Tables for Search in Classification System | Generierte Tabellen für die Suche im Klassensystem |
| GEN\_TABLES\_SUB | Generated Tables for Multiple-Value Characteristics | Generierte Tabellen für mehrwertige Merkmale |
| TCLD | Characteristic Data from Standard | Merkmaldaten aus Norm |
| KSSK | Allocation Table: Object to Class | Zuordnungstabelle: Objekt zu Klasse |
| AUSP | Characteristic Values | Merkmalswerte |

BO CA\_SPECIAL\_CLASS

| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE |
| --- | --- | --- |
| LKLAH | Class Header (Special Objects) | Klassenkopf (Sonderobjekte). |
| LKSML | Characteristics of a Class | Merkmale zu  Klassen |
| LKSSK | Allocation Table: Object to Class | Zuordnungstabelle: Objekt zu Klasse |
| LSWOR | Classification System: Keywords | Klassifizierungssytem: Schlagwörter |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_CLASS  ~~till ET 2025 FP 0:  CA\_KLASS~~  CA\_SPECIAL\_CLASS  CA\_CLINT\_MAPPING
**Name (en):** Classes
**General Description**
=======================

A classification system is describing objects (e.g., materials, batches, documents) with the help of characteristics. Objects to which the same characteristics have been assigned are grouped into classes. Similar classes are summarized by a common class type (KLAH-KLART).  

In other words: Objects are organized per class type, class, characteristics and characteristic values. This ensures that objects with similar or identical characteristics can be found quickly.  

Each class type represents a self-contained world, the class types have no linkage to each other.

 

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FI\_FAGL
**Name (en):** Accounting documents (new GL)
**General Description**
=======================

Like business object FI\_DOC (for source systems with classic GL), FI\_FAGL includes tables, which should be split into separate BOs for most projects. The reason the BO is delivered as it is, is to make sure no important tables are forgotten, while keeping LU ONE from becoming bloated. Please check which tables are necessary for your project.

For most projects, it makes sense to migrate the actual documents (BKPF / BSEG) separately from the index tables. Depending on data volume and system performance, the index tables should be split into multiple BOs, if needed.

================================================================================

| ONRKS | Object Number Index, Cost Center | Objektnummernindex Kostenstelle | ONR00 |
| COSC | CO Object: Assignment Original Costing Sheets |  | ONRKS |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COSTCT
**Name (en):** cost center master data
**General Description**
=======================

**What is a cost center?**

* A cost center is a place in the company where services are performed, and costs are incurred
* Cost centers are a part of components of management accounting
* It´s a physical location where the costs of business transactions are shown and mapped

**When do you need a cost center?**

* You need a cost center, when you have in your company services, which are performed and costs which are incurred

**What should be considered by creating a cost center?**

* The cost center should be an independent and clearly separated area specific validation

================================================================================

=======================

In SAP RE-FX (Flexible Real Estate Management), a **Business Entity** represents a logical grouping of real estate objects—such as buildings, rental units, or land parcels—based on their economic purpose or geographical location.

Example Structure

| Level | Object Type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Business Entity | RE-FX Business Entity | “Residential Park North City” |
| Building | Building | “Building A” |
| Rental Object | Rental Object | “Apartment 1A” |

**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the equipment. An equipment represents a physical object which can be independently maintained in the industry. It is an asset that can be moved from one place to another. Examples of equipment can be transportation utilities, measurement devices, PC systems, machine spare parts, etc.

title: Assortment
page_id: 246485736
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/246485736
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Retail Assortment |
| Name (de) | Retail Sortiment |
| ET Business Object | Z\_RET\_SOR  Z\_RET\_ASSORT    ~~Z\_RET\_SOR~~  ~~Z\_RET\_ASSORT~~ |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| WAZB | Module plant-group assignment | Baustein-Werksgruppenzuordnung |  |
| WLK1 | Listing Conditions SAP Retail Assortments | Listungs-Konditionen SAP Retail Sortimente |  |
| WRS1 | Assortment | Sortiment | x (Z\_RET\_SOR) |
| WRS1\_LAYCONV | Assortments Where Layout Listing Conditions Checked and Conv | Sortimente, deren Layoutlistungskonditionen geprüft und umg |  |
| WRS6 | Material groups in the assortment | Warengruppen zum Sortiment |  |
| WRST | Assortment description | Bezeichnung Sortiment |  |
| WRSZ | Assignment of assortment user to assortment | Zuordnung Sortimentsnutzer zu Sortiment |  |
| WSOF | Module Assignment (SAP Retail) to Plants | Zuordnung Bausteine SAP Retail zu Betrieben |  |
| WSOH | Assortment Modules SAP Retail | Sortiments-Bausteine SAP Retail | x (Z\_RET\_ASSORT) |
| WSOK | Assortment objects | Sortimentsobjekte |  |
| WSOP | Modules SAP Retail / Item Data | Bausteine SAP Retail / Positions-Daten |  |
| WSORP | Control Parameters for Assortments and Listing | Parameter zur Steuerung der Sortimente und der Listung |  |
| WSOT | SAP Retail Modules - Descriptions | Bausteine SAP Retail - Bezeichnungen |  |

**ET Business Object:** Z\_RET\_SOR  Z\_RET\_ASSORT    ~~Z\_RET\_SOR~~  ~~Z\_RET\_ASSORT~~
**Name (en):** Retail Assortment
**General Description**
=======================

This component allows you to create merchandise assortments and assortment modules and assign articles to them. Articles can be automatically assigned directly to assortments as a result of checking rules during article maintenance or assortment maintenance functions. Alternatively, you can manually assign articles to assortment modules and then in turn assign the modules to assortments. In both cases, the system automatically generates listing conditions for each valid combination of article and assortment.

By assigning assortments to sites you control which assortments — and which articles — a store can purchase or sell. A site cannot purchase or sell an article that is not contained in one of its assortment.

these tables belong to the **Assortment and Listing Management** area of **SAP Retail**.  
They define which materials (articles) are *listed* and *available* in which **stores or store groups**, via so-called **assortments (WRS*)*\* and \**assortment modules (WSO*)\*\*.  
Here’s the structured explanation in English:

---

| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Explanation / Functional Role** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **WRS1** | Assortment | **Master table** for Retail assortments. Each record defines an assortment (e.g., “Fashion Spring 2025”) with validity, type (general, local, layout), and controlling parameters. Used to group which articles are valid for which sites. |
| **WRST** | Assortment Description | Contains **language-dependent texts** for assortments from `WRS1` (the descriptive names displayed in Retail maintenance and reporting). |
| **WRS6** | Material Groups in the Assortment | Holds **the article hierarchy or material group assignments** that belong to an assortment. Defines which merchandise categories are included in that assortment. |
| **WRSZ** | Assignment of Assortment User to Assortment | Links **assortment users (plants, stores, DCs, etc.)** to specific assortments, controlling which assortment applies to which site. |
| **WRS1\_LAYCONV** | Assortments Where Layout Listing Conditions Checked and Converted | Technical table used during **layout-driven assortment/listing checks** — indicates which assortments were evaluated and converted during the layout process. Mostly used in background or migration routines. |
| **WLK1** | Listing Conditions SAP Retail Assortments | Stores **listing conditions** per assortment and article (e.g., validity dates, listing reason, source of listing). Key for determining article availability at site level. |
| **WSOH** | Assortment Modules (Header) | **Master table** for Retail assortment modules. Each module groups a set of materials (e.g., “Beverage Module”) that can be assigned to multiple assortments. Used for modular planning and rollout. |
| **WSOT** | Assortment Module Descriptions | Language-dependent texts for assortment modules (`WSOH`). |
| **WSOP** | Assortment Modules / Item Data | Contains **the items (materials)** that belong to an assortment module. It’s the detailed link between module and article. |
| **WSOF** | Module Assignment to Plants | Assigns assortment modules (`WSOH`) to specific sites (plants or store groups). This enables **modular rollout** of assortments to multiple stores. |
| **WAZB** | Module Plant-Group Assignment | Groups plants/stores for module assignments — e.g., “Store Group North” assigned to beverage module. Used to simplify large-scale distribution of assortment modules. |
| **WSORP** | Control Parameters for Assortments and Listing | Contains **customizing parameters** controlling how assortments and listings behave (e.g., automatic listing checks, validity overlaps, dependency rules). |
| **WSOK** | Assortment Objects | Technical link table defining **which business objects (articles, variants, materials)** belong to an assortment or module. Supports flexible object types beyond just materials. |

---

### 🔶 **Functional Summary**

These tables together form the **Assortment and Listing Engine** in SAP Retail:

1. **Assortment Definition:**  
   `WRS1` (header) + `WRST` (text) + `WRS6` (material groups).
2. **Assignment to Sites:**  
   `WRSZ` links assortments to stores or DCs.
3. **Listing Conditions:**  
   `WLK1` determines which materials are actually valid for sale in each site.
4. **Assortment Modules:**  
   `WSOH`, `WSOT`, `WSOP`, and `WSOF` handle reusable module structures.
5. **Control Logic:**  
   `WSORP` and `WAZB` manage process parameters and plant groups.

---

### 🔶 **Transformation / S/4HANA Context**

* Still relevant in **S/4HANA Retail & Fashion**; however:

  + Many of these are now exposed via **Fiori apps** like *Manage Assortments* and *Manage Listing Conditions*.
  + Analytical access is through CDS Views (`I_Assortment`, `I_AssortmentModule`, `I_ListingCondition`).
  + Custom developments should **use APIs or CDS Views** instead of direct DB access (Clean Core principle).
  + Integration with **SAP CAR Unified Demand Forecasting** or **Omnichannel Article Availability (OAA)** may change listing and assortment relevance logic.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** SD\_FREIGH  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60FREIGH)
**Name (en):** SD Shipment Costs
**General Description**
=======================

This component contains the functions for calculating and settling shipment costs in transportation. The following items describe how the shipment costs are determined in the shipment, how they are transmitted to accounting, and how they are settled with the service provider. The system also shows you how to obtain information regarding the shipment costs to be expected in the order.

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

Revenue Accounting Contract allows you to assign sales document items to Performance Obligations (POBs). A performance obligation is automatically created and assigned when you add new sales items. These assignments can be changed manually and you can assign several items to one POB.

title: Storage Bin
page_id: 117441199
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117441199
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Storage Bin |
| Name (de) | Lagerplatz |
| ET Business Object | WM\_STBIN |
| S/4 Business Object |  |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| LAGP | Storage bins | Lagerplätze |  |

**General Description**

A storage type generally contains several storage spaces. These are called storage bins in Warehouse Management (WM).

Storage bin is the smallest unit of space in a warehouse and it describes the position in the warehouse where the goods are or can be stored.

Since the address of a storage bin is often referred to as a coordinate, for e.g. coordinate 01-02-03 refers to a storage bin in row 1, stack 2, and level 3.

**ET Business Object:** WM\_STBIN
**Name (en):** Storage Bin

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

CO- line- items are created automatically in Controlling if a document is relevant to costs or revenues. A Controlling object, for example: customer output items, internal orders and cost centers, is assigned to the document when it is posted.

All primary costs can be evaluated immediately in the cost accounting.

The CO-line- items worked in a single- circuit system

================================================================================

| S/4 SDT Business Object | REFX\_Land |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | REFX\_Land |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VIBDOBJASS | Object Assignment | Objektzuordnung |  |
| VIBDCNSUBJECT | Contract Object | Vertragsgegenstand |  |
| VIBDPR | Land | Land | X |
| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) |  |
| JCDO | Change Documents for Status Object (Table JSTO) | Änderungsbelege zum Statusobjekt (Tabelle JSTO) |  |
| VIBDAO | Architectural Object | Architektonisches Objekt |  |
| VIBDMEAS | Measurement | Bemessung |  |
| VIBDNODE | Higher-Level Architectural Object | Übergeordnetes Arch. Objekt |  |

**ET Business Object:** REFX\_Land
**Name (en):** RE-FX Land
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP RE-FX, **Land** refers to an independent real estate object that represents a **plot of land** or **property**. It is part of the **architectural view** in the **dual-view model** of RE-FX and serves as the foundation for assigning buildings, rental objects, and contracts.

A Land object (also referred to as a “plot”) includes:

* **Object number (OBJNR)**: typically starts with “IG\*”
* **Address and geodata** (e.g., parcel number, cadastral information)
* **Assignment to a business entity**
* **Ownership details** (e.g., owner, usage rights)
* **Area specifications** (e.g., total area, built-up area)
* **Link to buildings and rental objects**
* **Valuation-relevant data** (e.g., land value, type of use)

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** PM\_MP
**Name (en):** Measuring Point
**General Description**
=======================

A means of measuring a parameter for an object. A measuring point describes the characteristics of a physical measuring device, for example, a thermometer on a machine.

Values measured by a measuring point can increase and decrease, whereas values on a counter either run forwards or backwards.

================================================================================

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| SIPT\_CM\_EXT\_REF | Long external References to Credit memo |  |  |
| SIPT\_CM\_REF | Credit Memo References as external/Non Signed invoice |  |  |
| SIPT\_GAPS\_BKPF | Digital Signature Portugal: Gaps for FI Documents |  |  |

| SIPT\_HIST\_MATNR | Signature PT: History of material descriprion changes |  |  |
| SIPT\_IF\_BKPF | Portugal: Digital Signature at Interface Acc. Documents |  |  |
| SIPT\_IF\_VBRK | Portugal: Digital Signature for Interface Billing Document |  |  |
| SIPT\_LAST\_KUNNR | Signature PT: Latest specific customer data for check |  |  |
| SIPT\_LAST\_MATNR | Signature PT: Latest material texts for check |  |  |
| SIPT\_NUMLOCK | Signature PT: Everlasting lock of corrupted number range |  |  |
| SIPT\_NUMST\_FI | Signature PT:  Starting point for FI documents |  |  |
| SIPT\_NUMST\_SD | Signature PT:  Billing Document Number From |  |  |
| SIPT\_PRINT\_CHK | Table for logging printing in FI/SD/FI-CA |  |  |
| SIPT\_REG | Digital Signature Portugal: Master Data Registration |  |  |
| SIPT\_REG\_CAP | Signature Portugal: Registration of DS Capabilities for App. |  |  |
| SIPT\_REG\_KUNNR | Digitial Signature Portugal: Customer Number Registration |  |  |
| SIPT\_REG\_MATNR | Digitial Signature Portugal: Material Number Registration |  |  |
| SIPT\_REG\_WERKS | Digital Signature Portugal: Plant Abroad Registration |  |  |
| SIPT\_VBRK | Portugal: Digital Signature for Billing Document |  |  |
| SIPT\_VBRK\_DATE | Signature PT:  Chronological Numbering, Last Billing Date |  |  |
| SIPT\_VER\_KUNNR | Signature PT: Versions of customer address data |  |  |
| SIPT\_VER\_MATNR | Signature PT: Versions of material description changes |  |  |
| WSPT\_CNTG\_LOG | Contingency Approval log table |  |  |
| WSPT\_LIKP | Documents For Portugal Webservice |  |  |
| WSPT\_LIKP\_REJ | Rejected documents for Portugal Webservice |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** LOC\_DISI\_PT  ~~SD\_SIPT~~
**Name (en):** Digital Signature for Portugal
**General Description**
=======================

According to legal requirements in Portugal, as of January 1, 2011, software that is used to create accounting, billing, and delivery documents must be able to digitally sign the data using an RSA algorithm.The digital signature solution for Portugal allows you to fulfill these legal requirements outlined in Portaria n.º 363/2010 de 23 de Junho). The solution runs automatically and digitally signs all your required documents without your active involvement.

This digital signature software is certified by the tax authorities in Portugal (AT). It uses private encryption keys to production systems, each system using its key to automatically digitally sign the following documents:

* Billing documents including invoices, proforma invoices, debit memos, and credit memos for a Portuguese sales organization
* Accounting documents including invoices, debit memos, and credit memos created manually for a Portuguese company code
* Delivery documents

The Portuguese tax authorities can then use SAP’s public key to check the authenticity of the digital signatures on your documents.

Note

**Note**

In Portugal, it is legally required that accounting, billing, and delivery documents are sequentially numbered with no gaps existing in the sequence. Where such number gaps exist, problems can be encountered with automatic generation of digital signatures. You can use digital signature apps to eliminate document numbering gaps and generate missing digital signatures.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** CO\_SET  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60SET)
**Name (en):** CO Sets
General Description
===================

Sets are flexible structures for organizing hierarchies and quantities. Values can be grouped or value intervals can be specified in one or more records in a hierarchy.

This is not included in our standard migration content. Talk to your project manager for specific scope description in order to include or exclude sets from data migration.

\* mapping of values in table SETLEAF

\* Mapping Object determination is dependend on SETLEAF-SETCLASS.

\* Values are only mapped if both are same.

\* It is not possible to guess - if values

\* differentiate - what value may be the right one

\* necessary additional tasks

\* - maintain /CBSGMBH/C\_MDD; add SETHEADER-TABNAME/FIELDNAME to

\* corresponding MO

\* - maintain /CBSGMBH/C\_MA; add references to main BO’s for

\* busines object CO\_SET for all used MO’s.

================================================================================

| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| COCC | PP-PI: Attributes for Characteristics | PP-PI Attribute zu Merkmalen |  |
| COCC2 | PP-PI: Customer-Specific Attributes for Characteristics | PP-PI Kundenspezifische Attribute zu Merkmalen |  |
| COCH | Process Management: Control Recipe Header | Batch Management - Steuerrezeptkopf |  |
| COCOA | Process Management: Operation Assignment Table | Batch Management - Operation Allocation Table |  |
| COFT | Process Management: Process Instructions in Control Recipe | Batch Management - Steuerrezept Prozeßvorgaben |  |
| COFV | Process Management: Process Instr. Charact. in Ctrl Recipe | Batch Management - Steuerrezept Vorgabemerkmale |  |
| COMD | Process Management: Error Log for Message Destination | Batch Manegement - Message Destination Errorlog |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_COCB  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60COCB)
**Name (en):** Orders Batch Management
**General Description**
=======================

Batch Management can be defined as tagging an alphanumeric or a numeric code to the material produced or material procured. Every batch number has an inventory quantity associated with it. This 10 digit code in SAP helps in uniquely identifying a material and the quantity associated with it.

If [Batch Management](https://help.sap.com/doc/9f3cbf53d25ab64ce10000000a174cb4/3.6/en-US/frameset.htm?d3feb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html "Go to document: Batch Management") is used, you can execute batch determination for picks in the Warehouse Management system (WMS) when creating transfer orders, thus optimizing your internal warehouse processes. The criteria the system uses to determine batches are relatively general (for example, a certain shelf life expiration date or a certain batch status).

If you want to take into account differentiated specifications during [batch determination](https://help.sap.com/doc/9f3cbf53d25ab64ce10000000a174cb4/3.6/en-US/frameset.htm?0dfeb753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html "Go to document: batch determination") , for example from the sales order, execute the charge determination at a lower level, for example when you create the outbound delivery or when you create a production or process order. In this case, you cannot execute a new batch determination in the WMS.

================================================================================

title: ACDOCA_CORR_D_DOC_S4_LDG
page_id: 257921446
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/257921446
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | S4 to S4 - ledger solution ACDOCA w/o BKPF |
| Name (de) | S4 to S4 - Ledgerlösung ACDOCA ohne BKPF |
| ET Business Object |  |
| S/4 Business Object | ACDOCA\_CORR\_D\_DOC\_S4\_LDG |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| ACDOCA | Universal Journal Entry Line Items |  |  |

**Name (en):** S4 to S4 - ledger solution ACDOCA w/o BKPF
**General Description**
=======================

Technical documents are created for systems that have been migrated to S/4. As these documents do not have a BKPF record, they must be selected separately in this BO  
   
The technical data records that are created during balance migration usually have the document number starting with DG\*\*, DC\*\*\* or DU\*\*.  
In addition

===> BSTAT is 'C' (balance carried forward and balance adjustments from migration).  
===> MIG\_SOURCE is the same:

'G'/'F'----For general ledger balance adjustment records  
'C'--------For CO balance adjustment records  
'N'--------For ML balance adjustment records  
'A' -------For AA balance adjustment records  
'U'--------For balance adjustment records from the general ledger that clear the balances from other modules.

Since data from the general ledger, CO, ML, AA, etc. is merged in the SAP S/4HANA system, the line items of each module are migrated individually, with the exception of the general ledger and CO, where the line items are merged where possible.  
The same applies if there is a difference between line items and the balance in CO, e.g. the same is written from technical documents in ACDOCA whose document number starts with 'DC\*\*' and mig\_source = 'C'. For the general ledger, the same data records are written with 'DG\*\*' (or DF\*\*) mig\_source = 'G' or 'F'.

These documents must also be duplicated accordingly for the ledgers in S4; this is done using the OIE /CBSGMBH/4S4S\_OIE\_ACDOCA\_COR

================================================================================

title: Foreign trade
page_id: 120095389
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120095389
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Foreign trade data |
| Name (de) | Foreign trade data |
| ET Business Object | MM\_FTDATA  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60FTDATA) |
| S/4 Business Object | S4 specific settings |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| EIKP | Foreign Trade: Export/Import Header Data | Außenhandel: Export/Import Kopfdaten | X |
| EIPO | Foreign Trade: Export/Import: Item Data | Außenhandel: Export/Import: Positionsdaten |  |
| EIEI | Foreign Trade: Export/Import Communication Data | Außenhandel: Export/Import Kommunikationsdaten |  |
| EIUV | Foreign Trade: Incompletion Log for Foreign Trade Data | Außenhandel: Unvollständigkeitsprotokoll Außenhandelsdaten |  |
| EIZO | FT: Exp/Imp ident. of goods, pres. of goods for cust.declar. | Außenhandel: Export/Import Nämlichkeiten, Gestellungen |  |

**ET Business Object:** MM\_FTDATA  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60FTDATA)
**Name (en):** Foreign trade data
**General Description**
=======================

The Foreign Trade component is integrated with Materials Management for handling imports and with Sales and Distribution for exports.

Foreign trade data appears at the header and in the items themselves in purchase orders and shipping notifications (inbound deliveries). Commodity codes and custom tarriff number are maintained in the material master.

**Comparison between foreign trade, international trade and GTS**

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| **Foreign Trade** | Foreign Trade is the SAP extension where products export and import relevant data is managed within the ECC system Foreign Trade is integrated within MM for imports and within SD for exports  Foreign trade allows:   * Use of all import and export processes and efficient integration into the logistics chain * Automatic verification of export licenses based on current regulations * Simplify reporting with automated procedures for creating, printing, and submitting reports * Identification of products for preference processing * Efficient handling of business processes through EDI communication * Foreign trade data can be called up in all relevant SAP components (before goods issue); even after posting the goods issue, data can still be changed in the invoice |
| **International Trade** | International Trade is the SAP extension where products export and import relevant data is managed within the S4 system  ITM covers basic requirements for export control, Intrastat reporting and product classification. It has some limitations in its functionality from an application point of view.  International Trade is integrated within MM for imports and within SD for exports  International trade allows:   * Intrastat declarations * Tariffing and classification * Legal control for purchase orders, orders and deliveries * Letter of credit (as part of the FI component) |
| **GTS** | Global Trade Services is a dedicated SAP that allows companies to support and define import and export trade processes in SAP ERP.  SAP GTS helps to automate and streamline trade processes so you can control costs, reduce the risk of penalties and fines, and clear customs faster  International trade allows:   * Intrastat declarations * Tariffing and classification * Legal control for purchase orders, orders and deliveries * Letter of credit (as part of the FI component) |

**Terminology**

| Commodity Code | Tariff Code | Control Class |
| --- | --- | --- |
| * It is a six-, eight-, or ten-digit numerical sequence which uniquely identifies and classify a trade product * It is export-relevant * The six-digit commodity codes are known as the [HS codes](https://dhl-freight-connections.com/en/logistics-dictionary/hs-code/). The codes are used internationally for monitoring trade volumes and applying international trade measures to goods.  The eight-digit commodity codes are used for export declarations and [Intrastat commodity codes](https://dhl-freight-connections.com/en/logistics-dictionary/intrastat-commodity-codes) declaration. The Intrastat commodity codes declaration is a system for collecting statistics on the trade in goods between the EU member states.  The ten-digit commodity codes are used for the importation of goods from outside the EU and they are needed for TARIC imports declaration. | * It is a nine-, eleven-, or thirteen-digit numerical sequence which uniquely identifies and classify a trade product * It is import-relevant * The first six digits of the customs tariff number are known as the HS code. | * It is a unique code for classifying goods and requires a license from an authority for controlling goods movement * It is used for special products like military equipment |

================================================================================
