title: Product group
page_id: 124947771
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124947771
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Product Group |
| Name (de) | Produktgruppe |
| ET Business Object | PP\_PRDGRP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60PRDGRP) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| PGMI | Product Group/Member Allocation | Zuordnung Produktgruppe / Mitglied |  |
| PGZU | Product Group/Member Quantity Conversions | Umrechnung Mengen Produktgruppe / Mitglied |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PRDGRP  (till ET 2021 FP 2 PP60PRDGRP)
**Name (en):** Product Group
**General Description**
=======================

Product group is a grouping of similar products. Product groups are defined for a product group type. The product group type specifies the criterion by which grouping takes place (for example, size). The product groups represent the instances of this grouping (for example, small, medium, large). A product group combines other product groups and material. Product groups can be multi-level or single-level.   
You plan product group data in standard SOP. The standard SOP is documented in [Sales and Operations Planning (SOP)](https://help.sap.com/viewer/a5822ccc7c174662b87423ce5f63b778/6.17.17/en-US/af6cbd534f22b44ce10000000a174cb4.html). 

The BO PP\_PRDGRP contains the tables needed to migrate Product Group Data. There are only 2 tables, one Header, with a child table linked by its Table Key Relationship (TKR). This BO has no dependency to any other PP Business Object.

================================================================================

| PMSDO | PM organizational data for SD documents | PM Organisationsdaten für SD-Belege |  |

**ET Business Object:** CS\_ORDER
**Name (en):** CS Service Order
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the service orders (CS) and the maintenance orders (PM) , see table T350-SERVICE. . A service order is a short-term agreement between the service provider and the service recipient, in which one-time services are ordered by the service recipient and resource-related billing performed upon completion. Maintenance orders are cost collectors in PM and any maintenance cannot be done without it. Orders store the actual time spent on maintenance tasks and the consumption of spare parts.

================================================================================

| JCDS | Change Documents for System/User Statuses (Table JEST) | Änderungsbelege für System-/Anwenderstatus (Tabelle JEST) |  |
| JEST | Individual Object Status | Einzelstatus pro Objekt |  |
| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| MCIPMIS | PMIS: Master data characteristics for PMIS before image | PMIS: Stammdatenmerkmale für PMIS before image |  |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |
| **QMEL** | **Quality Notification** | **Qualitätsmeldunq** | X |
| QMFE | Quality notification - items | Qualitaetsmeldung - Positionen |  |
| QMIH | Quality message - maintenance data excerpt | Qualitaetsmeldung - Abschnitt Instandhaltungsdaten |  |
| QMMA | Quality notification - activities | Qualitaetsmeldung - Aktionen |  |
| QMSM | Quality notification - tasks | Qualitaetsmeldung - Maßnahmen |  |
| QMUR | Quality notification - causes | Qualitaetsmeldung - Ursachen |  |
| QNAOD\_AOBJECT | Assigned Objects for Notification Item | Zugeordnete Objekte zur Meldungsposition |  |

**ET Business Object:** PS\_CLAIMS  (till ET 2025 FP 0 )
**Name (en):** PS Claims
**General Description**
=======================

Means with which customer notifications are created and managed in the areas of Plant Maintenance and Customer Service. The following notification types are predefined in the standard system:

* Customer notification

  Informing a customer of a malfunction or damage that occurs
* Service request

  Request for service to be performed
* Activity report

  Documentation of activities that have been performed

In addition to these standard notification types, you can also define your own user-specific notification types.

If a customer device has to be repaired or maintained, the contact person on site at the customer can enter a service notification directly in the SAP system, or inform the customer service department of the problem by telephone, fax or mail. For the latter, the customer service employee for the device vendor or manufacturer enters the notification in the system.

A service notification consists of a notification header and one or more items.

Notification header data is information that is used to identify and manage the notification. It is valid for the whole notification.

Depending on the notification type, the notification items contain data describing in greater detail the problem or malfunction that occurred, or the activity that was performed.

check other BO

\* For QM notification type (02) use BO QM\_NOTIFI "TA QM03

\* For PM notification type (01,05) use BO PM\_NOTIFI "TA IW23/IQS3

\* For PS claims/notification type (04) use BO PS\_CLAIM" TA CLM3

\* For CS notifications (03) use BO CS\_NOTIFI  "TA IW53

================================================================================

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

| Name (de) | Business Partner on the fly für Kontaktpersonen zu Lieferanten ( OIE need, align with DEV ) |
| ET Business Object | MD2BP\_CP\_VEND |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | n/a |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | MD2BP\_CP\_VEND |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** MD2BP\_CP\_VEND
**Name (en):** CP BP for Vendor on the fly ( OIE need, align with DEV )
**General Description**
=======================

This BO uses KNVK, LFA1 and the associated address data in tables ADRP, ADCP, ADRC, ADR3, ADR2, ADR5 and ADR6 to create

* the contact person business partner   
  (BUT000-TYPE is set to '1' for Person; role in BUT100-RLTYP is set to default 'BUP001' for Contact Person)
* the relationship between Supplier Businesspartner and Contact Person Businesspartner that also leads to the address of the company  
  (BUT050, BUT051, BUT052)
* the personal address of the Contact Person Businesspartner  
  (BUT020, BUT021\_FS, ADRP, ADCP, ADRC, ADR3, ADR2, ADR5 and ADR6)

For creation of Contact Person Businesspartners, you need to add an OIE:

We currently have an OIE called **ZCBS\_OIE\_CP\_OTF\_NEW** in the S4X(900), which can be used under the following restrictions:

* S/4-Supplier and BP must have the same number
* Because the OIE uses a Bapi as basis, the data is checked against customizing and against the supplier business partner. If there are any inconsistencies, the creation of the Contact Person Businesspartner is aborted. Same happens if the interfaces for the Bapi are incorrect.

If the number of BUT000-PARTNER was not determined by NGN when the supplier business partner was created, but was set manually (e.g. because equality between LFA1-LIFNR and BUT000-PARTNER was desired), the OIE **ZCBS\_OIE\_BP\_PERSON\_OIE** must be used to create the contact person business partner. This OIE does not check the partner number of the vendor business partner against the NRIV customizing as OIE **ZCBS\_OIE\_CP\_OTF\_NEW** does.

================================================================================

space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307368115
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Cost Elements / Cost Element B-Segment |
| Name (de) | CO Cost Element CSKA - Chart of Accounts / CO Cost Element CSKB - Controlling Area |
| ET Business Object | CO\_CECSKA  CO\_CECSKB |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | [CO\_COSTELEM\_PRIM\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/140794949/CO+-+Cost+Elements+-+S+4+specific+handling)  [CO\_COSTELEM\_SEC\_SDT](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/140794949/CO+-+Cost+Elements+-+S+4+specific+handling) |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | N/A |
| IDOC Business Object | [CO60COELEM\_IDOC COELEM](https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/spaces/CCETSLT/pages/191171165/CO60COELEM_IDOC+COELEM) |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CSKA | Cost Elements (Data Dependent on Chart of Accounts) | Kostenarten (Kontenplanabhängige Daten) |  |
| CSKU | Cost Element Texts | Kostenartentexte | CSKA |
| CSKB | Cost Elements (Data Dependent on Controlling Area) | Kostenarten (Kostenrechnungskreisabhängige Daten) | CSKA |
| SKA1 | G/L Account Master (Chart of Accounts) |  | CSKA |
| SKB1 | G/L account master (company code) |  | SKA1 |
| SKAT | G/L Account Master Record (Chart of Accounts: Description) |  | CSKA |
| SKAS | G/L account master (chart of accounts: key word list) |  | CSKA |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_CECSKA  CO\_CECSKB
**Name (en):** Cost Elements / Cost Element B-Segment
**General Description**
=======================

The cost components of the product cost elements determine how the costed material is valuated.

For each cost element, you specify whether the assigned costs are to be included in further valuations.

Primary costs are, for example material costs or costs of external procurement. In the financial accounting, the primary costs are taken directly from the expense accounts of the income statement.

Secondary costs have no equivalent in financial accounting. The costs are determined via internal activity allocations and assigned to the cost centers. An example of this is the use of internal services.

The cost category is divided into two business objects, because CSKA is dependent on the chart of accounts, but CSKB is dependent on the cost accounting key.

In SAP S/4HANA, the distinction between cost elements and G/L accounts is blurred. Both are now maintained within the G/L account master data, and cost elements are essentially a special type of G/L account.

================================================================================

**Name (en):** Electronic Bank Statement
**General Description**
=======================

With S/4HANA On Premise 2023 (OP2023), a string-based persistence for memo lines of bank statements and lockbox files has been developed. The long-term goal is to replace the database tables FEBRE and FEBRE\_ORIG with the table FEB\_MEMOLINE. To achieve that, OP2023 introduces the database table FEB\_MEMOLINE as an additional persistence, which is kept in sync with the classic tables. If active (by customizing), this database table is used by CDS views for read accesses of the memo lines.

The OIE will create these table

---
title: Treasury and Risk Management (TRM)
page_id: 124953028
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/124953028
---
**Overview**
============

**General remarks on classification/configuration:**

Complexity can be arbitrarily high: NEVER offer at a fixed price without having carried out a thorough analysis and defined the target condition!

The treasury module can be roughly divided into the following parts:

* **Transaction Manager for financial transactions:**
* **Analyzer**
* **Business partners and other master data (currency and security rates, swap rates, interest rates, etc.)**

The migration effort strongly depends on which sub-modules are in use, i.e. which financial transactions are used and whether the analyzer functionality is in use.

**Worth knowing:**

* Treasury is an FI subledger, comparable to the asset subledger. You can use this to represent financial transactions such as stock or option purchases or money market transactions (entry, valuation). It is not absolutely necessary to post from the subledger to the general ledger. Transactions are created for each company code, the unique transaction number can be found in table VTBFHA in field RFHA (important key), but a quasi-object number is often used at table level (BUSTRANSID).
* Since option transactions can also be managed, the transactions often refer to each other. This is the case when an option is exercised and followed by a security transaction (keyword Underlying Transaction, caution during selection!).
* To create a treasury transaction, a business partner (bank, country, company, and so on) is required as a counterparty. These are managed using transaction BP and must be created with role TR0151. It is therefore necessary to migrate the Business Partner tables (BO CA60BP).
* How market data is handled must be decided individually. Especially if a market data interface is used, the corresponding market data tables (for example, TCURR) are under enormously large.
* The valuation of financial transactions may take place in different valuation areas (keyword: parallel valuation).

**Notable transaction:**

FTR\_EDIT

In S4 there is one change to "Money Marker: Product Types":

* Cash Flow Generation config needs to be filled.

**Master Data: Transactions**
-----------------------------

* Transaction FTR\_CREATE creates a deal
* FTR\_00 diplays all deals:   Product type

Deals can be frames: which means they save the conditions but have no postings, product type 56A has subordinate deals.

Payment details are pulled from BP master data.

    Status / activity 1- contract means no postings are allwoed yet. Transaction FTR\_EDIT with settle:  not to be confused with the CO settlement. Settle in TR only means the transaction is open for postings.

Cash flow: the values that later will be posted to the general ledger with Transaction TPM44.

This gives us the posting protocol:

To have a look at the already posted values from the deals use transaction TPM20. This overview also gives us the FI document to jump to transaction FB03.

Transaction TPM13 show us all transactional data in the subledger TR: Treasury position flows

S4 specific

Different beviour within SAP Coding see   
<https://me.sap.com/notes/0001617638>

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the inspection characteristics.

Inspection characteristics are used to describe the inspection criteria for materials, parts and products. They can be created at plant level to simplify and standardize the inspection planning activities and to standardize the nomenclature. This allows to plan inspections systematically, uniformly and economically.

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| LRF\_WKQU | Assignment User ->Queue | Zuordnung Benutzer ->Queue |  |
| NKLV | Number ranges in WM | Nummernkreise im LVS |  |
| T346X | Exceptions: Queue Forwarding | Ausnahmefälle: Queue-Forwarding |  |

**ET Business Object:** WM\_USRCUST  (till ET 2021 FP 2 WM60USRCUST)
**Name (en):** WM User and Customizing
**General Description**
=======================

'User' is assigned with specific system logon name to process 'Queue'.

A queue is a logical file to which transfer orders (TOs) to process are assigned. These transfer orders are assigned to queues according to defined physical areas in the warehouse, for e.g.: picking area for putaway activities.

The TOs are processed by users equipped with radio frequency (RF) terminals.

Each user can be assigned to only one queue in a warehouse.

================================================================================

page_id: 217851144
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/217851144
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Planned independent requirement |
| Name (de) | Planprimärbedarf |
| ET Business Object | PP\_PLINRE |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| PBED | Independent Requirements Data | Primaerbedarfsdaten |  |
| PBHI | Independent Requirements History | Primaerbedarfshistorie |  |
| PBIC | Ind. reqmts index for customer requirements (without RV) | Primaerbedarfsindex fuer Kundenbedarf ( ohne RV ) |  |
| PBID | Planned Independent Requirements Index: MRP Area | Planprimärbedarfsindex: Dispobereich |  |
| PBIM | Independent Requirements for Material | Primaerbedarfsindex fuer Material |  |
| PBIV | Ind. reqmts index for consump. of exter. non-variable parts | Primaerbedarfsindex fuer Verrechnung fremde Gleichteile |  |
| T459U | Settings for Maintaining Planned Independent Requirements | Einstellungen für Primärbedarfspflege |  |
| TPHVP | Header: Planning Profile | Kopf  Vorplanungsprofil |  |
| TPLVP | Planning Profile | Vorplanungsprofil |  |
| TPSVP | Planning profile lines | Vorplanungsprofilzeile |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PLINRE
**Name (en):** Planned independent requirement
**General Description**
=======================

A Planned Independent Requirement (DE Planprimärbedarf oder auch Vorplanungsbedarfe genannt) is a planned requirement for a material and plant without reference to a sales order. It contains a time series with the planned material requirements (Planned Independent Requirement items) and also identifying and administrative information.

PIR's can be a result from sales and operations planning (SOP), from other SAP planning solutions (for example from SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain, a cloud-based planning software for supply chain management) or it is also possible to create and maintain PIR's manually (MD61/MD62/MD63).

Create PIR

MRP-Run

MD04 (Stock/Requiremets List)

================================================================================

| BSES | Document Control Data (Obsolete) | Beleg Steuerdaten (obsolet don't use) | BSEG |
| BSET | Tax Data Document Segment | Belegsegment Steuerdaten | BKPF |

| BSIW | Index table for customer bills of exchange used | Indextabelle für verwendetete Debitoren-Wechsel | BKPF |
| BSIX | Index table for customer bills of exchange used | Indextabelle für verwendete Debitoren-Wechsel | BKPF |
| BSPL | Split Posting Procedures | Gesplittete Buchungsvorgänge | BKPF |
| BVOR | Intercompany posting procedures | Buchungskreisübergreifende Buchungsvorgänge | BKPF |
| DEFTAX\_ITEM | Data for Deferred Taxes | Daten zu gestundeten Steuern | BSEG |
| FAGLFLEXA | General Ledger: Actual Line Items | Hauptbuch: Ist-Einzelposten | BKPF |
| FAGL\_SPLINFO | Splittling Information of Open Items | Aufteilungsinformation der OP's | BKPF |
| FAGL\_SPLINFO\_VAL | Splitting Information of Open Item Values | Aufteilungsinformation der OP's Werte | BKPF |
| RF048 | Subseq.BA/PC Adjustmnt: Index/Distributn Vector (Calculate) | FI-Nachbelastung Bilanz: Index/Verteilungsvektor (Berechnen) | BKPF |
| TSTOR | Accounting Documents with Reverse Posting Date | Buchhaltungsbelege mit Datum der Umkehrbuchung | BKPF |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_FAGL
**Name (en):** Accounting documents (new GL)
**General Description**
=======================

Like business object FI\_DOC (for source systems with classic GL), FI\_FAGL includes tables, which should be split into separate BOs for most projects. The reason the BO is delivered as it is, is to make sure no important tables are forgotten, while keeping LU ONE from becoming bloated. Please check which tables are necessary for your project.

For most projects, it makes sense to migrate the actual documents (BKPF / BSEG) separately from the index tables. Depending on data volume and system performance, the index tables should be split into multiple BOs, if needed.

================================================================================

---
title: Restart of cancelled jobs in jobnet during import
page_id: 193097970
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/193097970
---
***Overview***
==============

During an import of data some jobs can get cancelled during the runtime. In some cases (like the import of ACDOCA/ML), it can be necessary to restart the job at the respective breaking point. In this article a short work introduction will be given in how to restart such jobs.

**Attention**: before restarting the cancelled jobs, it is necessary to analyze the reason for the cancellation and solve the issue (e.g. reduce the package size for import). Otherwise the jobs could get cancelled again.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** MM\_LAYER  (till ET 2021 FP 2 MM60LAYER)
**Name (en):** MM - LIFO Material Layer (Sel. MYKEY = idR. WERKS)
**General Description**
=======================

LIFO valuation allows you to separately valuate the increase in stock of a material for various settlement periods (fiscal year or month). You can use various procedures to valuate the increase for each settlement period, for example, the average delivered prices. 

Source figure: <https://help.sap.com/doc/8578b6535fe6b74ce10000000a174cb4/3.6/en-US/b65fb6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html>

================================================================================

title: SD_VFKK - Shipment costs
page_id: 82808398

---
Tables
======

The archiving object SD\_VFKK writes data from the following tables to archive files during archiving sessions. During the subsequent deletion run, the archived data is deleted from these database tables. Database tables from which only archived data is archived but not deleted are not included in this grouping.

| VFKGRP | (Freight) Group Conditions |
| VFKK | Shipment Costs: Header Data |

| VFKONV | (Freight) Conditions |
| VFKP | Shipment Costs: Item Data |
| VFPA | Partner for Shipment Costs |
| VFSI | Shipment Costs: Sub-item Data |
| VFZP | Correct Original Assignmen of Conditions |

**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| DFKKCRH | Creditworthiness Record: Total of one Year | Bonitätssatz: Summen eines Jahres |  |
| DFKKCRP | Creditworthiness Record: Generating Actions | Bonitätssatz: Erzeugende Aktionen |  |

**Name (en):** FI-CA creditworthiness record
**General Description**
=======================

The creditworthiness of a business partner provides information on the business partner‘s payment history and influences the selection of activities for dunning and/or returns and also the calculation of charges.   
Various different business transactions, such as returns, dunning notices, and write-offs update the creditworthiness in the system automatically. Creditworthiness record can be transferred from external systems or manually.

================================================================================

---
title: FSCM TRM Master Agreement
page_id: 307365783
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/307365783
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | MD FSCM TRM Master Agreements |
| Name (de) | MD FSCM TRM Master Agreements |
| ET Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VTBMA |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VTBMA |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VTBMA |
| IDOC Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VTBMA | Master Agreement | Rahmenvertrag | X |
| VTBMACR | M. Agreement: Permitted Currencies | Rahmenvertrag: Zulässige Währungen |  |
| VTBMADT | Mast. Agreement: Permitted Trans. Types | Rahmenvertrag: Zulässige Geschäftsarten |  |
| VTBMALE | Master Agreement: Permitted Co. Codes | Rahmenvertrag: Zulässige Buchungskreise |  |
| VTBMAPA | Master Agreement: Permitted Bus. Partners | Rahmenvertrag: Zulässige Geschäftspartner |  |

**ET Business Object:** MD\_FSCM\_TRM\_VTBMA
**Name (en):** MD FSCM TRM Master Agreements
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP's Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) module, a master agreement is a contract that defines the general terms and conditions for a specific type of transaction, such as money market transactions or other financial agreements. It essentially provides the framework within which individual transactions are conducted, ensuring consistency and compliance with the organization's standards. Master agreements can cover various aspects like pricing, interest rates, settlement methods, and risk management policies. They ensure that individual transactions adhere to the agreed-upon standards, simplifying the process and reducing the risk of errors. 

The FSCM TRM Master Agreement business object is used to transfer the corresponding data.

================================================================================

==========

All Tables

| FOTRFDITM | VAT Refund Items | Vorsteuervergütungs Posten |  |
| FOTRFDITMCHG | Change Documents, VAT Refund Item | Änderungsbelege Vorsteurvergütungs Item |  |

| FOTTDCLXML | Country-Specific ATR Reporting Data (XML Format) | landesspezifisch UVA Meldedaten (XML Format) |  |
| FOTTPRTXML | Country-Specific ATR Log Data (XML Format) | landesspezifisch UVA Protokolldaten (XML Format) |  |

**ET Business Object:** FI\_FOT
**Name (en):** Electronic Tax Statement
**General Description**
=======================

You can use the Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases (RFUMSV00) program to create tax reports (tax books) according to VAT types. It enables you to maintain your VAT registers per VAT types. You can create separate input and output tax reports about your sales/purchases activities in the country, in the European Union, or outside the European Union according to VAT types. You are required to store the tax reports internally for audit purposes. You submit the statement on a monthly basis.

================================================================================

---
title: Scenario for long-term planning
page_id: 217851156
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/217851156
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Scenario for long-term planning |
| Name (de) | Planungsszenarien der Langfristplanung |
| ET Business Object | PP\_PLSCN |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| MDDMAP | Mapping Table for External User for MRP Evaluations | Mapping-Tabelle externer User für Disposition-Auswertungen |  |
| MDDMAPT | Mapping Table for External User/Vendor with Default Setting | Mapping-Tabelle externer User-Lieferant mit Voreinstellung |  |
| MDFD | MRP firming date | Fixierungsdaten der Bedarfsplanung |  |
| MDFDDB | Firming Data of MRP - MRP Area | Fixierungsdaten der Bedarfsplanung - Dispobereich |  |
| MDKP | Header Data for MRP Document | Kopfdaten Dispositionsbeleg |  |
| MDKPDB | Header Data for MRP Document | Kopfdaten Dispositionsbeleg |  |
| MDVL | Planning file entry for long-term planning | Planungsvormerkung Langfristplanung |  |
| P44V | Planning Versions | Planung: Versionen |  |
| PLPB | Long-term planning: Independent requirements versions | Langfristplanung: Planprimärbedarfsversionen |  |
| PLPT | Long-term planning: Plants of planning scenario | Langfristplanung: Werke zum Planungsszenario |  |
| PLSC | Planning scenarios in long-term planning | Planungsszenarien der Langfristplanung |  |
| TCX0L | Scheduling long term planning: Levels and control parameters | Terminierung Langfristplanung: Ebenen und Steuerungspar. |  |

**ET Business Object:** PP\_PLSCN
**Name (en):** Scenario for long-term planning
**General Description**
=======================

Long-term planning (LTP) is used to **simulate** various business scenarios to help in production and procurement planning by using existing master data and other information. Companies can use this tool to test various hypothetical assumptions affecting business decisions. When compared with material requirements planning (MRP), **LTP doesn’t affect the database of results that was created while running normal (operative) MRP**. However, you can use simulation mode in operational MRP to get similar results as in LTP.

However, if the results from simulative planning of LTP are satisfactory, **you can also transfer them to operative planning in standard MRP**. While it’s easy to set up LTP, it’s equally easy to delete the planning scenarios when you no longer need them.

General business procedure:

1. Enter the PIR in a simulative version (TA MD61 with version "02").
2. Create planning scenario (TA MS31)
3. Run LTP (simulative MRP), and save the results (TA MS02/MS03).
4. Evaluate the LTP stock/requirements list (TA MS04).

================================================================================

title: FSCM TRM Rule management

**General Description**
=======================

FTR rule management is migrated via this business object. The corresponding data is maintained in the Rules tab (transaction FTR\_CREATE or FTR\_EDIT) when a financial transaction is created.

This tab is only visible for the Financial instrument, for example Facility, if it has been set up in Customizing.

---
title: SD Scheduling Agreement
page_id: 120127437
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120127437
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | SD Scheduling Agreement |
| Name (de) | SD Lieferpläne |
| ET Business Object | SD\_SCHEAG  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60SCHEAG) |
| S/4 Business Object | ~~SDS4SCHEAG~~ SD\_SCHEAG\_SDT |
**General Description**
=======================

A customer scheduling agreement is an outline agreement with the customer containing delivery quantities and dates. These are then entered as schedule lines in a delivery schedule. You can either create schedule lines when you create the scheduling agreement or you can create them later.

You fulfill a scheduling agreement by creating the deliveries in the schedule as they become due. You process deliveries for a scheduling agreement in exactly the same way as you process a normal delivery. After you have carried out the delivery, the system updates the Delivered quantity field in the scheduling agreement item with the delivery quantity. The following graphic shows the document flow for scheduling agreements.
**Tables**
==========

**ET Business Object:** SD\_SCHEAG  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60SCHEAG)
**Name (en):** SD Scheduling Agreement

================================================================================

All Tables

|  |  |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TYC
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Markt Data
**General Description**
=======================

As part of the migration of the FSCM TRM Market Data business object, Treasury and Risk Management market data is migrated.

Basis spreads are, for example, premiums or discounts on one side of a basis swap that make the swap a fair transaction. The spread refers to the first of the two assigned reference interest rates.

It is possible to define tenor spreads and currency spreads. The definition of basis spreads (transaction**RMBSM)** is a prerequisite for the following activities:

* Upload basis spread market data to the system
* Build basis spread curves from basis spreads with different maturities

Credit spreads are also part of this data and can be maintained via transaction **RMCSM.**

================================================================================

=======================

| JSTO | Status object information | Informationen zum Statusobjekt |  |
| MCIPMIS | PMIS: Master data characteristics for PMIS before image | PMIS: Stammdatenmerkmale für PMIS before image |  |
| ONR00 | General Object Number | Allgemeine Objektnummer |  |
| **QMEL** | **Quality Notification** | **Qualitätsmeldunq** | X |
| QMFE | Quality notification - items | Qualitaetsmeldung - Positionen |  |
| QMIH | Quality message - maintenance data excerpt | Qualitaetsmeldung - Abschnitt Instandhaltungsdaten |  |
| QMMA | Quality notification - activities | Qualitaetsmeldung - Aktionen |  |
| QMSM | Quality notification - tasks | Qualitaetsmeldung - Maßnahmen |  |
| QMUR | Quality notification - causes | Qualitaetsmeldung - Ursachen |  |
| QNAOD\_AOBJECT | Assigned Objects for Notification Item | Zugeordnete Objekte zur Meldungsposition |  |

**ET Business Object:** PM\_NOTIFI  (till ET 2025 FP 0 )
**Name (en):** PM Notification
**General Description**
=======================

Means with which customer notifications are created and managed in the areas of Plant Maintenance and Customer Service. The following notification types are predefined in the standard system:

* Customer notification

  Informing a customer of a malfunction or damage that occurs
* Service request

  Request for service to be performed
* Activity report

  Documentation of activities that have been performed

In addition to these standard notification types, you can also define your own user-specific notification types.

If a customer device has to be repaired or maintained, the contact person on site at the customer can enter a service notification directly in the SAP system, or inform the customer service department of the problem by telephone, fax or mail. For the latter, the customer service employee for the device vendor or manufacturer enters the notification in the system.

A service notification consists of a notification header and one or more items.

Notification header data is information that is used to identify and manage the notification. It is valid for the whole notification.

Depending on the notification type, the notification items contain data describing in greater detail the problem or malfunction that occurred, or the activity that was performed.

check other BO

Please always clarify the relevant notification types with the customer.

\* For QM notification type (02) use BO  QM\_NOTIFI "TA QM03

\* For PM notification type (01,05) use BO PM\_NOTIFI "TA IW23/IQS3

\* For PS claims/notification type (04) use BO PS\_CLAIM" TA CLM3

\* For CS notifications (03) use BO  CS\_NOTIFI  "TA IW53

================================================================================

==========

All Tables

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| AEOI | ECH: Object Management Records for Change Master | Änderungsdienst: Objektverwaltungssätze zu Änderungsstamm |  |
| AEDT | Engineering Change Management: Alternative Dates | Änderungsdienst : Datumselemente |  |

| AEEF | ECM: effectivity | ECM: Gültigkeit |  |
| AEPF | ECM: effectivity | ECM: Gültigkeit |  |
| AEPLST | Identical object in explosion (item) | Homogenitätsobjekt einer Auflösung ( Position ) |  |
| AEPREC | Identical object in explosion (header) | Homogenitätsobjekt einer Auflösung ( Kopf ) |  |
| TECN | Effectivity object: change numbers | Gültigkeitsobjekt: Änderungsnummern |  |
| TECS | Master Effectivity Object | Stamm Gültigkeitsobjekt |  |
| TECT | Description of Effectivity Object | Texte für Gültigkeitsobjekt |  |
| EDGE | Relation Between Change Numbers | Relationen zwischen Änderungsnummern |  |
| ENODE | Nodes ECH | Knoten ÄRG |  |

**General Description**

This BO contains the required tables to migrate change master data. The change master can be used to change various aspects of production basic data (for example, BOMs, task lists, materials, and documents) with history (with date effectivity) or depending on specific conditions (with parameter effectivity).

The structure is comprised of a header table, plus several child tables linked underneath by their respective Table Key Relationships (TKR).

This BO acts as a super BO to several other BOs.

**ET Business Object:** PLM\_CHGNUM  PLM\_CHGNUM\_AEOI
**Name (en):** Change Number (ECM)

==========

| CUVCPR\_ID\_INT | Conversion: internal ID for configuration profile | Umschlüsselung: interne ID für Konfigurationsprofile |  |

**ET Business Object:** VC\_CONFPROF
**Name (en):** Configuration Profile
**General Description**
=======================

Configuration profiles can be maintained for configurable objects to define central settings for configuring the object.

You can create several configuration profiles with different settings for an object. If an object has several configuration profiles, you must select a profile during configuration.

You can maintain configuration profiles for all configurable objects:

* Configurable materials (Configuration profiles are prerequisites for configuring a material using Variant Configuration)
* Configurable standard networks
* Configurable general maintenance task lists
* Configurable model service specifications

================================================================================

page_id: 120127461

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |

| Name (de) | SD Merkmalsschnellerfassung in Verkaufsbelegen |
| ET Business Object | SD\_VBMUE  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60VBMUE) |
| S/4 Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE |
| VBMUE | Sales Document: Characteristic Overview | Verkaufsbeleg: Merkmalsübersicht |
| VBMUET | Sales Document: Characteristic Overview Description | Verkaufsbeleg: Merkmalsübersicht Bezeichnung |
| VBMUEZ | Sales Document: Characteristic Overview Assignments | Verkaufsbeleg: Merkmalsübersicht Zuordnungen |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_VBMUE  (till ET 2021 FP2 SD60VBMUE)
**Name (en):** SD Fast Entry of Characteristics in Sales Documents
**General Description**
=======================

In the past you always had to go the characteristic value assignment screen for each item in order to assign values to the characteristics of materials when entering a sales document with several configurable materials.

Using fast entry of characteristics you can enter not only item data such as material and quantity in a screen but also value assignments for up to ten characteristics. This greatly reduces entry effort, particularly for materials with few characteristics and for preconfigured materials.

================================================================================

==========

All Tables

| Table | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Master table |
| CDHDR | Change document header | Änderungsbelegkopf | X |
| CDPOS | Change document items | Änderungsbelegpositionen |  |
| CDPOS\_UID | Additional Table for Inclusion of TABKEY>70 Characters | Zusatztabelle zur Aufnahme des TABKEYs>70 Zeichen |  |

**ET Business Object:** CA\_CDOC\_CA CA\_CDOC\_FI-CO CA\_CDOC\_MM CA\_CDOC\_PP-CS CA\_CDOC\_QM-PS CA\_CDOC\_SD  new with ET 2023 FP2  CA\_PCDOC  ~~obsolete CA\_CDOC~~
**Name (en):** Change documents CA Change documents FI / CO Change documents MM Change documents PP / CS Change documents QM / PS Change documents SD  Planned change documents
**General Description**
=======================

Logging changes to a business object as change document, that object must have been defined as a change document object in the system. In its definition, a change document object combines tables that represent a business object in the system. In the definition it can also be determined whether the individual fields should be documented in the case of deletions. A change document object represents an object class in CDHDR- OBJECTCLAS. 

 To improve performance and identification, Business Objects for change documents have been splitted

================================================================================

title: CO - Table COKEY
page_id: 117445311
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445311
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | table cokey |
| Name (de) | table cokey |
| ET Business Object | CO\_COKEY  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COKEY) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_COKEY  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COKEY) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| COKEY | CO key sub-numbers | CO-Key Subnummern |  |
| COKEY2 | CO Key Subnumbers Enhancement Table | CO-Key Subnummern Erweiterungstabelle | COKEY |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_COKEY  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60COKEY)
**Name (en):** table cokey
General Description
===================

The table cokey is a standard sap table used to store controlling key entrepreneur data and is available within R/3 SAP systems depending on version and release level.

The field HRKFT in the tables COEP, the summary record tables COS\* and in the AUAS refers   
to the COKEY2 table via the COKEY table and there it is checked whether the segments from the   
COKEY2 table correspond with those from the object numbers.  
If, in the cost object (sales orders, WBS elements, orders, cost centers, reconciliation object)   
the profit center is changed and consequently also the segment assignment, then in COEP, COS\* and   
AUAS, the HRKFT field must be changed so that the segments from COKEY2 match those of the cost objects from the account assignment.   
In COEP, the totals record tables and AUAS, the profit center is not changed over,   
because the profit center is not contained in these tables, but in these tables there is the field   
CO subkey (HRKFT) which refers to the segment.  
Below is a description of the case where the segment is changed due to the change in the profit center.

================================================================================

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TRCORT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRM Deals
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP Treasury, a "deal" is a term used to represent a financial transaction or agreement. It's a generic term used to describe various types of transactions, such as buying or selling securities, managing cash, or undertaking other financial activities. "Deals" are not only managed within SAP Treasury but are also integrated with other SAP modules. This integration enables the system to track the financial effects of the deal, for example, by influencing the balance sheet and cash flow.

If you create new TKRs for these tables during a project, please submit a content ticket, so that we can change this in our LU ONE. (This does not require a lot of effort - a screenshot of table C\_BOD for your BO is enough!)

================================================================================

=======================

The product cost collector (abbreviation: PCC) is used as a cost object in the product cost by period scenario.

A product cost collector collects the actual costs incurred in each period to produce a material. In the process, the product becomes the main cost.

**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| TRFT\_BUSTRANS | Treasury: TRF Business Transaction | Treasury: TRF-Geschäftsvorfall |  |
| TRFT\_CALC\_POS | Treasury: TRF Calculation Position | Treasury: TRF-Berechnungsbestand |  |
| TRFT\_CLASS\_POS | Treasury: TRF Class Position | Treasury: TRF-Gattungsbestand |  |
| TRFT\_FLOW | Treasury: Table with TRF Flows | Treasury: Tabelle mit TRF-Bewegungen |  |
| TRFT\_MAN\_POST | Manual Posting | Manuelle Buchung |  |
| TRFT\_MAN\_PT\_FLOW | TRF: Flows for Manual Posting | TRF: Bewegungen für manuelle Buchung |  |
| TRFT\_MATCH\_FLOW | Treasury: Table with Matching Process Flows | Treasury: Tabelle mit Abgleichsprozessbewegungen |  |
| TRFT\_MATCH\_TRANS | Treasury: Matching Process Business Transaction | Treasury: Abgleichsprozessgeschäftsvorfall |  |
| TRFT\_PACC\_BANK | Payment Information | Zahlungsinformation |  |
| TRFT\_POS\_ACCOUNT | Treasury: Futures Account Master Data | Treasury: Positionskontostammdaten |  |
| TRFT\_SUBTOTAL | Treasury: Table with TRF Transaction Figure | Treasury: Tabelle mit TRF-Verkehrszahlen |  |
| TRFT\_SUBTRANS | Treasury: TRF Business Subtransaction | Treasury: TRF-Teilgeschäftsvorfall |  |

**ET Business Object:** FSCM\_TRM\_TRFT
**Name (en):** FSCM TRFT Transaction Data
**General Description**
=======================

In SAP Treasury, listed options and futures are standardized derivative contracts traded on exchanges, distinct from over-the-counter (OTC) derivatives. Listed options grant the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy or sell an underlying asset at a specific price by a certain date. Futures contracts obligate the holder to buy or sell the underlying asset at a predetermined price on a future date. If you want to buy a listed option or a future, you need to have created them in the system as a class (transaction FWZZ), as well as having created a futures account (transaction TPM4). A futures account is a management unit that allows you to manage your positions. The futures accounts created in the system usually correspond to actual futures accounts.

Transaction data created in connection with listed options and futures is migrated using the business object FSCM\_TRM\_TRFT.

================================================================================

title: LOC Digital Signature for Spain
page_id: 295231208
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/295231208
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Digital Signature for Spain |
| Name (ES) | [Suministro Inmediato de Información del IVA](https://sede.agenciatributaria.gob.es/Sede/iva/suministro-inmediato-informacion.html) |
| ET Business Object | LOC\_DISI\_ES |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | LOC\_DISI\_ES |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | LOC\_DISI\_ES |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| EDOESSII\_BUN | eDocument Spain SII List |  |  |
| EDOESSII\_ICP | eDocument Spain SII Single Document for Incoming Cash |  |  |
| EDOESSII\_SDOC | eDocument Spain SII Single Document |  |  |
| EDOESSII\_SDOCHIS | eDocument Spain SII Single Document History |  |  |

**ET Business Object:** LOC\_DISI\_ES
**Name (en):** Digital Signature for Spain
**General Description**
=======================

On 1 July 2017, the Spanish SII regulations for mandatory electronic reporting of VAT information came into force for the following taxpayers:

Companies that are considered ‘large companies’ and have an annual turnover in Spain of more than EUR 6,010,121.04.  
Organisations that belong to VAT groups in Spain.  
Taxpayers registered in the ‘REDEME’ scheme (special monthly VAT refund scheme).  
The SII model also applies to non-resident and non-established organisations. In addition, organisations can also register voluntarily.

In the event that the XML file required by the Spanish tax authorities is not submitted on time or in the event of incomplete or incorrect data, a penalty of 0.5% of the invoice amount may be imposed, with a minimum of EUR 300 and a maximum of EUR 6,000 per quarter.

SII legal requirements for electronic reporting of VAT information in Spain  
The SII requirements for electronic submission of VAT information in Spain require the reporting of the following four ledgers to the AEAT E-Office of the Spanish tax authority.

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

Warranty management in SAP plays a crucial role across industries since it allows automatic tracking of product/services under warranty period and as well re-imbursement of claim expenses with in validity. This document would provide brief about Warranty Management features available in SAP and related technical configuration to implement Warranty Management solution in SAP.

| FAGL\_011AC\_XBRL | Fin. Stmt Vers.: Assignment of FS Items to Asset Classes | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Anlagenklassen |  |
| FAGL\_011CC\_XBRL | F/S Version: Calculation Rule for F/S Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Rechenregel für Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011FC | Fin. Statement Structure: Assignment FS Item - Funct. Areas | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Funktionsbereiche |  |
| FAGL\_011FC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Assignment of FS Items to FunctAreas | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Funktionsbereiche |  |
| FAGL\_011GC\_XBRL | Fin. Stmt Vers.: Assignmt of FS Items to Asset Hist.Sht Grps | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Anl.Gittergruppen |  |
| FAGL\_011KC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Assgmt Fin. Stmt Item - Key Figures | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Kennzahlen |  |
| FAGL\_011NC\_XBRL | F/S Version: Technical Names for F/S Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Technische Namen zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011PC | Fin. Statement Structure: Items in Fin. Statement Structure | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011PC\_T | Fin. Statement Version: Items (Without Internal Node IDs) | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen (ohne interne Knoten IDs) |  |
| FAGL\_011PC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Items (Without Internal Node IDs) | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen (ohne interne Knoten IDs) |  |
| FAGL\_011PCI | F/S Structure: Texts of 1st Item of F/S Structure | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte der 1. Position der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011PCI\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Items in Financial Statement Version | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Positionen der Bil/GuV-Struktur |  |
| FAGL\_011QT | Fin. Statement Structure: Text for Fin. Statement Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011QT\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Structure: Text for Fin. Statement Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Texte zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011SC | Fin. Statement Struct.: Assignment Fin. Stmnt Item: Setname | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Position - Setname |  |
| FAGL\_011TC | Fin. Statement Structure: Timestamp for Fin. Stmt Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zeitstempel zu Bil/GuV-Positionen |  |
| FAGL\_011VC | Fin. Statement Structure: Contra Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Saldowechselpositionen |  |
| FAGL\_011VC\_XBRL | Finanical Statement Version: Contra Items | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Saldowechselpositionen |  |
| FAGL\_011ZC | Fin. Statement Structure: Assignment FS Items - G/L Account | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Hauptbuchkonten |  |
| FAGL\_011ZC\_XBRL | Fin. Statement Version: Assignment of FS Items to G/L Accts | Bil/GuV-Struktur: Zuordnung Bil/GuV-Pos. - Hauptbuchkonten |  |

**General Description**

Financial Statement Versions are used to display Balance Sheets and Profit & Loss Statements according to different accounting principles. G/L accounts need to be added or re-assigned from time to time. For this reason, this is not always considered to be FI customizing, but sometimes also considered to be FI master data.

Financial Statement Versions can be found in the FAGL\_011\* tables. They are migrated via business object FI\_STMT\_VERSION:

There are more (filled) FAGL\_011\* tables than the ones shown in the screenshot above. So, please make sure to add all the ones that are relevant for your project.

**ET Business Object:** FI\_STMT\_VERSION  (formerly FI\_FAGLFIST)
**Name (en):** Financial Statement Versions

================================================================================

---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | Commodity Pricing Engine |
| Name (de) | Engine für Commodity-Preisfindung |
| ET Business Object | SD\_CPE |
| S/4 SDT Business Object | tbd |
| S/4 to S/4 Business Object | tbd |
| IDOC Business Object | n/a |
**Tables**
==========

All Tables

| tables | Description EN | Bezeichnung DE | Header table |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| CPED\_DEFFORMDOC | CPE Formula - Default in Document | CPE-Formel - Vorschlag im Beleg |  |
| CPED\_EXCHRATE | CPE Term - Exchange Rate | CPE Term - Exchange Rate |  |
| CPED\_EXTENSION | CPE Extension Data | Daten CPE-Erweiterung |  |
| CPED\_FORMCONVIN | CPE Formula - Input for Conversion | CPE-Formel - Eingabe für Umrechnung |  |
| CPED\_FORMCONVOUT | CPE Formula - Result of Conversion | CPE-Formel - Ergebnis der Umrechnung |  |
| CPED\_FORMDOC | CPE Formula - Grouping and General Result | CPE-Formel - Gruppierung und allgemeines Ergebnis |  |
| CPED\_FORMINPUT | CPE Formula - Changed  Input for General Data | CPE-Formel - geänderte Eingabe für allgemeine Daten |  |
| CPED\_KNUMVTOGUID | CPE Caller - Mapping KNUMV and Document Item to GUID | CPE-Aufrufender - Zuordnung KNUMV und Belegosition zu GUID |  |
| CPED\_PERIODDTIN | CPE Term - Changed Input Data for Period Determination | CPE-Term - Geänderte Eingabedaten für Zeitraumermittlung |  |
| CPED\_PERIODDTOUT | CPE Term - Result Data of Period Determination | CPE-Term - Ergebnisdaten der Zeitraumermittlung |  |
| CPED\_PRCFIX\_DEF | Price Fixation Definition | Definition Preisfixierung |  |
| CPED\_PRICEQUOT | Price Quotation | Kursnotierung |  |
| CPED\_QUOTATION | CPE Term - Quotation | CPE Term - Quotation |  |
| CPED\_TERMCONVIN | CPE Term - Input for Conversion | CPE-Term - Eingabe für Umrechnung |  |
| CPED\_TERMCONVOUT | CPE Term - Result of Conversion | CPE-Term - Ergebnis der Umrechnung |  |
| CPED\_TERMGRPOUT | CPE Term - Changed Grouping and General Term Result | CPE-Term - geänderte Gruppierung und allgemeine Termergebnis |  |
| CPED\_TERMINPUT | CPE Term - Changed  Input for General Data | CPE-Term - geänderte Eingabe für allgemeine Daten |  |
| CPED\_TERMOUT | CPE Term - General Result | CPE-Term - allgemeines Ergebnis |  |
| CPED\_TERMROUNDIN | CPE Term - Input for Rounding | CPE-Term - Eingabe für Rundung |  |
| CPED\_TIER | CPE Tiered Pricing | CPE Tiered Pricing |  |

**ET Business Object:** SD\_CPE
**Name (en):** Commodity Pricing Engine
**General Description**
=======================

A tool for calculating the price of commodities.

The commodity pricing engine (CPE) retrieves pricing formulas in business documents and evaluates them to calculate final prices for commodities. The resulting price is then transferred to the condition types that are used in commodity pricing.

CPE enhances pricing by using complex rules to calculate a condition rate and a condition value. These rules define how market data from exchanges or other providers are considered.

================================================================================

**General Description**
=======================

The reconciliation ledger is a tool for summarizing the presentation of controlling traffic figures. It is used to reconcile controlling with financial accounting.

The reconciliation ledger provides an overview of all costs incurred.

**ET Business Object:** UTIL\_DM\_CONNOBJ
**Name (en):** Connection Object
**General Description**
=======================

A connection object is usually a building but can also be a piece of property or a facility, such as a fountain or construction site.  
Since an address is allocated to a connection object, the connection object links premises, device locations and connections to the postal regional structure.

The connection object is a functional location from the Plant Maintenance (PM) application component.

**ET Business Object:** CO\_RESSOURCE
**Name (en):** Ressources
**General Description**
=======================

A resource comprises goods and services that are supplied to the company both externally and internally in order to produce operational services.

Resources cannot be used in actual. For this purpose, they can only be created as single values and not in hierarchies.

Resource planning will replace detailed planning in the future.

---
title: CO - Profit Center Accounting (EC-PCA)
page_id: 117445340
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/117445340
---
Overview
========

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (en) | PCA-Profit Center Accounting |
| Name (de) | PCA-Profit Center Accounting |
| ET Business Object | CO\_PCACC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PCACC) |
| S/4 Business Object | CO\_PCACC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PCACC) |
| IDOC Business Object |  |
Tables
======

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **tables** | **Description** | **Bezeichnung DE** | **Header table** |
| GLPCT | EC-PCA: Totals Table | EC-PCA: Summentabelle |  |
| GLPCA | EC-PCA: Actual Line Items | EC-PCA: Ist-Einzelposten |  |
| GLPCC | EC-PCA: Transaction Attributes | EC-PCA: Bewegungsattribute |  |
| GLPCO | EC-PCA: Object Table for Account Assignment Elements | EC-PCA: Objekttabelle Kontierungselemente |  |
| GLPCP | EC-PCA: Plan Line Items | EC-PCA: Plan-Einzelposten |  |
| GLSPC | Profit Center Accounting: Line Items | Profit Center Rechnung  Einzelposten |  |
| GLTPC | Profit Center Accounting | Profit Center Rechnung |  |

**ET Business Object:** CO\_PCACC  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CO60PCACC)
**Name (en):** PCA-Profit Center Accounting
General Description
===================

Profit Center Accounting (EC-PCA) supports the calculation of an internal operating profit for profit centers according to the period accounting method or the cost-of-sales method.

A profit center is a management-oriented organizational unit, which means that it is used for internal controlling.

The division of the company into profit centers forms the basis for controlling internal areas of responsibility.

Note the effects on EC-PCA when changing over master data or organizational data. Tables in PCA are object-based. An object number stands for certain constellations. These are contained in the object number tables GLPCO. There is an internal cbs correction program that adapts the object numbers in the table and in the totals table to changes in, for example, organizational units. (Report in SHC tree).

GLPCA
-----

Table GLPCA contains profit center actual line items. Some fields are grouped together to form a specific object number that can be found on tables GLPCO and GLPCC:

The five fields from RBUKRS to RSCOPE are assigned to a unique object number in table GLPCO while the 14 fields from RACCT to RMVCT are assigned to a unique object number in table GLPCC.

GLPCO
-----

The GLPCO object number can be found in GLPCT field ROBJNR. The five fields form a unique index, i.e. each object number consists of a unique combination of these five fields. If you map any of these fields, you have to do unique index mapping for GLPCO-OBJNR.

GLPCC
-----

The GLPCC object number can be found in GLPCT field COBJNR. The 14 fields form a unique index, i.e. each object number consists of a unique combination of these 14 fields. If you map any of these fields, you have to do unique index mapping for GLPCC-OBJNR.

GLPCT
-----

Though GLPCT-ROBJNR links to GLPCO and GLPCT-COBJNR links to GLPCC, the corresponding fields can be found in GLPCT as well. So all three tables have to be consistent. So if you create unique index mappings for object numbers from GLPCO or GLPCC. you have to convert GLPCT using the same unique index mappings created during the conversion of GLPCO / GLPCC. Or you might have to re-build GLPCT based on GLPCA (and GLPCP).

Note that also GLPCT-SOBJNR is linked to GLPCO.

================================================================================

---

**ET Business Object:** SD\_CREDITCARD
**Name (en):** Payment Cards
**General Description**
=======================

To quickly and efficiently process sales orders with payment cards, you can store card data in the payer master record. When you create a sales order, you simply call up a list of cards for the customer using the value help. The system automatically copies the card data into the sales order for the card or cards that you choose from the respective list.

The one-time customer record is a collective account, in the master record, for a group of unrelated customers. For this reason, you cannot maintain payment card data for one-time customers.

================================================================================

title: Sampling Schemes
page_id: 120116394
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120116394
---
**Overview**
============

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name (EN) | Sampling Schemes |
| Name (DE) | Stichprobenpläne |
| ET Business Object | QM\_SAS  (till ET 2021 FP 2 QM60SAS) |
| S/4 Business Object | N/A |
**Tables**
==========

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Table** | **Description (EN)** | **Bezeichnung (DE)** | **Main Table** |
| QDPA | Sampling scheme-instructions | Stichprobenplan-Anweisungen |  |
| QDPK | Sampling scheme header | Stichprobenplan-Kopf | X |
| QDPKT | Sampling scheme: texts | Stichprobenplan: Texte |  |
| QDPP | Sampling scheme item | Stichprobenplan-Position |  |

**ET Business Object:** QM\_SAS  (till ET 2021 FP 2 QM60SAS)
**Name (en):** Sampling Schemes
**General Description**
=======================

This BO contains the required tables to migrate the sampling schemes. 

A sampling scheme is a collection of one or more sampling tables, each of which contain one or more sampling plans. The sampling plans were grouped together on the basis of various determining criteria (for example, acceptable quality level and inspection severity).

================================================================================

---
title: Asset migration using IDOCs
page_id: 120110609
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/120110609
---
General description
===================

Article under construction!!!!

The migration of assets can be done not only via DB migration but also with other approaches as iDocs. Besides of this, when we load assets, we must think of two topics:

* Migration of Master Data
* Migration of Asset Values

To migrate assets different ways can be used:

* Migration of Master Data via DB update + Load of Asset values via iDocs.
* Load of Master Data via iDocs + Load of Asset values via iDocs.

================================================================================

40%

**table of content**

**ET Business Object:** (CA\_OUTPUT  (till ET 2021 FP 2 CA60OUTPUT))  Business Object will be created with  BO Status 97
**Name (en):** Output conditions
**General Description**
=======================

Output determination is the process to determine the "media” such as printouts, faxes, e-mails, or EDI that are sent from one business to any of its business partners. Condition records store this preferred mode of communication. The condition records can be related to organizational units. They are stored in the B\* tables and table NACH. This is master data.

Processed messages, for example in purchase orders, can be found in table NAST.

================================================================================

---
title: Logical Split - Manual update (directly in ET-Tables)
page_id: 193088089
space: unknown
url: https://confluence.cbs-consulting.de/pages/193088089
---
**Overview**
============

Normally the logical split is maintained directly in the respective BO (see also: [Business Object Splits (cbs-consulting.de)](https://m.cbs-consulting.de/slt/concepts/export_splits.html)). Another way to maintain the logical split would be directly through ET-Tables instead of the BO. This can be necessary due to the fact that there are too many splits to maintain them manually in the BO. As a workaround, the ET-Tables can be filled up through SE16N (make sure to get the authorization for maintaining tables in SE16N).

**The ET-Tables would be:**

* /CBSGMBH/C\_SPL      Header Table for BO-Split Customizing
* /CBSGMBH/C\_SPL\_L   Data for logical BO-Split
* /CBSGMBH/C\_SPL\_S   Selections of logical BO-Split
